-- =============================================================
-- Migration: Work Order Tables
-- Description: Projects, work orders, tasks, notes, and templates
-- =============================================================

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: wo_projects
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.wo_projects (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    organization_id uuid NOT NULL,
    name text NOT NULL,
    description text,
    status text DEFAULT 'active'::text NOT NULL,
    metadata jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
    project_type text,
    library_id uuid,
    CONSTRAINT wo_projects_status_check CHECK ((status = ANY (ARRAY['active'::text, 'completed'::text, 'archived'::text])))
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.wo_projects
    ADD CONSTRAINT wo_projects_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: work_orders
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.work_orders (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    organization_id uuid NOT NULL,
    unit_id uuid NOT NULL,
    template_id uuid NOT NULL,
    trade_name text NOT NULL,
    status text DEFAULT 'pending'::text,
    assigned_contractor_id uuid,
    inspector_id uuid,
    start_date timestamp with time zone,
    completion_date timestamp with time zone,
    inspector_signoff_date timestamp with time zone,
    metadata jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.work_orders
    ADD CONSTRAINT work_orders_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: work_order_tasks
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.work_order_tasks (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    work_order_id uuid NOT NULL,
    room_id uuid NOT NULL,
    row_order integer DEFAULT 0,
    task_data jsonb NOT NULL,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.work_order_tasks
    ADD CONSTRAINT work_order_tasks_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: work_order_notes
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.work_order_notes (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    work_order_id uuid NOT NULL,
    author_id uuid NOT NULL,
    content text NOT NULL,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.work_order_notes
    ADD CONSTRAINT work_order_notes_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: work_order_templates
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.work_order_templates (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    organization_id uuid,
    trade_name text NOT NULL,
    version integer DEFAULT 1,
    is_active boolean DEFAULT true,
    definition jsonb NOT NULL,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    scope text DEFAULT 'company'::text NOT NULL,
    project_id uuid,
    parent_template_id uuid,
    copied_from_template_id uuid,
    created_by uuid,
    updated_by uuid,
    library_id uuid,
    CONSTRAINT valid_app_scope CHECK ((((scope = 'app'::text) AND (organization_id IS NULL) AND (project_id IS NULL)) OR (scope <> 'app'::text))),
    CONSTRAINT valid_company_scope CHECK ((((scope = 'company'::text) AND (organization_id IS NOT NULL) AND (project_id IS NULL)) OR (scope <> 'company'::text))),
    CONSTRAINT valid_project_scope CHECK ((((scope = 'project'::text) AND (organization_id IS NOT NULL) AND (project_id IS NOT NULL)) OR (scope <> 'project'::text))),
    CONSTRAINT work_order_templates_scope_check CHECK ((scope = ANY (ARRAY['app'::text, 'company'::text, 'project'::text])))
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.work_order_templates
    ADD CONSTRAINT work_order_templates_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

-- =============================================================
-- Migration: Core Tables
-- Description: Organizations, rooms, template libraries, and metric definitions
-- =============================================================

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: organizations
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.organizations (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    name text NOT NULL,
    slug text NOT NULL,
    settings jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.organizations
    ADD CONSTRAINT organizations_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: metric_definitions
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.metric_definitions (
    metric_key text NOT NULL,
    metric_label text NOT NULL,
    category text NOT NULL,
    trade_key text,
    unit_of_measure text,
    default_formula text,
    description text,
    sort_order integer DEFAULT 0
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.metric_definitions
    ADD CONSTRAINT metric_definitions_pkey PRIMARY KEY (metric_key);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: rooms
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.rooms (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    organization_id uuid NOT NULL,
    name text NOT NULL,
    display_order integer DEFAULT 0,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.rooms
    ADD CONSTRAINT rooms_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: template_libraries
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.template_libraries (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    name text NOT NULL,
    description text,
    project_type text NOT NULL,
    scope text DEFAULT 'app'::text NOT NULL,
    organization_id uuid,
    icon text,
    color text,
    metadata jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
    is_active boolean DEFAULT true NOT NULL,
    is_default boolean DEFAULT false NOT NULL,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
    CONSTRAINT template_libraries_scope_check CHECK ((scope = ANY (ARRAY['app'::text, 'company'::text]))),
    CONSTRAINT valid_app_library CHECK ((((scope = 'app'::text) AND (organization_id IS NULL)) OR (scope <> 'app'::text))),
    CONSTRAINT valid_company_library CHECK ((((scope = 'company'::text) AND (organization_id IS NOT NULL)) OR (scope <> 'company'::text)))
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.template_libraries
    ADD CONSTRAINT template_libraries_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

bash>cd /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold && cat supabase/migrations/20260222000002_projec...

-- =============================================================
-- Migration: Project Lifecycle & Template Inheritance
-- Description:
--   1. Expand wo_projects.status to include 'staged' and 'paused'
--   2. Add is_frozen column to work_order_templates
--   3. Allow NULL definition for lazy-copy project templates
--   4. Create freeze_project_templates() trigger function
--   5. Seed app-scope templates from existing company templates
-- =============================================================

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 1. Expand project status CHECK constraint
-- -----------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE public.wo_projects DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS wo_projects_status_check;
ALTER TABLE public.wo_projects ADD CONSTRAINT wo_projects_status_check
  CHECK (status IN ('staged', 'active', 'paused', 'completed', 'archived'));

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 2. Add is_frozen to templates
-- -----------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE public.work_order_templates
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS is_frozen BOOLEAN DEFAULT false;

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 3. Allow NULL definition for lazy-copy templates
--    (project templates reference parent until frozen)
-- -----------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE public.work_order_templates
  ALTER COLUMN definition DROP NOT NULL;

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 4. Trigger: freeze project templates on activation
--    When a project transitions to 'active', copy the
--    definition from the parent company template into
--    each unfrozen project template.
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.freeze_project_templates()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  -- Only fire when status changes TO 'active'
  IF NEW.status = 'active' AND (OLD.status IS DISTINCT FROM 'active') THEN
    UPDATE public.work_order_templates pt
    SET
      definition = ct.definition,
      is_frozen = true,
      updated_at = now()
    FROM public.work_order_templates ct
    WHERE pt.project_id = NEW.id
      AND pt.scope = 'project'
      AND pt.parent_template_id = ct.id
      AND pt.is_frozen = false
      AND pt.definition IS NULL;
  END IF;
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_freeze_project_templates ON public.wo_projects;
CREATE TRIGGER trg_freeze_project_templates
  AFTER UPDATE OF status ON public.wo_projects
  FOR EACH ROW
  EXECUTE FUNCTION public.freeze_project_templates();

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 5. Seed app-scope templates
--    Create app-scope copies of each unique trade
--    from the existing company templates (org 00...01)
-- -----------------------------------------------
INSERT INTO public.work_order_templates (
  id, organization_id, trade_name, version, is_active,
  definition, scope, project_id, is_frozen
)
SELECT
  -- Deterministic UUIDs: A0000000-... prefix for app templates
  ('a0000000-0000-0000-0000-' || lpad(row_number() OVER (ORDER BY trade_name)::text, 12, '0'))::uuid,
  NULL,                    -- no org for app-scope
  trade_name,
  1,
  true,
  definition,              -- copy full definition
  'app',                   -- app scope
  NULL,                    -- no project
  false                    -- not frozen (app templates are the source of truth)
FROM public.work_order_templates
WHERE scope = 'company'
  AND organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001'
  AND is_active = true
ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;

-- Update the existing company templates to point back to their app source
UPDATE public.work_order_templates ct
SET copied_from_template_id = at.id
FROM public.work_order_templates at
WHERE at.scope = 'app'
  AND ct.scope = 'company'
  AND ct.organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001'
  AND ct.trade_name = at.trade_name
  AND ct.copied_from_template_id IS NULL;
(venv) root@d2d162d983f0:/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold#

kvps.memories:
The tenant onboarding system uses a full-stack architecture with a SolidJS frontend (http://localhost:3005) and a Hono-based API (port 3000). Upon successful onboarding, the tenant should be directed to login at http://localhost:3004/ for work orders.

Frontend routes include /tenants, /tenant/:id, and a public /onboard form. The /onboard form requires an 'industry' select box (populated from trades templates) and a new 'description' field.

The Hono API provides the following endpoints:
- POST /onboard (Public onboarding)
- GET /onboard (List organizations)
- GET/PATCH /onboard/:id (View/Edit profiles)
- GET/POST /onboard/:id/admins (Manage admin users)

Database management uses Supabase migrations (up to 20260222000005_tenant_onboarding.sql) via 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' or 'npm run db:migrate' with the '--local' flag. Migrations include 'NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';' to refresh the PostgREST cache.

The 'organizations' table in the public schema includes: company_email, phone, address_line1, address_line2, city, state, zip, country, contact_name, contact_title, website, is_active, and onboarding_status (pending | active | suspended).

logs.heading: 2 memories and 1 relevant solutions found

kvps.memories:
The tenant onboarding system uses a full-stack architecture with a SolidJS frontend (http://localhost:3005) and a Hono-based API (port 3000). Upon successful onboarding, the tenant should be directed to login at http://localhost:3004/ for work orders.

Frontend routes include /tenants, /tenant/:id, and a public /onboard form. The /onboard form requires an 'industry' select box (populated from trades templates) and a new 'description' field.

The Hono API provides the following endpoints:
- POST /onboard (Public onboarding)
- GET /onboard (List organizations)
- GET/PATCH /onboard/:id (View/Edit profiles)
- GET/POST /onboard/:id/admins (Manage admin users)

Database management uses Supabase migrations (up to 20260222000005_tenant_onboarding.sql) via 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' or 'npm run db:migrate' with the '--local' flag. Migrations include 'NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';' to refresh the PostgREST cache.

A custom PowerShell script 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' and npm script 'db:migrate' were added to handle incremental Supabase migrations using the '--local' flag without wiping data.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Supabase local migrations failing to apply, missing tables/columns, or PostgREST schema cache becoming stale (leading to 'Could not find column' errors) after database updates.

# Solution
Implement a robust local development workflow using a PowerShell script (`apply-migration.ps1`) to ensure consistency between the database, the API cache, and TypeScript types.

### 1. Automated Migration Script Logic
Create a script to sequence the following operations:
- **Environment Check**: Verify Supabase is running with `npx supabase status`. If down, run `npx supabase start`.
- **Apply Migrations**: Run `npx supabase migration up --local` to apply pending changes without affecting remote environments.
- **Refresh PostgREST Cache**: Force a schema reload to prevent stale API errors using: `npx supabase db execute --local -q "NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';"`.
- **Regenerate Types**: Update TypeScript definitions immediately (e.g., `pnpm db:types` or `npx supabase gen types typescript`).

### 2. Alternative & Manual Fixes
- **Manual Refresh**: Execute `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` in the Supabase SQL Editor.
- **Migration Integration**: Append the `NOTIFY` command to the end of `.sql` migration files for automatic refreshing.
- **Status Verification**: Use `supabase migration list --local` to identify unapplied migrations.
- **Hard Reset**: If the API fails to recognize changes after a notify command, run `supabase stop && supabase start`.

### 3. Local Connectivity
When accessing the local database from within other Docker containers, use `host.docker.internal` on port `54322`.

kvps.memories:
The tenant onboarding system uses a full-stack architecture with a SolidJS frontend (http://localhost:3005), a Hono-based API (port 3000), and a Supabase database. Upon successful onboarding, the tenant should be directed to login at http://localhost:3004/ for work orders.

Frontend routes include /tenants, /tenant/:id, and a public /onboard form. The /onboard form requires an 'industry' select box (populated from trades templates) and a new 'description' field.

The Hono API provides the following endpoints:
- POST /onboard (Public onboarding)
- GET /onboard (List organizations)
- GET/PATCH /onboard/:id (View/Edit profiles)
- GET/POST /onboard/:id/admins (Manage admin users)

Database management uses Supabase migrations (up to 20260222000005_tenant_onboarding.sql) via 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' or 'npm run db:migrate' with the '--local' flag. Migrations include 'NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';' to refresh the PostgREST cache.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Implementing a multi-tenant onboarding system with Supabase that handles organization creation, admin user setup, and industry-specific template initialization.

# Solution

### 1. Backend API Architecture (Hono/Supabase)
- **Transaction Flow**: Create a backend API route that accepts organization and admin details. Use a Supabase client with the `service_role` key to bypass RLS during onboarding.
- **Organization & User Setup**:
    a. Create the organization record in the `organizations` table.
    b. Create the admin user via `supabase.auth.admin.createUser`.
    c. Set `user_metadata` with `organization_id` and `role: 'admin'` to enable RLS.
- **Industry & Template Logic**:
    a. **Schema**: Add `industry` and `description` columns to the `organizations` table.
    b. **Endpoints**: Create `GET /onboard/trades` to fetch master templates (scope='app'). Ensure specific routes are defined before parameterized routes (e.g., `/onboard/trades` before `/onboard/:id`).
    c. **Template Copying**: Implement a helper `copyTemplatesToOrg(orgId, tradeFilter?)`. If `industry === 'General Contractor'`, copy all app-scope templates; otherwise, filter by the selected trade. Insert into `work_order_templates` with `scope = 'company'` and the new `organization_id`.

### 2. Frontend Implementation (SolidJS)
- **Form Elements**: Use `createResource` to fetch trades for an 'industry' `<select>` box. Add a `<textarea>` for 'description'.
- **Admin Client**: Use a separate `supabaseAdmin` client for management tasks with `persistSession: false` and a unique `storageKey` (e.g., 'sb-admin') to avoid session conflicts.
- **Success Handling**: Upon successful onboarding, redirect the user to the target dashboard URL (e.g., `http://localhost:3004/`).

### 3. Database Maintenance
- Always include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` after migrations to refresh the PostgREST cache.

# Problem
Supabase local migrations failing to apply, missing tables/columns, or PostgREST schema cache becoming stale (leading to 'Could not find column' errors) after database updates.

# Solution
Implement a robust local development workflow using a PowerShell script (`apply-migration.ps1`) to ensure consistency between the database, the API cache, and TypeScript types.

### 1. Automated Migration Script Logic
Create a script to sequence the following operations:
- **Environment Check**: Verify Supabase is running with `npx supabase status`. If down, run `npx supabase start`.
- **Apply Migrations**: Run `npx supabase migration up --local` to apply pending changes without affecting remote environments.
- **Refresh PostgREST Cache**: Force a schema reload to prevent stale API errors using: `npx supabase db execute --local -q "NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';"`.
- **Regenerate Types**: Update TypeScript definitions immediately (e.g., `pnpm db:types` or `npx supabase gen types typescript`).

### 2. Alternative & Manual Fixes
- **Manual Refresh**: Execute `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` in the Supabase SQL Editor.
- **Migration Integration**: Append the `NOTIFY` command to the end of `.sql` migration files for automatic refreshing.
- **Status Verification**: Use `supabase migration list --local` to identify unapplied migrations.
- **Hard Reset**: If the API fails to recognize changes after a notify command, run `supabase stop && supabase start`.

### 3. Local Connectivity
When accessing the local database from within other Docker containers, use `host.docker.internal` on port `54322`.

========
-- =============================================================
-- Migration: Project Lifecycle & Template Inheritance
-- Description:
--   1. Expand wo_projects.status to include 'staged' and 'paused'
--   2. Add is_frozen column to work_order_templates
--   3. Allow NULL definition for lazy-copy project templates
--   4. Create freeze_project_templates() trigger function
--   5. Seed app-scope templates from existing company templates
-- =============================================================

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 1. Expand project status CHECK constraint
-- -----------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE public.wo_projects DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS wo_projects_status_check;
ALTER TABLE public.wo_projects ADD CONSTRAINT wo_projects_status_check
  CHECK (status IN ('staged', 'active', 'paused', 'completed', 'archived'));

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 2. Add is_frozen to templates
-- -----------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE public.work_order_templates
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS is_frozen BOOLEAN DEFAULT false;

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 3. Allow NULL definition for lazy-copy templates
--    (project templates reference parent until frozen)
-- -----------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE public.work_order_templates
  ALTER COLUMN definition DROP NOT NULL;

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 4. Trigger: freeze project templates on activation
--    When a project transitions to 'active', copy the
--    definition from the parent company template into
--    each unfrozen project template.
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.freeze_project_templates()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  -- Only fire when status changes TO 'active'
  IF NEW.status = 'active' AND (OLD.status IS DISTINCT FROM 'active') THEN
    UPDATE public.work_order_templates pt
    SET
      definition = ct.definition,
      is_frozen = true,
      updated_at = now()
    FROM public.work_order_templates ct
    WHERE pt.project_id = NEW.id
      AND pt.scope = 'project'
      AND pt.parent_template_id = ct.id
      AND pt.is_frozen = false
      AND pt.definition IS NULL;
  END IF;
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_freeze_project_templates ON public.wo_projects;
CREATE TRIGGER trg_freeze_project_templates
  AFTER UPDATE OF status ON public.wo_projects

bash>cd /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold && sed -n '150,175p' supabase/seed.sql && echo '...

--
-- Master catalog: promote each company template to an app-scoped catalog entry.
-- IDs are derived by replacing the '20000000' prefix with '30000000' so they
-- are stable and predictable after every reset.
--
INSERT INTO public.work_order_templates (id, organization_id, trade_name, version, is_active, definition, created_at, updated_at, scope, project_id, parent_template_id, copied_from_template_id, created_by, updated_by, library_id)
SELECT
    replace(id::text, '20000000', '30000000')::uuid,
    NULL,
    trade_name,
    version,
    is_active,
    definition,
    created_at,
    updated_at,
    'app',
    NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL
FROM public.work_order_templates
WHERE scope = 'company' AND organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001';

-- Link the company templates back to their catalog source
UPDATE public.work_order_templates
SET copied_from_template_id = replace(id::text, '20000000', '30000000')::uuid
WHERE scope = 'company' AND organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001';


========
INSERT INTO public.work_order_templates (
  id, organization_id, trade_name, version, is_active,
  definition, scope, project_id, is_frozen
)
SELECT
  -- Deterministic UUIDs: A0000000-... prefix for app templates
  ('a0000000-0000-0000-0000-' || lpad(row_number() OVER (ORDER BY trade_name)::text, 12, '0'))::uuid,
  NULL,                    -- no org for app-scope
  trade_name,
  1,
  true,
  definition,              -- copy full definition
  'app',                   -- app scope
  NULL,                    -- no project
  false                    -- not frozen (app templates are the source of truth)
FROM public.work_order_templates
WHERE scope = 'company'
  AND organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001'
  AND is_active = true
ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;

-- Update the existing company templates to point back to their app source
UPDATE public.work_order_templates ct
SET copied_from_template_id = at.id
FROM public.work_order_templates at
WHERE at.scope = 'app'
  AND ct.scope = 'company'
  AND ct.organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001'
  AND ct.trade_name = at.trade_name
  AND ct.copied_from_template_id IS NULL;

thoughts: Now I have the full answer. The early-work/ subfolder is NOT referenced in supabase config.toml - Supabase only processes migrations in the top-level migrations/ folder, not subfolders. So the early-work migrations (which create Painting and Carpentry as company-scope templates) are NOT applied during `supabase db reset`.
Here's the flow:
1. Supabase runs migrations from supabase/migrations/ (top-level only, NOT early-work/)
2. Migration 20260222000002 tries to copy company-scope templates to app-scope, but at this point only the templates from OTHER migrations exist (not the early-work ones)
3. Then seed.sql runs - it inserts Painting (20000000-...-001) and Carpentry (20000000-...-002) as company-scope, AND the remaining templates (Plumbing, Flooring, etc.) as company-scope
4. seed.sql ALSO promotes all company templates to app-scope with 30000000-... IDs
So actually, if seed.sql runs correctly, ALL 8 trades (including Painting and Carpentry) SHOULD exist as app-scope templates.

But wait - the seed.sql inserts company templates first (Painting at line ~130, remaining at line ~141), then promotes them to app-scope (line ~156). So the order within seed.sql matters and it looks correct.

{
    "thoughts": [
        "Supabase isn't running or accessible from this container. That's fine - I can answer the user's question based on the code analysis.",
        "",

        "3. The migration 20260222000002 ALSO creates app-scope templates from company templates",
        "4. So IF the database has been seeded properly, Painting and Carpentry SHOULD be in the app-scope templates and SHOULD appear in the /onboard/trades dropdown",
        "",
        "The potential issue: if the database was reset and only migrations ran (without seed.sql), or if the early-work migrations didn't run (they're in a subfolder not picked up by Supabase), then the templates might be missing.",
        "",
        "Let me give the user a clear answer about where these trades are defined and what might be going wrong."
    ],
    "headline": "Providing answer about Painting and Carpentry trade locations",
    "tool_name": "response",
    "tool_args": {
        "text": "Great question — **Painting** and **Carpentry** are both defined in the codebase. Here's exactly where they live:

## Where They're Defined

### 1. `supabase/seed.sql` (company-scope)
Both are seeded as **company-scope** templates for the demo org (`00000000-...001`):
- **Painting** → `id: 20000000-...-000000000001` — Walls (12-step) + Ceilings (Popcorn)
- **Carpentry** → `id: 20000000-...-000000000002` — Baseboards, Closets, Doors

### 2. `supabase/seed.sql` (app-scope promotion, lines ~150-170)
The seed file then **promotes all company templates to app-scope** by copying them with `30000000-...` IDs:
```sql
INSERT INTO work_order_templates (...)
SELECT replace(id::text, '20000000', '30000000')::uuid, NULL, trade_name, ..., 'app', ...
FROM work_order_templates
WHERE scope = 'company' AND organization_id = '00000000-...-001';
```
So Painting → `30000000-...-001` and Carpentry → `30000000-...-002` as app-scope.

### 3. Migration `20260222000002` (backup mechanism)
This migration ALSO creates app-scope templates with `a0000000-...` IDs using the same approach.

## Why They Might Not Appear in the Onboarding Dropdown

The `/onboard/trades` endpoint queries:
```sql
WHERE scope = 'app' AND is_active = true
```

If the database was **reset without running `seed.sql`**, or if the migration ran **before** the company templates were inserted, the app-scope copies won't exist.

### ⚠️ Key Issue: `early-work/` subfolder
The original Painting & Carpentry templates are created in `supabase/migrations/early-work/20260201200501_seed_work_orders.sql` — but **Supabase doesn't process migrations in subfolders**. The `early-work/` folder is not referenced in `supabase/config.toml`. So these only get created when `seed.sql` runs.

## Fix: Ensure `supabase db reset` runs properly
Running `supabase db reset` should:
1. Apply all top-level migrations
2. Run `seed.sql` which inserts Painting + Carpentry as company-scope, then promotes them to app-scope

After a proper reset, all **8 trades** should appear in the dropdown:
> Appliances, Carpentry, Electrical, Flooring, HVAC, Painting, Plumbing, Window Coverings

Would you like me to verify by running a database reset, or is there a specific issue you're seeing with the dropdown?"
    }
}

logs.heading: 3 memories and 2 relevant solutions found

kvps.memories:
The 'supabase/migrations/early-work/' subfolder is not automatically processed by Supabase because it is not referenced in 'supabase/config.toml'.

A custom PowerShell script 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' and npm script 'db:migrate' handle incremental Supabase migrations using the '--local' flag. Note: The 'supabase/migrations/early-work/' subfolder is not automatically processed by Supabase because it is not referenced in 'supabase/config.toml'.

The tenant onboarding system uses a full-stack architecture with a SolidJS frontend (http://localhost:3005), a Hono-based API (port 3000), and a Supabase database. Upon successful onboarding, the tenant should be directed to login at http://localhost:3004/ for work orders.

Frontend routes include /tenants, /tenant/:id, and a public /onboard form. The /onboard form requires an 'industry' select box and a new 'description' field.

The Hono API (apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts) provides the following endpoints:
- POST /onboard (Public onboarding)
- GET /onboard (List organizations)
- GET /onboard/trades (Retrieves distinct trade names from active app-scope templates for industry selection)
- GET/PATCH /onboard/:id (View/Edit profiles)
- GET/POST /onboard/:id/admins (Manage admin users)

Database management uses Supabase migrations (up to 20260222000005_tenant_onboarding.sql) via 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' or 'npm run db:migrate' with the '--local' flag. Migrations include 'NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';' to refresh the PostgREST cache.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Implementing a multi-tenant onboarding system with Supabase that handles organization creation, admin user setup, and industry-specific template initialization, including troubleshooting missing trade templates in dropdowns.

# Solution

### 1. Backend API Architecture (Hono/Supabase)
- **Transaction Flow**: Create a backend API route using `service_role` to bypass RLS during onboarding.
- **Organization & User Setup**: Create organization record; create admin via `supabase.auth.admin.createUser`; set `user_metadata` with `organization_id` and `role: 'admin'`.
- **Industry & Template Logic**:
    - **Endpoints**: Create `GET /onboard/trades` to fetch master templates where `scope = 'app'`. Ensure specific routes are defined before parameterized routes.
    - **Template Copying**: Implement `copyTemplatesToOrg(orgId, tradeFilter?)`. If 'General Contractor', copy all app-scope templates; otherwise, filter by trade. Insert with `scope = 'company'` and the new `organization_id`.

### 2. Troubleshooting Missing Trades (e.g., Painting, Carpentry)
- **Data Source Filtering**: The `/onboard/trades` API filters for `scope = 'app'`. If trades are missing, they likely exist only as `company` scope in the source.
- **Migration Structure**: Verify migrations aren't hidden in subfolders (e.g., `supabase/migrations/early-work/`). Supabase only processes top-level migrations unless configured in `config.toml`.
- **Seeding & Promotion Logic**: Ensure `supabase/seed.sql` contains logic to promote templates. This involves selecting `company` scope templates and re-inserting them with `scope = 'app'` and a specific UUID prefix (e.g., changing `20000000` to `30000000`).
- **Fix**: Run `supabase db reset` to apply top-level migrations and execute `seed.sql` to perform this promotion.

### 3. Frontend Implementation (SolidJS)
- **Form Elements**: Use `createResource` to fetch trades for an industry dropdown.
- **Admin Client**: Use a separate `supabaseAdmin` client with `persistSession: false` and a unique `storageKey` (e.g., 'sb-admin') to avoid session conflicts.

### 4. Database Maintenance
- Always include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` after migrations to refresh the PostgREST cache.

# Problem
Supabase local migrations failing to apply, missing tables/columns, or PostgREST schema cache becoming stale (leading to 'Could not find column' errors) after database updates.

# Solution
Implement a robust local development workflow using a PowerShell script (`apply-migration.ps1`) to ensure consistency between the database, the API cache, and TypeScript types.

### 1. Automated Migration Script Logic
Create a script to sequence the following operations:
- **Environment Check**: Verify Supabase is running with `npx supabase status`. If down, run `npx supabase start`.
- **Apply Migrations**: Run `npx supabase migration up --local` to apply pending changes without affecting remote environments.
- **Refresh PostgREST Cache**: Force a schema reload to prevent stale API errors using: `npx supabase db execute --local -q "NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';"`.
- **Regenerate Types**: Update TypeScript definitions immediately (e.g., `pnpm db:types` or `npx supabase gen types typescript`).

### 2. Alternative & Manual Fixes
- **Manual Refresh**: Execute `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` in the Supabase SQL Editor.
- **Migration Integration**: Append the `NOTIFY` command to the end of `.sql` migration files for automatic refreshing.
- **Status Verification**: Use `supabase migration list --local` to identify unapplied migrations.
- **Hard Reset**: If the API fails to recognize changes after a notify command, run `supabase stop && supabase start`.

### 3. Local Connectivity
When accessing the local database from within other Docker containers, use `host.docker.internal` on port `54322`.

[Development Setup](./docs/setup/dev-setup.md) • [Usage](./docs/guides/usage.md) Or see DeepWiki generated documentation: [![Ask DeepWiki](https://deepwiki.com/badge.svg)](https://deepwiki.com/agent0ai/agent-zero)
> ### 🚨 **AGENT ZERO SKILLS** 🚨 > **Skills System** - portable, structured agent capabilities using the open `SKILL.md` standard (compatible with Claude Code, Codex and more). > > **Plus:** Git-based Projects with authentication for public/private repositories - clone codebases directly into isolated workspaces. > > See [Usage Guide](./docs/guides/usage.md) and [Projects Tutorial](./docs/guides/projects.md) to get started.
[![Showcase](/docs/res/showcase-thumb.png)](https://youtu.be/lazLNcEYsiQ) ## A personal, organic agentic framework that grows and learns with you - Agent Zero is not a predefined agentic framework. It is designed to be dynamic, organically growing, and learning as you use it. - Agent Zero is fully transparent, readable, comprehensible, customizable, and interactive. - Agent Zero uses the computer as a tool to accomplish its (your) tasks. # ⚙️ Installation Click to open a video to learn how to install Agent Zero: [![Easy Installation guide](/docs/res/easy_ins_vid.png)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=w5v5Kjx51hs) A detailed setup guide for Windows, macOS, and Linux with a video can be found in the Agent Zero Documentation at [this page](./docs/setup/installation.md). ### ⚡ Quick Start ```bash # Pull and run with Docker docker pull agent0ai/agent-zero docker run -p 50001:80 agent0ai/agent-zero # Visit http://localhost:50001 to start ``` # 💡 Key Features 1. **General-purpose Assistant** - Agent Zero is not pre-programmed for specific tasks (but can be). It is meant to be a general-purpose personal assistant. Give it a task, and it will gather information, execute commands and code, cooperate with other agent instances, and do its best to accomplish it. - It has a persistent memory, allowing it to memorize previous solutions, code, facts, instructions, etc., to solve tasks faster and more reliably in the future. ![Agent 0 Working](/docs/res/ui_screen2.png) 2. **Computer as a Tool**

kvps.solutions:
{'problem': "The 'industry' and 'description' fields added to the database were missing from the Organization Profile management page in the SolidJS frontend.", 'solution': "1. Update the `OrgProfile` interface in `apps/tenant-portal/src/pages/TenantDetailPage.tsx` to include `industry: string | null;` and `description: string | null;`.
2. Implement a `fetchTrades` helper function to retrieve available industries from the API: `const fetchTrades = async () => { const res = await fetch(`${API}/onboard/trades`); return res.ok ? res.json() : []; };`.
3. Initialize a SolidJS resource within the component: `const [trades] = createResource(fetchTrades);`.
4. Modify the JSX to include an industry `<select>` dropdown and a description `<textarea>`.
5. Ensure the dropdown includes a manual option for 'General Contractor' and maps through the `trades()` resource for other options.
6. Bind the `value` and `onInput`/`onChange` handlers to the existing `update` and `dirty` state logic to ensure the 'Save Profile' functionality automatically includes these new fields in the Supabase update payload."}

logs.heading: 2 memories and 1 relevant solutions found

kvps.memories:
The tenant onboarding system uses a full-stack architecture with a SolidJS frontend (http://localhost:3005), a Hono-based API (port 3000), and a Supabase database. Upon successful registration/onboarding, the system redirects users to the local work orders login page at http://localhost:3004/.

Frontend routes include /tenants, /tenant/:id (TenantDetailPage.tsx), and a public /onboard form. Both the /onboard form and the Tenant Detail page's Organization Profile section include an 'industry' selection dropdown and a 'description' textarea.

The Hono API (apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts) provides the following endpoints:
- POST /onboard (Public onboarding)
- GET /onboard (List organizations)
- GET /onboard/trades (Retrieves distinct trade names from active app-scope templates for industry selection)
- GET/PATCH /onboard/:id (View/Edit profiles)
- GET/POST /onboard/:id/admins (Manage admin users)

Database management uses Supabase migrations (up to 20260223000001_org_industry_description.sql) via 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' or 'npm run db:migrate' with the '--local' flag. Migrations include 'NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';' to refresh the PostgREST cache.

Project 'voicelog-scaffold' Overview:

Architecture & Tech Stack:
- Frontend: SolidJS (Port 3005)
- API: Hono (Port 3000)
- Database/Auth: Supabase (Local development environment)

kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Implementing a multi-tenant onboarding system and organization management interface using SolidJS and Supabase, including handling industry-specific templates and profile field synchronization.

# Solution

### 1. Backend API & Database Architecture (Hono/Supabase)
- **Transaction Flow**: Use `service_role` to bypass RLS during onboarding. Create organization and admin user via `supabase.auth.admin.createUser` with `organization_id` in metadata.
- **Industry & Template Logic**: 
    - Create `GET /onboard/trades` to fetch master templates (`scope = 'app'`).
    - Implement `copyTemplatesToOrg`: If 'General Contractor', copy all; otherwise, filter by trade. Insert with `scope = 'company'` and the new `organization_id`.
- **Schema Maintenance**: Always include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` after migrations to refresh PostgREST cache.

### 2. Frontend Implementation (SolidJS/Tenant Portal)
- **Organization Profile Management**: To add missing fields (e.g., 'industry', 'description') to the UI:
    - Update the `OrgProfile` interface in `TenantDetailPage.tsx` to include the new nullable string fields.
    - Use `createResource` with a `fetchTrades` helper (calling `/onboard/trades`) to populate dropdowns.
    - Implement JSX for an industry `<select>` (including a manual 'General Contractor' option) and a description `<textarea>`.
    - Bind values to existing `update` and `dirty` state logic to ensure the Supabase update payload includes the new fields.
- **Admin Client**: Use a separate `supabaseAdmin` client with `persistSession: false` and a unique `storageKey` to avoid session conflicts.

### 3. Troubleshooting & Data Integrity
- **Missing Trades**: If trades are missing from dropdowns, verify they are promoted from `company` to `app` scope in `seed.sql` and that migrations are in the top-level `supabase/migrations/` folder.
- **Migration Application**: Run `supabase db reset` to apply migrations and execute seeding logic for template promotion.

kvps.memories:
The tenant onboarding system uses a full-stack architecture with a SolidJS frontend (http://localhost:3005), a Hono-based API (port 3000), and a Supabase database. Upon successful registration/onboarding, the system redirects users to the local work orders login page at http://localhost:3004/.

Frontend routes include /tenants, /tenant/:id (TenantDetailPage.tsx), and a public /onboard form. Both the /onboard form and the Tenant Detail page's Organization Profile section include an 'industry' selection dropdown and a 'description' textarea.

The Hono API (apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts) provides the following endpoints:
- POST /onboard (Public onboarding)
- GET /onboard (List organizations)
- GET /onboard/trades (Retrieves distinct trade names from active app-scope templates for industry selection)
- GET/PATCH /onboard/:id (View/Edit profiles)
- GET/POST /onboard/:id/admins (Manage admin users)

Database management uses Supabase migrations (up to 20260223000001_org_industry_description.sql) via 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' or 'npm run db:migrate' with the '--local' flag. Migrations include 'NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';' to refresh the PostgREST cache.

kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Implementing a multi-tenant onboarding system and organization management interface using SolidJS and Supabase, including handling industry-specific templates and profile field synchronization.

# Solution

### 1. Backend API & Database Architecture (Hono/Supabase)
- **Transaction Flow**: Use `service_role` to bypass RLS during onboarding. Create organization and admin user via `supabase.auth.admin.createUser` with `organization_id` in metadata.
- **Industry & Template Logic**: 
    - Create `GET /onboard/trades` to fetch master templates (`scope = 'app'`).
    - Implement `copyTemplatesToOrg`: If 'General Contractor', copy all; otherwise, filter by trade. Insert with `scope = 'company'` and the new `organization_id`.
- **Schema Maintenance**: Always include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` after migrations to refresh PostgREST cache.

### 2. Frontend Implementation (SolidJS/Tenant Portal)
- **Organization Profile Management**: To add missing fields (e.g., 'industry', 'description') to the UI:
    - Update the `OrgProfile` interface in `TenantDetailPage.tsx` to include the new nullable string fields.
    - Use `createResource` with a `fetchTrades` helper (calling `/onboard/trades`) to populate dropdowns.
    - Implement JSX for an industry `<select>` (including a manual 'General Contractor' option) and a description `<textarea>`.
    - Bind values to existing `update` and `dirty` state logic to ensure the Supabase update payload includes the new fields.
- **Admin Client**: Use a separate `supabaseAdmin` client with `persistSession: false` and a unique `storageKey` to avoid session conflicts.

### 3. Troubleshooting & Data Integrity
- **Missing Trades**: If trades are missing from dropdowns, verify they are promoted from `company` to `app` scope in `seed.sql` and that migrations are in the top-level `supabase/migrations/` folder.
- **Migration Application**: Run `supabase db reset` to apply migrations and execute seeding logic for template promotion.

# Problem
Supabase local migrations failing to apply, missing tables/columns, or PostgREST schema cache becoming stale (leading to 'Could not find column' errors) after database updates.

# Solution
Implement a robust local development workflow using a PowerShell script (`apply-migration.ps1`) to ensure consistency between the database, the API cache, and TypeScript types.

### 1. Automated Migration Script Logic
Create a script to sequence the following operations:
- **Environment Check**: Verify Supabase is running with `npx supabase status`. If down, run `npx supabase start`.
- **Apply Migrations**: Run `npx supabase migration up --local` to apply pending changes without affecting remote environments.
- **Refresh PostgREST Cache**: Force a schema reload to prevent stale API errors using: `npx supabase db execute --local -q "NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';"`.
- **Regenerate Types**: Update TypeScript definitions immediately (e.g., `pnpm db:types` or `npx supabase gen types typescript`).

### 2. Alternative & Manual Fixes
- **Manual Refresh**: Execute `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` in the Supabase SQL Editor.
- **Migration Integration**: Append the `NOTIFY` command to the end of `.sql` migration files for automatic refreshing.
- **Status Verification**: Use `supabase migration list --local` to identify unapplied migrations.
- **Hard Reset**: If the API fails to recognize changes after a notify command, run `supabase stop && supabase start`.

### 3. Local Connectivity
When accessing the local database from within other Docker containers, use `host.docker.internal` on port `54322`.

The Tenant Detail page (TenantDetailPage.tsx) Organization Profile section features an industry dropdown and description textarea (Migration 20260223000001). The industry dropdown is populated via the '/onboard/trades' API endpoint, which fetches distinct trade names from active templates and specifically includes a 'General Contractor' option.

The 'work_order_templates' table stores trade templates (id, organization_id, trade_name, definition, scope). App-scope templates (organization_id=NULL) act as masters, while company-scope templates for organization '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' are defined in 'supabase/seed.sql'. Specifically, the Painting template (ID: 20000000-...-001) includes Walls and Ceilings, and the Carpentry template (ID: 20000000-...-002) includes Baseboards, Closets, and Doors. The 'supabase/seed.sql' file contains logic to promote these company-scope templates to app-scope by replacing the '20000000' ID prefix with '30000000'. During onboarding (POST /onboard), 'General Contractor' assigns all app-scope templates, while specific trades assign only their respective templates.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Implementing a multi-tenant onboarding system and organization management interface using SolidJS and Supabase, including handling industry-specific templates and profile field synchronization.

# Solution

### 1. Backend API & Database Architecture (Hono/Supabase)
- **Transaction Flow**: Use `service_role` to bypass RLS during onboarding. Create organization and admin user via `supabase.auth.admin.createUser` with `organization_id` in metadata.
- **Industry & Template Logic**: 
    - Create `GET /onboard/trades` to fetch master templates (`scope = 'app'`).
    - Implement `copyTemplatesToOrg`: If 'General Contractor', copy all; otherwise, filter by trade. Insert with `scope = 'company'` and the new `organization_id`.
- **Schema Maintenance**: Always include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` after migrations to refresh PostgREST cache.

### 2. Frontend Implementation (SolidJS/Tenant Portal)
- **Organization Profile Management**: To add missing fields (e.g., 'industry', 'description') to the UI:
    - Update the `OrgProfile` interface in `TenantDetailPage.tsx` to include the new nullable string fields.
    - Use `createResource` with a `fetchTrades` helper (calling `/onboard/trades`) to populate dropdowns.
    - Implement JSX for an industry `<select>` (including a manual 'General Contractor' option) and a description `<textarea>`.
    - Bind values to existing `update` and `dirty` state logic to ensure the Supabase update payload includes the new fields.
- **Admin Client**: Use a separate `supabaseAdmin` client with `persistSession: false` and a unique `storageKey` to avoid session conflicts.

### 3. Troubleshooting & Data Integrity
- **Missing Trades**: If trades are missing from dropdowns, verify they are promoted from `company` to `app` scope in `seed.sql` and that migrations are in the top-level `supabase/migrations/` folder.
- **Migration Application**: Run `supabase db reset` to apply migrations and execute seeding logic for template promotion.

kvps.memories:
The tenant onboarding system uses a full-stack architecture with a SolidJS frontend (http://localhost:3005), a Hono-based API (port 3000), and a Supabase database. Upon successful registration/onboarding, the system redirects users to the local work orders login page at http://localhost:3004/.

Frontend routes include /tenants, /tenant/:id (TenantDetailPage.tsx), and a public /onboard form. Both the /onboard form and the Tenant Detail page's Organization Profile section include an 'industry' selection dropdown and a 'description' textarea.

The Hono API (apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts) provides the following endpoints:
- POST /onboard (Public onboarding)
- GET /onboard (List organizations)
- GET /onboard/trades (Retrieves distinct trade names from active app-scope templates for industry selection)
- GET/PATCH /onboard/:id (View/Edit profiles)
- GET/POST /onboard/:id/admins (Manage admin users)

Database management uses Supabase migrations (up to 20260223000001_org_industry_description.sql) via 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' or 'npm run db:migrate' with the '--local' flag. Migrations include 'NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';' to refresh the PostgREST cache.

The Tenant Detail page (TenantDetailPage.tsx) Organization Profile section features an industry dropdown and description textarea (Migration 20260223000001). The industry dropdown is populated via the '/onboard/trades' API endpoint, which fetches distinct trade names from active templates and specifically includes a 'General Contractor' option.

kvps.memories:
The project uses a monorepo structure with a SolidJS frontend (tenant-portal) and a Hono API (api).

The tenant onboarding system is implemented in the `voicelog-scaffold` codebase.

The `organizations` table includes `industry` and `description` columns added via migration `20260223000001_org_industry_description.sql`.

The `TenantDetailPage.tsx` component in `apps/tenant-portal` was updated to include an industry dropdown and a description textarea in the Organization Profile section.

The industry dropdown is populated from the `/onboard/trades` API endpoint, which lists distinct trade names from app-scope templates.

The `PATCH /onboard/:id` API endpoint in `apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts` was updated to reassign trade templates when the industry is changed: 'General Contractor' assigns all trades, while specific industries assign only matching trades.

The `TenantDetailPage.tsx` was modified to use the `PATCH /onboard/:id` API for saving profile changes to ensure trade reassignment logic is triggered.

A sysadmin user was created with email `sysadmin@example.com`, password `sysadmin123`, and role `sysadmin` for access to the portal at `http://localhost:3005/`.

A setup script `scripts/create-sysadmin.sh` was created to recreate the sysadmin user after database resets.

Successful onboarding redirects users to the work orders login page at `http://localhost:3004/`.

The industry dropdown itself is populated via the `GET /onboard/trades` endpoint which fetches distinct trade names from active app-scope templates.

The Hono API (apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts) provides the following endpoints:
- POST /onboard (Public onboarding): Assigns trade templates based on industry ('General Contractor' assigns all; specific trades assign matching templates).
- GET /onboard (List organizations)
- GET /onboard/trades (Retrieves distinct trade names from active app-scope templates for industry selection)
- GET/PATCH /onboard/:id (View/Edit profiles): The PATCH endpoint reassigns trade templates when the industry is changed, following the same logic as the POST endpoint.
- GET/POST /onboard/:id/admins (Manage admin users)

Database management uses Supabase migrations (up to 20260223000001_org_industry_description.sql) via 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' or 'npm run db:migrate' with the '--local' flag. Migrations include 'NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';' to refresh the PostgREST cache.

Write-Host "`n=== Step 1: Killing dev servers ===" -ForegroundColor Cyan

npx tsx scripts/create_user.ts
Pop-Location

Push-Location "$PSScriptRoot\.."
pnpm dev

-- =============================================================
-- Migration: Row Level Security
-- Description: Enable RLS and create tenant isolation policies
-- =============================================================

-- Enable RLS on application tables
ALTER TABLE public.organizations ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.rooms ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.units ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.unit_rooms ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.unit_documents ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.work_orders ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.work_order_tasks ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.work_order_notes ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.work_order_templates ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.template_libraries ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.project_documents ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.document_images ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.image_links ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;


-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Organizations
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE POLICY "Users can view their organization"
  ON public.organizations FOR SELECT
  USING (id = current_user_organization_id());

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Rooms
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE POLICY "Tenant isolation for rooms"
  ON public.rooms FOR ALL
  USING (organization_id = current_user_organization_id());

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Units
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE POLICY "Tenant isolation for units"
  ON public.units FOR ALL
  USING (organization_id = current_user_organization_id());

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Unit Rooms
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE POLICY "Users can view unit rooms from their organization"
  ON public.unit_rooms FOR SELECT
  USING (EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM units WHERE units.id = unit_rooms.unit_id
    AND units.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()
  ));

CREATE POLICY "Users can insert unit rooms for their organization"
  ON public.unit_rooms FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM units WHERE units.id = unit_rooms.unit_id
    AND units.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()
  ));

CREATE POLICY "Users can update unit rooms from their organization"
  ON public.unit_rooms FOR UPDATE
  USING (EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM units WHERE units.id = unit_rooms.unit_id
    AND units.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()
  ));

CREATE POLICY "Users can delete unit rooms from their organization"
  ON public.unit_rooms FOR DELETE
  USING (EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM units WHERE units.id = unit_rooms.unit_id
    AND units.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()
  ));

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Unit Documents
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE POLICY "Users can view unit documents from their organization"
  ON public.unit_documents FOR SELECT
  USING (EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM units WHERE units.id = unit_documents.unit_id
    AND units.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()
  ));

CREATE POLICY "Users can link documents to their organization units"
  ON public.unit_documents FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM units WHERE units.id = unit_documents.unit_id
    AND units.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()
  ));

CREATE POLICY "Users can delete unit document links from their organization"
  ON public.unit_documents FOR DELETE
  USING (EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM units WHERE units.id = unit_documents.unit_id
    AND units.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()
  ));

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Work Orders
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE POLICY "Tenant isolation for work orders"
  ON public.work_orders FOR ALL
  USING (organization_id = current_user_organization_id());

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Work Order Tasks
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE POLICY "Tenant isolation for tasks"
  ON public.work_order_tasks FOR ALL
  USING (EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM work_orders
    WHERE work_orders.id = work_order_tasks.work_order_id
    AND work_orders.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()
  ));

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Work Order Notes
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE POLICY "Tenant isolation for notes"
  ON public.work_order_notes FOR ALL
  USING (EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM work_orders
    WHERE work_orders.id = work_order_notes.work_order_id
    AND work_orders.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()
  ));

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Work Order Templates (3-tier: app, company, project)
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE POLICY "Tenant isolation for templates"
  ON public.work_order_templates FOR ALL
  USING (organization_id = current_user_organization_id());

CREATE POLICY "app_templates_select"
  ON public.work_order_templates FOR SELECT
  USING (scope = 'app');

CREATE POLICY "company_templates_select"
  ON public.work_order_templates FOR SELECT
  USING (scope = 'company' AND organization_id = current_user_organization_id());

CREATE POLICY "company_templates_modify"
  ON public.work_order_templates FOR ALL
  USING (scope = 'company' AND organization_id = current_user_organization_id());

CREATE POLICY "project_templates_select"
  ON public.work_order_templates FOR SELECT
  USING (scope = 'project' AND organization_id = current_user_organization_id());

CREATE POLICY "project_templates_modify"
  ON public.work_order_templates FOR ALL
  USING (scope = 'project' AND organization_id = current_user_organization_id());

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- WO Projects
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE POLICY "wo_projects_tenant_isolation"
  ON public.wo_projects FOR ALL
  USING (organization_id = current_user_organization_id());

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Template Libraries (3-tier: app, company)
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE POLICY "app_libraries_select"
  ON public.template_libraries FOR SELECT
  USING (scope = 'app');

CREATE POLICY "company_libraries_select"
  ON public.template_libraries FOR SELECT
  USING (scope = 'company' AND organization_id = current_user_organization_id());

CREATE POLICY "company_libraries_modify"
  ON public.template_libraries FOR ALL
  USING (scope = 'company' AND organization_id = current_user_organization_id());

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Project Documents
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE POLICY "Users can view documents from their organization"
  ON public.project_documents FOR SELECT
  USING (get_project_organization_id(project_id) = current_user_organization_id());

CREATE POLICY "Users can insert documents to their organization projects"
  ON public.project_documents FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (get_project_organization_id(project_id) = current_user_organization_id());

CREATE POLICY "Users can update documents from their organization"
  ON public.project_documents FOR UPDATE
  USING (get_project_organization_id(project_id) = current_user_organization_id());

CREATE POLICY "Users can delete documents from their organization"
  ON public.project_documents FOR DELETE
  USING (get_project_organization_id(project_id) = current_user_organization_id());

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Document Images
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE POLICY "Users can view images from their organization documents"
  ON public.document_images FOR SELECT
  USING (EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM project_documents
    WHERE project_documents.id = document_images.document_id
    AND get_project_organization_id(project_documents.project_id) = current_user_organization_id()
  ));

CREATE POLICY "Users can insert images to their organization documents"
  ON public.document_images FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM project_documents
    WHERE project_documents.id = document_images.document_id
    AND get_project_organization_id(project_documents.project_id) = current_user_organization_id()
  ));

CREATE POLICY "Users can update images from their organization documents"
  ON public.document_images FOR UPDATE
  USING (EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM project_documents
    WHERE project_documents.id = document_images.document_id
    AND get_project_organization_id(project_documents.project_id) = current_user_organization_id()
  ));

CREATE POLICY "Users can delete images from their organization documents"
  ON public.document_images FOR DELETE
  USING (EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM project_documents
    WHERE project_documents.id = document_images.document_id
    AND get_project_organization_id(project_documents.project_id) = current_user_organization_id()
  ));

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Image Links
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE POLICY "Users can view image links from their organization"
  ON public.image_links FOR SELECT
  USING (
    (entity_type = 'unit' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM units WHERE units.id = image_links.entity_id AND units.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()))
    OR (entity_type = 'work_order' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM work_orders WHERE work_orders.id = image_links.entity_id AND work_orders.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()))
    OR (entity_type = 'room' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM rooms WHERE rooms.id = image_links.entity_id AND rooms.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()))
  );

CREATE POLICY "Users can create image links for their organization entities"
  ON public.image_links FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (
    (entity_type = 'unit' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM units WHERE units.id = image_links.entity_id AND units.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()))
    OR (entity_type = 'work_order' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM work_orders WHERE work_orders.id = image_links.entity_id AND work_orders.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()))
    OR (entity_type = 'room' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM rooms WHERE rooms.id = image_links.entity_id AND rooms.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()))
  );

CREATE POLICY "Users can delete image links from their organization"
  ON public.image_links FOR DELETE
  USING (
    (entity_type = 'unit' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM units WHERE units.id = image_links.entity_id AND units.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()))
    OR (entity_type = 'work_order' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM work_orders WHERE work_orders.id = image_links.entity_id AND work_orders.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()))
    OR (entity_type = 'room' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM rooms WHERE rooms.id = image_links.entity_id AND rooms.organization_id = current_user_organization_id()))
  );

-- =============================================================
-- Migration: Utility Functions
-- Description: Helper functions used by tables, triggers, and RLS
-- =============================================================


CREATE FUNCTION public.current_user_organization_id() RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
BEGIN
    -- For now, return from auth.users metadata
    -- In production, this would query a user_organizations table
    RETURN (auth.jwt() -> 'user_metadata' ->> 'organization_id')::UUID;
END;
$$;



CREATE FUNCTION public.get_default_library_for_type(p_project_type text) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE sql STABLE
    AS $$
    SELECT id FROM template_libraries
    WHERE project_type = p_project_type
    AND scope = 'app'
    AND is_default = true
    AND is_active = true
    LIMIT 1;
$$;



CREATE FUNCTION public.get_project_organization_id(p_project_id uuid) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
    SELECT organization_id FROM wo_projects WHERE id = p_project_id;
$$;



CREATE FUNCTION public.search_agent_memories(query_embedding public.vector, match_threshold double precision DEFAULT 0.7, match_count integer DEFAULT 5, filter_area text DEFAULT NULL::text, filter_source text DEFAULT NULL::text, filter_metadata jsonb DEFAULT NULL::jsonb) RETURNS TABLE(id uuid, content text, metadata jsonb, area text, source text, similarity double precision, created_at timestamp with time zone)
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT
    am.id,
    am.content,
    am.metadata,
    am.area,
    am.source,
    1 - (am.embedding <=> query_embedding) AS similarity,
    am.created_at
  FROM agent_memories am
  WHERE
    1 - (am.embedding <=> query_embedding) > match_threshold
    AND (filter_area IS NULL OR am.area = filter_area)
    AND (filter_source IS NULL OR am.source = filter_source)
    AND (filter_metadata IS NULL OR am.metadata @> filter_metadata)
  ORDER BY am.embedding <=> query_embedding
  LIMIT match_count;
END;
$$;



CREATE FUNCTION public.update_agent_memories_timestamp() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
BEGIN
  NEW.updated_at = now();
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;



CREATE FUNCTION public.update_updated_at_column() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
BEGIN
    NEW.updated_at = now();
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;



CREATE FUNCTION public.upsert_agent_memory(p_content text, p_embedding public.vector, p_metadata jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb, p_area text DEFAULT 'main'::text, p_source text DEFAULT 'agent_zero'::text, p_source_memory_id text DEFAULT NULL::text) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
  result_id UUID;
BEGIN
  -- If source_memory_id provided, upsert by it
  IF p_source_memory_id IS NOT NULL THEN
    INSERT INTO agent_memories (content, embedding, metadata, area, source, source_memory_id)
    VALUES (p_content, p_embedding, p_metadata, p_area, p_source, p_source_memory_id)
    ON CONFLICT (source, source_memory_id)
      WHERE source_memory_id IS NOT NULL
    DO UPDATE SET
      content = EXCLUDED.content,
      embedding = EXCLUDED.embedding,
      metadata = EXCLUDED.metadata,
      area = EXCLUDED.area
    RETURNING id INTO result_id;
  ELSE
    INSERT INTO agent_memories (content, embedding, metadata, area, source)
    VALUES (p_content, p_embedding, p_metadata, p_area, p_source)
    RETURNING id INTO result_id;
  END IF;

  RETURN result_id;
END;
$$;

    "headline": "Creating sysadmin RLS policies migration",
    "tool_name": "code_execution_tool",
    "tool_args": {
        "runtime": "python",
        "session": 0,
        "reset": false,
        "code": "migration = """-- =============================================================
-- Migration: Sysadmin RLS Policies
-- Description: Add is_sysadmin() helper and full-access policies
--              for users with role='sysadmin' in user_metadata.
--              Pages at localhost:3005 are sysadmin-only.
-- =============================================================

-- Helper: returns TRUE when the authenticated user has role = 'sysadmin'
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.is_sysadmin() RETURNS boolean
    LANGUAGE plpgsql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
BEGIN
    RETURN coalesce(
        (auth.jwt() -> 'user_metadata' ->> 'role') = 'sysadmin',
        false
    );
END;
$$;

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Sysadmin full-access policies for every
-- RLS-enabled table (SELECT + ALL operations)
-- -----------------------------------------------

-- Organizations
CREATE POLICY "sysadmin_full_access_organizations"
  ON public.organizations FOR ALL
  USING (is_sysadmin())
  WITH CHECK (is_sysadmin());

-- Rooms
CREATE POLICY "sysadmin_full_access_rooms"
  ON public.rooms FOR ALL
  USING (is_sysadmin())
  WITH CHECK (is_sysadmin());

  USING (is_sysadmin())
  WITH CHECK (is_sysadmin());

  WITH CHECK (is_sysadmin());

  USING (is_sysadmin())
  WITH CHECK (is_sysadmin());

-- Work Orders
CREATE POLICY "sysadmin_full_access_work_orders"
  ON public.work_orders FOR ALL
  USING (is_sysadmin())
  WITH CHECK (is_sysadmin());

-- Work Order Tasks
CREATE POLICY "sysadmin_full_access_work_order_tasks"
  ON public.work_order_tasks FOR ALL
  USING (is_sysadmin())
  WITH CHECK (is_sysadmin());

-- Work Order Notes

  USING (is_sysadmin())
  WITH CHECK (is_sysadmin());

-- Work Order Templates

  USING (is_sysadmin())

-- Template Libraries

-- WO Projects
CREATE POLICY "sysadmin_full_access_wo_projects"

-- Project Documents

-- Document Images

-- Image Links

  ON public.image_links FOR ALL
  USING (is_sysadmin())
  WITH CHECK (is_sysadmin());

-- Notify PostgREST to reload schema cache
NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';
"""

print(f'Created: {path}')"

}
