kvps.memories:
Project 'voicelog-scaffold' Overview:

Architecture & Tech Stack:
- Frontend: SolidJS (Port 3005)
- API: Hono (Port 3000)
- Database/Auth: Supabase (Local development environment)

Project Locations:
- Local Path: C:\Users\3900X\Documents\GitHub\agentZero\voicelog-scaffold
- Agent Environment Path: /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold

Infrastructure Details:
- Local Supabase dashboard/services: http://127.0.0.1:54321
- PostgreSQL database: postgresql://postgres:postgres@127.0.0.1:54322/postgres (or host.docker.internal:54322 from within containers)
- API layer: PostgREST and Hono.

A sysadmin user (email: `sysadmin@example.com`, password: `sysadmin123`, role: `sysadmin`) was created for the portal at `http://localhost:3005/`. To facilitate database resets, a setup script `scripts/create-sysadmin.sh` was created to automate the recreation of this user.

The 'supabaseAdmin' client in the project is configured with the service_role key and 'persistSession: false' to bypass RLS for administrative tasks.

The tenant onboarding system is implemented in the `voicelog-scaffold` codebase.

> [!NOTE]
> Since v0.9.7, custom prompts belong in `/a0/agents/<agent_name>/prompts/` rather than a shared `/prompts` folder. See the [Extensions guide](../developer/extensions.md#prompts) for details.

> [!NOTE]
> The Hacker profile is included in the main image. After launch, choose the **hacker** agent profile in Settings if you want the security-focused prompts and tooling. The "hacker" branch is deprecated.

![settings](../res/setup/settings/1-agentConfig.png)

### Chat Model Settings

- **Provider:** Select the chat model provider (e.g., Anthropic)
- **Model Name:** Choose the specific model (e.g., claude-sonnet-4-5)
- **Context Length:** Set the maximum token limit for context window
- **Context Window Space:** Configure how much of the context window is dedicated to chat history

![chat model settings](../res/setup/settings/2-chat-model.png)

**Model naming is provider-specific.**

Use `claude-sonnet-4-5` for Anthropic, but use `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5` for OpenRouter. If you see "Invalid model ID," verify the provider and naming format on the provider website, or search the web for "<name-of-ai-model> model naming".

> [!TIP]
> **Context window tuning:** Set the total context window size first (for example, 100k), then adjust the chat history portion as a fraction of that total. A large fraction on a very large context window can still be enormous.

> [!TIP]
> **API URL:** URL of the API endpoint for the chat model - only needed for some providers like Ollama, LM Studio, Azure, etc.

### Utility Model Configuration

- **Provider & Model:** Select a model for utility tasks like memory organization and summarization
- **Temperature:** Adjust the determinism of utility responses

> [!NOTE]
> Utility models need to be strong enough to extract and consolidate memory reliably. Very small models (e.g., 4B) often fail at this; 70B-class models or high-quality cloud "flash/mini" models work best.

### Embedding Model Settings [Optional]

- **Provider:** Choose the embedding model provider (e.g., OpenAI)
- **Model Name:** Select the specific embedding model (e.g., text-embedding-3-small)

> [!NOTE]
> Agent Zero uses a local embedding model by default (runs on CPU), but you can switch to OpenAI embeddings like `text-embedding-3-small` or `text-embedding-3-large` if preferred.

### Speech to Text Options

- **Model Size:** Choose the speech recognition model size
- **Language Code:** Set the primary language for voice recognition
- **Silence Settings:** Configure silence threshold, duration, and timeout parameters for voice input

### API Keys

Configure API keys for various service providers directly within the Web UI. Click `Save` to confirm your settings.

> [!NOTE]
> **OpenAI API vs Plus subscription:** A ChatGPT Plus subscription does not include API credits. You must provide a separate API key for OpenAI usage in Agent Zero.

> [!TIP]
> For OpenAI-compatible providers (e.g., custom gateways or Z.AI/GLM), add the API key under **External Services → Other OpenAI-compatible API keys**, then select **OpenAI Compatible** as the provider in model settings.

> [!CAUTION]
> **GitHub Copilot Provider:** When using the GitHub Copilot provider, after selecting the model and entering your first prompt, the OAuth login procedure will begin. You'll find the authentication code and link in the output logs. Complete the authentication process by following the provided link and entering the code, then you may continue using Agent Zero.

### Authentication

- **UI Login:** Set username for web interface access
- **UI Password:** Configure password for web interface security
- **Root Password:** Manage Docker container root password for SSH access

![settings](../res/setup/settings/3-auth.png)

### Development Settings

- **RFC Parameters (local instances only):** Configure URLs and ports for remote function calls between instances
- **RFC Password:** Configure password for remote function calls
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Supabase local development issues: migrations failing, stale PostgREST schema cache, or losing sysadmin users after database resets.

# Solution
Implement a robust local development workflow and persistence strategy for Supabase.

### 1. Automated Migration & Cache Refresh
Use a script (e.g., `apply-migration.ps1`) to sequence operations:
- **Environment Check**: Verify status with `npx supabase status`; start if necessary.
- **Apply Migrations**: Run `npx supabase migration up --local`.
- **Refresh PostgREST Cache**: Prevent 'Could not find column' errors by forcing a reload: `npx supabase db execute --local -q "NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';"`.
- **Regenerate Types**: Run `npx supabase gen types typescript` immediately after migrations.

### 2. Persistent Sysadmin User Creation
To maintain a sysadmin user across `supabase db reset`, create a shell script (`scripts/create-sysadmin.sh`) using the Admin API:
```bash
# Use host.docker.internal:54321 if running inside a container
curl -X POST "http://localhost:54321/auth/v1/admin/users" \
  -H "apikey: SERVICE_ROLE_KEY" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer SERVICE_ROLE_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "email": "sysadmin@example.com",
    "password": "sysadmin123",
    "email_confirm": true,
    "user_metadata": { "role": "sysadmin", "full_name": "System Administrator" }
  }'
```

### 3. Local Connectivity & Troubleshooting
- **Docker Networking**: Access the local DB from other containers via `host.docker.internal` on port `54322` (DB) or `54321` (API).
- **Manual Refresh**: Execute `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` in the SQL Editor if the API is stale.
- **Hard Reset**: If issues persist, run `supabase stop && supabase start`.

# Problem
Implementing a multi-tenant onboarding system and organization management interface using SolidJS and Supabase, including handling industry-specific templates, profile field synchronization, and automatic template re-assignment upon industry updates.

# Solution

### 1. Backend API & Database Architecture (Hono/Supabase)
- **Transaction Flow**: Use `service_role` to bypass RLS during onboarding. Create organization and admin user via `supabase.auth.admin.createUser` with `organization_id` in metadata.
- **Industry & Template Logic**: 
    - Create `GET /onboard/trades` to fetch master templates (`scope = 'app'`).
    - **Automatic Assignment (PATCH /onboard/:id)**: Detect industry changes by comparing `oldIndustry` with the new payload. If changed, delete existing `company` scope templates (where `copied_from_template_id` is not null) and trigger `copyTemplatesToOrg`.
    - **copyTemplatesToOrg**: If 'General Contractor', copy all; otherwise, filter by trade. Insert with `scope = 'company'` and the new `organization_id`.
- **Schema Maintenance**: Always include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` after migrations to refresh PostgREST cache.

### 2. Frontend Implementation (SolidJS/Tenant Portal)
- **Organization Profile Management**: Update `TenantDetailPage.tsx` to include `industry` and `description` fields.
- **Data Fetching**: Use `createResource` to fetch trades from `${API}/onboard/trades`.
- **Save Logic**: Use the `PATCH` API endpoint (`${API}/onboard/${orgId}`) instead of direct Supabase client updates to ensure server-side industry logic (template copying) is triggered.
- **UI Components**: 
    - **Industry**: Use a `<select>` dropdown mapping through `trades()`.
    - **Description**: Use a `<textarea>` with `rows={3}`.
    - **State Binding**: Bind values using `(dirty() ? { ...org(), ...draft() } : org())` and handle changes via `update('field', value)`.

### 3. Code Snippets

**Backend Industry Update Logic:**
```typescript
let oldIndustry = (await adminClient.from('organizations').select('industry').eq('id', id).single()).data?.industry;
const newIndustry = payload.industry;
if (newIndustry !== undefined && newIndustry !== oldIndustry) {
  await adminClient.from('work_order_templates').delete().eq('organization_id', id).eq('scope', 'company').not('copied_from_template_id', 'is', null);
  newIndustry === 'General Contractor' ? await copyTemplatesToOrg(id) : await copyTemplatesToOrg(id, newIndustry);
}
```

**Frontend Save Implementation:**
```typescript
const saveProfile = async () => {
  const payload = dirty() ? draft() : {};
  await fetch(`${API}/onboard/${props.orgId}`, {
    method: 'PATCH',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify(payload),
  });
};
```

### 4. Troubleshooting & Data Integrity
- **Missing Trades**: Verify trades are promoted from `company` to `app` scope in `seed.sql`.
- **Migration Application**: Run `supabase db reset` to apply migrations.
- **Admin Client**: Use a separate `supabaseAdmin` client with `persistSession: false` to avoid session conflicts.

logs.heading: A0: Updating reset-and-start.ps1 to include sysadmin creation
logs.content:
{
    "thoughts": [
        "Good, the migration is created. Now I need to update the reset-and-start.ps1 script to include the create-sysadmin.sh call. Since it's a PowerShell script running on Windows, I need to add a step that calls the sysadmin creation script. On Windows, I can use curl or invoke the script via bash/wsl, but since the script uses curl which is available on Windows, I'll adapt it to use PowerShell's Invoke-RestMethod or just call the script differently.",
        "",
        "Actually, looking at the PS1 script, it runs npx and pnpm commands directly - it's running on the host Windows machine. The create-sysadmin.sh is a bash script. I should either:",
        "1. Create a PowerShell equivalent, or",
        "2. Use curl.exe which is available on Windows 10+",
        "",
        "Let me create a PowerShell-native step in the reset-and-start.ps1 that creates the sysadmin user."
    ],
    "headline": "Updating reset-and-start.ps1 to include sysadmin creation",
    "tool_name": "code_execution_tool",
    "tool_args": {
        "runtime": "python",
        "session": 0,
        "reset": false,
        "code": "path = '/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/scripts/reset-and-start.ps1'

with open(path, 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Add sysadmin creation step between user seeding and dev server start
old_step4 = 'Write-Host "`n=== Step 4: Starting dev servers ===" -ForegroundColor Cyan'

new_steps = '''Write-Host "`n=== Step 4: Creating sysadmin user ===" -ForegroundColor Cyan
$sysadminBody = @{
    email          = "sysadmin@example.com"
    password       = "sysadmin123"
    email_confirm  = $true
    user_metadata  = @{
        role      = "sysadmin"
        full_name = "System Administrator"
    }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3

$serviceRoleKey = "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZS1kZW1vIiwicm9sZSI6InNlcnZpY2Vfcm9sZSIsImV4cCI6MTk4MzgxMjk5Nn0.EGIM96RAZx35lJzdJsyH-qQwv8Hdp7fsn3W0YpN81IU"
try {
    $response = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "http://127.0.0.1:54321/auth/v1/admin/users" `
        -Method Post `
        -Headers @{
            "apikey"        = $serviceRoleKey
            "Authorization" = "Bearer $serviceRoleKey"
            "Content-Type"  = "application/json"
        } `
        -Body $sysadminBody
    Write-Host "  Sysadmin created: $($response.email)" -ForegroundColor Green
} catch {
    Write-Host "  Sysadmin creation skipped (may already exist): $($_.Exception.Message)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
}

Write-Host "`n=== Step 5: Starting dev servers ===" -ForegroundColor Cyan'''

content = content.replace(old_step4, new_steps)

with open(path, 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)

print('reset-and-start.ps1 updated with sysadmin creation step')"
    }
}

thoughts: Good, the migration is created. Now I need to update the reset-and-start.ps1 script to include the create-sysadmin.sh call. kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Supabase local development issues: migrations failing, stale PostgREST schema cache, or losing sysadmin users after database resets.

# Solution
Implement a robust local development workflow and persistence strategy for Supabase.

### 1. Automated Migration & Cache Refresh
Use a script (e.g., `apply-migration.ps1`) to sequence operations:
- **Environment Check**: Verify status with `npx supabase status`; start if necessary.
- **Apply Migrations**: Run `npx supabase migration up --local`.
- **Refresh PostgREST Cache**: Prevent 'Could not find column' errors by forcing a reload: `npx supabase db execute --local -q "NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';"`.
- **Regenerate Types**: Run `npx supabase gen types typescript` immediately after migrations.

### 2. Persistent Sysadmin User Creation
To maintain a sysadmin user across `supabase db reset`, create a shell script (`scripts/create-sysadmin.sh`) using the Admin API:
```bash
# Use host.docker.internal:54321 if running inside a container
curl -X POST "http://localhost:54321/auth/v1/admin/users" \
  -H "apikey: SERVICE_ROLE_KEY" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer SERVICE_ROLE_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "email": "sysadmin@example.com",
    "password": "sysadmin123",
    "email_confirm": true,
    "user_metadata": { "role": "sysadmin", "full_name": "System Administrator" }
  }'
```

### 3. Local Connectivity & Troubleshooting
- **Docker Networking**: Access the local DB from other containers via `host.docker.internal` on port `54322` (DB) or `54321` (API).
- **Manual Refresh**: Execute `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` in the SQL Editor if the API is stale.
- **Hard Reset**: If issues persist, run `supabase stop && supabase start`.

kvps.memories:
The 'supabaseAdmin' client in the project is configured with the service_role key and 'persistSession: false' to bypass RLS for administrative tasks.

Successful onboarding redirects users to the work orders login page at `http://localhost:3004/`.

The project uses a monorepo structure containing a SolidJS frontend (tenant-portal) and a Hono API (api). The tenant onboarding system uses this full-stack architecture with the frontend at http://localhost:3005, the Hono-based API at port 3000, and a Supabase database. Upon successful registration/onboarding, the system redirects users to the local work orders login page at http://localhost:3004/.

Frontend routes include /tenants, /tenant/:id (TenantDetailPage.tsx), and a public /onboard form. Both the /onboard form and the Tenant Detail page's Organization Profile section include an 'industry' selection dropdown and a 'description' textarea.

The Hono API (apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts) provides the following endpoints:
- POST /onboard (Public onboarding): Assigns trade templates based on industry ('General Contractor' assigns all; specific trades assign matching templates).
- GET /onboard (List organizations)
- GET /onboard/trades (Retrieves distinct trade names from active app-scope templates for industry selection)
- GET/PATCH /onboard/:id (View/Edit profiles): The PATCH endpoint reassigns trade templates when the industry is changed, following the same logic as the POST endpoint.
- GET/POST /onboard/:id/admins (Manage admin users)

Database management uses Supabase migrations (up to 20260223000001_org_industry_description.sql) via 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' or 'npm run db:migrate' with the '--local' flag. Migrations include 'NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';' to refresh the PostgREST cache.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Supabase local development issues: migrations failing, stale PostgREST schema cache, or losing sysadmin users after database resets.

# Solution
Implement a robust local development workflow and persistence strategy for Supabase.

### 1. Automated Migration & Cache Refresh
Use a script (e.g., `apply-migration.ps1`) to sequence operations:
- **Environment Check**: Verify status with `npx supabase status`; start if necessary.
- **Apply Migrations**: Run `npx supabase migration up --local`.
- **Refresh PostgREST Cache**: Prevent 'Could not find column' errors by forcing a reload: `npx supabase db execute --local -q "NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';"`.
- **Regenerate Types**: Run `npx supabase gen types typescript` immediately after migrations.

### 2. Persistent Sysadmin User Creation
To maintain a sysadmin user across `supabase db reset`, create a shell script (`scripts/create-sysadmin.sh`) using the Admin API:
```bash
# Use host.docker.internal:54321 if running inside a container
curl -X POST "http://localhost:54321/auth/v1/admin/users" \
  -H "apikey: SERVICE_ROLE_KEY" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer SERVICE_ROLE_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "email": "sysadmin@example.com",
    "password": "sysadmin123",
    "email_confirm": true,
    "user_metadata": { "role": "sysadmin", "full_name": "System Administrator" }
  }'
```

### 3. Local Connectivity & Troubleshooting
- **Docker Networking**: Access the local DB from other containers via `host.docker.internal` on port `54322` (DB) or `54321` (API).
- **Manual Refresh**: Execute `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` in the SQL Editor if the API is stale.
- **Hard Reset**: If issues persist, run `supabase stop && supabase start`.

# Problem
Implementing a multi-tenant onboarding system and organization management interface using SolidJS and Supabase, including handling industry-specific templates, profile field synchronization, and automatic template re-assignment upon industry updates.

# Solution

### 1. Backend API & Database Architecture (Hono/Supabase)
- **Transaction Flow**: Use `service_role` to bypass RLS during onboarding. Create organization and admin user via `supabase.auth.admin.createUser` with `organization_id` in metadata.
- **Industry & Template Logic**: 
    - Create `GET /onboard/trades` to fetch master templates (`scope = 'app'`).
    - **Automatic Assignment (PATCH /onboard/:id)**: Detect industry changes by comparing `oldIndustry` with the new payload. If changed, delete existing `company` scope templates (where `copied_from_template_id` is not null) and trigger `copyTemplatesToOrg`.
    - **copyTemplatesToOrg**: If 'General Contractor', copy all; otherwise, filter by trade. Insert with `scope = 'company'` and the new `organization_id`.
- **Schema Maintenance**: Always include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` after migrations to refresh PostgREST cache.

### 2. Frontend Implementation (SolidJS/Tenant Portal)
- **Organization Profile Management**: Update `TenantDetailPage.tsx` to include `industry` and `description` fields.
- **Data Fetching**: Use `createResource` to fetch trades from `${API}/onboard/trades`.
- **Save Logic**: Use the `PATCH` API endpoint (`${API}/onboard/${orgId}`) instead of direct Supabase client updates to ensure server-side industry logic (template copying) is triggered.
- **UI Components**: 
    - **Industry**: Use a `<select>` dropdown mapping through `trades()`.
    - **Description**: Use a `<textarea>` with `rows={3}`.
    - **State Binding**: Bind values using `(dirty() ? { ...org(), ...draft() } : org())` and handle changes via `update('field', value)`.

### 3. Code Snippets

**Backend Industry Update Logic:**
```typescript
let oldIndustry = (await adminClient.from('organizations').select('industry').eq('id', id).single()).data?.industry;
const newIndustry = payload.industry;
if (newIndustry !== undefined && newIndustry !== oldIndustry) {
  await adminClient.from('work_order_templates').delete().eq('organization_id', id).eq('scope', 'company').not('copied_from_template_id', 'is', null);
  newIndustry === 'General Contractor' ? await copyTemplatesToOrg(id) : await copyTemplatesToOrg(id, newIndustry);
}
```

**Frontend Save Implementation:**
```typescript
const saveProfile = async () => {
  const payload = dirty() ? draft() : {};
  await fetch(`${API}/onboard/${props.orgId}`, {
    method: 'PATCH',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify(payload),
  });
};
```

### 4. Troubleshooting & Data Integrity
- **Missing Trades**: Verify trades are promoted from `company` to `app` scope in `seed.sql`.
- **Migration Application**: Run `supabase db reset` to apply migrations.
- **Admin Client**: Use a separate `supabaseAdmin` client with `persistSession: false` to avoid session conflicts.

All done. Here's what was implemented:

---

### 1. ✅ Sysadmin RLS Policies

**New migration**: `20260223000002_sysadmin_rls_policies.sql`

- Created `is_sysadmin()` helper function — checks `auth.jwt() -> user_metadata ->> 'role' = 'sysadmin'`
- Added **full-access policies** (`FOR ALL` with `USING` + `WITH CHECK`) on all 14 RLS-enabled tables:
  - `organizations`, `rooms`, `units`, `unit_rooms`, `unit_documents`
  - `work_orders`, `work_order_tasks`, `work_order_notes`
  - `work_order_templates`, `template_libraries`
  - `wo_projects`, `project_documents`, `document_images`, `image_links`
- Sysadmin can read/write **all data across all tenants** at the database level

### 2. ✅ Frontend Sysadmin Gate (localhost:3005)

**Updated**: `apps/tenant-portal/src/App.tsx`

- Added `isSysadmin()` check that validates `session.user.user_metadata.role === 'sysadmin'`
- **On login**: if the user is not a sysadmin, they are immediately signed out and shown an "Access Denied" screen with a clear message: *"This portal is restricted to system administrators only."*
- **On page load**: existing sessions from non-sysadmin users are also rejected
- The `/onboard` route remains **public** (no auth required)
- Two layers of protection: **frontend gate** + **database RLS policies**

### 3. ✅ Updated `scripts/reset-and-start.ps1`

Added **Step 4** (between user seeding and dev server start) that creates the sysadmin user via PowerShell's `Invoke-RestMethod`:

```
Step 1: Kill dev servers
Step 2: Reset database (applies all migrations including new RLS policies)
Step 3: Seed users (alice + eric)
Step 4: Create sysadmin user (sysadmin@example.com / sysadmin123)  ← NEW
Step 5: Start dev servers
```

Gracefully handles the case where the sysadmin already exists.

---

### Files Changed/Created
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `supabase/migrations/20260223000002_sysadmin_rls_policies.sql` | **Created** — `is_sysadmin()` + 14 full-access policies |
| `apps/tenant-portal/src/App.tsx` | **Updated** — sysadmin role enforcement on login |
| `scripts/reset-and-start.ps1` | **Updated** — Step 4 creates sysadmin user |

> **Note**: The tenant portal's data operations use the `supabaseAdmin` client (service role key, bypasses RLS). The RLS policies provide defense-in-depth for any authenticated-role access (e.g., the work orders app at `:3004`). The primary sysadmin gate for `:3005` is the frontend session check.

kvps.memories:
Tenant onboarding system in `voicelog-scaffold` uses SolidJS frontend and Hono API.

Onboarding POST endpoint assigns trades based on industry: 'General Contractor' gets all app-scope templates, while specific trades get only their respective templates.

PATCH `/onboard/:id` endpoint reassigns trade templates when the organization's industry is updated by deleting existing company-scope templates and copying new ones.

Tenant onboarding form redirects to `http://localhost:3004/` for work order login upon success.

Sysadmin portal is located at `http://localhost:3005/` and is restricted to users with `role: 'sysadmin'` in their metadata.

Sysadmin user credentials: `sysadmin@example.com` / `sysadmin123`.

Database RLS policies include an `is_sysadmin()` helper function to grant full access to all tables for system administrators.

The `scripts/reset-and-start.ps1` PowerShell script includes a step to recreate the sysadmin user via the Supabase Auth API.

The `scripts/create-sysadmin.sh` bash script is available to manually create the sysadmin user.

Tenant portal uses two Supabase clients: `supabase` (anon key, RLS enforced) and `supabaseAdmin` (service role key, bypasses RLS).

{'problem': "Implement a tenant onboarding and profile management system that assigns trade templates based on industry selection, with specific logic for 'General Contractor' (all trades) vs specific trades, and ensures these assignments are updated when the industry is changed in the profile.", 'solution': "1. **Update API Routes**: Modify the `PATCH /onboard/:id` endpoint in `apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts` to detect industry changes. 
2. **Implement Trade Reassignment Logic**: In the PATCH handler, fetch the existing industry, compare it with the new payload, and if changed: 
   - Delete existing company-scope templates for that organization where `copied_from_template_id` is not null.
   - Call a helper function `copyTemplatesToOrg(orgId, industry)` which inserts copies of `app` scope templates into the `work_order_templates` table as `company` scope.
3. **Centralize Logic**: Update the frontend profile page (e.g., `TenantDetailPage.tsx`) to use the `PATCH` API endpoint for saving instead of direct Supabase client calls to ensure the server-side trade logic triggers.
4. **Industry Logic**: Implement a conditional check: if `industry === 'General Contractor'`, copy all active app templates; otherwise, filter by the specific `trade_name`."}

{'problem': "Secure a system administrator portal (localhost:3005) so only users with a 'sysadmin' role can access it, while maintaining a public onboarding route and ensuring data access via Row Level Security (RLS).", 'solution': "1. **Database Helper Function**: Create a PostgreSQL function `is_sysadmin()` that returns true if `auth.jwt() -> 'user_metadata' ->> 'role' = 'sysadmin'`.
2. **RLS Policies**: Create a migration adding `FOR ALL` policies to all application tables (e.g., `organizations`, `work_orders`, etc.) using `USING (is_sysadmin())` and `WITH CHECK (is_sysadmin())` to grant system-wide access to sysadmins.
3. **Frontend Enforcement**: In the main application component (e.g., `App.tsx`), implement a check after session establishment. If `session.user.user_metadata.role !== 'sysadmin'`, immediately call `supabase.auth.signOut()`, set an `accessDenied` state, and display a restricted access UI.
4. **Auth Integration**: Update the login handler to perform this same role validation immediately after a successful `signInWithPassword` call to prevent unauthorized entry."}

{'problem': 'Automate the creation of a system administrator user during local development environment resets.', 'solution': '1. **PowerShell Automation**: Add a step to the `reset-and-start.ps1` script that executes after `supabase db reset`.
2. **Admin API Call**: Use `Invoke-RestMethod` to POST to the Supabase Auth Admin API (`/auth/v1/admin/users`).
3. **Payload Configuration**: Include `email`, `password`, `email_confirm: $true`, and `user_metadata: @{ role = "sysadmin" }` in the JSON body.
4. **Authentication**: Pass the `service_role_key` in both the `apikey` and `Authorization: Bearer ...` headers to bypass standard registration restrictions.
5. **Error Handling**: Wrap the call in a try/catch block to gracefully handle cases where the user already exists.'}

kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Supabase local development and system administration: managing migrations, stale schema caches, securing the admin portal with RBAC, and automating user creation during environment resets.

# Solution

### 1. Automated Migration & Cache Refresh
Use a script (e.g., `apply-migration.ps1`) to sequence operations:
- **Environment Check**: Verify status with `npx supabase status`.
- **Apply Migrations**: Run `npx supabase migration up --local`.
- **Refresh PostgREST Cache**: Prevent 'Could not find column' errors: `npx supabase db execute --local -q "NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';"`.
- **Regenerate Types**: Run `npx supabase gen types typescript` immediately after migrations.

### 2. System Administrator Portal Security (RBAC)
To secure a portal (e.g., localhost:3005) for 'sysadmin' roles:
- **Database Helper**: Create a PostgreSQL function `is_sysadmin()` returning true if `auth.jwt() -> 'user_metadata' ->> 'role' = 'sysadmin'`.
- **RLS Policies**: Add `FOR ALL` policies to application tables using `USING (is_sysadmin())` and `WITH CHECK (is_sysadmin())`.
- **Frontend Enforcement**: In the main app component (e.g., `App.tsx`), if `session.user.user_metadata.role !== 'sysadmin'`, call `supabase.auth.signOut()` and redirect to a restricted UI.

### 3. Persistent Sysadmin User Creation (Automation)
To maintain a sysadmin user across `supabase db reset`, automate the Admin API call. 

**PowerShell Implementation (`reset-and-start.ps1`):**
```powershell
try {
    $body = @{
        email = "sysadmin@example.com"
        password = "sysadmin123"
        email_confirm = $true
        user_metadata = @{ role = "sysadmin"; full_name = "System Administrator" }
    } | ConvertTo-Json
    
    Invoke-RestMethod -Method Post -Uri "http://localhost:54321/auth/v1/admin/users" `
        -Headers @{ "apikey"="SERVICE_ROLE_KEY"; "Authorization"="Bearer SERVICE_ROLE_KEY" } `
        -ContentType "application/json" -Body $body
} catch {
    Write-Host "Admin user may already exist or service is unreachable."
}
```

**cURL Alternative:**
```bash
curl -X POST "http://localhost:54321/auth/v1/admin/users" \
  -H "apikey: SERVICE_ROLE_KEY" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer SERVICE_ROLE_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email": "sysadmin@example.com", "password": "sysadmin123", "email_confirm": true, "user_metadata": { "role": "sysadmin" }}'
```

### 4. Local Connectivity & Troubleshooting
- **Docker Networking**: Access DB via `host.docker.internal:54322` or API via `54321`.
- **Hard Reset**: If issues persist, run `supabase stop && supabase start`.

# Problem
Implementing a multi-tenant onboarding system and organization management interface using SolidJS and Supabase, including handling industry-specific templates, profile field synchronization, and automatic template re-assignment upon industry updates.

# Solution

### 1. Backend API & Database Architecture (Hono/Supabase)
- **Transaction Flow**: Use `service_role` to bypass RLS during onboarding. Create organization and admin user via `supabase.auth.admin.createUser` with `organization_id` in metadata.
- **Industry & Template Logic**: 
    - Create `GET /onboard/trades` to fetch master templates (`scope = 'app'`).
    - **Automatic Assignment (PATCH /onboard/:id)**: Modify the endpoint in `apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts` to detect industry changes. Fetch the existing industry and compare it with the new payload.
    - **Trade Reassignment**: If the industry changed, delete existing `company` scope templates (where `copied_from_template_id` is not null) and trigger `copyTemplatesToOrg`.
    - **copyTemplatesToOrg**: If `industry === 'General Contractor'`, copy all active app templates; otherwise, filter by the specific `trade_name`. Insert copies into `work_order_templates` with `scope = 'company'` and the new `organization_id`.
- **Schema Maintenance**: Always include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` after migrations to refresh PostgREST cache.

### 2. Frontend Implementation (SolidJS/Tenant Portal)
- **Organization Profile Management**: Update `TenantDetailPage.tsx` to include `industry` and `description` fields.
- **Data Fetching**: Use `createResource` to fetch trades from `${API}/onboard/trades`.
- **Save Logic**: Centralize logic by using the `PATCH` API endpoint (`${API}/onboard/${orgId}`) instead of direct Supabase client calls to ensure server-side trade logic triggers.
- **UI Components**: 
    - **Industry**: Use a `<select>` dropdown mapping through `trades()`.
    - **Description**: Use a `<textarea>` with `rows={3}`.
    - **State Binding**: Bind values using `(dirty() ? { ...org(), ...draft() } : org())` and handle changes via `update('field', value)`.

### 3. Code Snippets

**Backend Industry Update Logic:**
```typescript
let oldIndustry = (await adminClient.from('organizations').select('industry').eq('id', id).single()).data?.industry;
const newIndustry = payload.industry;
if (newIndustry !== undefined && newIndustry !== oldIndustry) {
  await adminClient.from('work_order_templates').delete().eq('organization_id', id).eq('scope', 'company').not('copied_from_template_id', 'is', null);
  newIndustry === 'General Contractor' ? await copyTemplatesToOrg(id) : await copyTemplatesToOrg(id, newIndustry);
}
```

**Frontend Save Implementation:**
```typescript
const saveProfile = async () => {
  const payload = dirty() ? draft() : {};
  await fetch(`${API}/onboard/${props.orgId}`, {
    method: 'PATCH',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify(payload),
  });
};
```

### 4. Troubleshooting & Data Integrity
- **Missing Trades**: Verify trades are promoted from `company` to `app` scope in `seed.sql`.
- **Migration Application**: Run `supabase db reset` to apply migrations.
- **Admin Client**: Use a separate `supabaseAdmin` client with `persistSession: false` to avoid session conflicts.

# Problem
 Access a local Supabase PostgreSQL database from within a Docker container (Agent Zero environment).
# Solution
 1. Identify the host machine's address from inside the container. Use `host.docker.internal` as it typically resolves to the Docker host on Windows/Mac.
2. Verify connectivity to the database port (default Supabase local port is `54322`) using a bash command: `timeout 3 bash -c "echo > /dev/tcp/host.docker.internal/54322"`.
3. Install the PostgreSQL client if not present: `apt-get install -y postgresql-client`.
4. Connect to the database using `psql` with the credentials provided by the Supabase start command (default: user `postgres`, password `postgres`, database `postgres`).
5. To list all tables across all schemas without pagination issues in a terminal, use: `PGPASSWORD=postgres PAGER=cat psql -h host.docker.internal -p 54322 -U postgres -d postgres -c "SELECT schemaname, tablename FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname NOT IN ('pg_catalog', 'information_schema') ORDER BY schemaname, tablename;"`.

Project 'voicelog-scaffold' Overview:

Architecture & Tech Stack:
- Frontend: SolidJS (Port 3005)
- API: Hono (Port 3000)
- Database/Auth: Supabase (Local development environment)

Project Locations:
- Local Path: C:\Users\3900X\Documents\GitHub\agentZero\voicelog-scaffold
- Agent Environment Path: /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold

Infrastructure Details:
- Local Supabase dashboard/services: http://127.0.0.1:54321
- PostgreSQL database: postgresql://postgres:postgres@127.0.0.1:54322/postgres (or host.docker.internal:54322 from within containers)
- API layer: PostgREST and Hono.

The tenant onboarding system in `voicelog-scaffold` is a full-stack implementation using a SolidJS frontend (tenant-portal) and a Hono API (api).
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Implementing a multi-tenant onboarding system and organization management interface using SolidJS and Supabase, including handling industry-specific templates, profile field synchronization, and automatic template re-assignment upon industry updates.

# Solution

### 1. Backend API & Database Architecture (Hono/Supabase)
- **Transaction Flow**: Use `service_role` to bypass RLS during onboarding. Create organization and admin user via `supabase.auth.admin.createUser` with `organization_id` in metadata.
- **Industry & Template Logic**: 
    - Create `GET /onboard/trades` to fetch master templates (`scope = 'app'`).
    - **Automatic Assignment (PATCH /onboard/:id)**: Modify the endpoint in `apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts` to detect industry changes. Fetch the existing industry and compare it with the new payload.
    - **Trade Reassignment**: If the industry changed, delete existing `company` scope templates (where `copied_from_template_id` is not null) and trigger `copyTemplatesToOrg`.
    - **copyTemplatesToOrg**: If `industry === 'General Contractor'`, copy all active app templates; otherwise, filter by the specific `trade_name`. Insert copies into `work_order_templates` with `scope = 'company'` and the new `organization_id`.
- **Schema Maintenance**: Always include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` after migrations to refresh PostgREST cache.

### 2. Frontend Implementation (SolidJS/Tenant Portal)
- **Organization Profile Management**: Update `TenantDetailPage.tsx` to include `industry` and `description` fields.
- **Data Fetching**: Use `createResource` to fetch trades from `${API}/onboard/trades`.
- **Save Logic**: Centralize logic by using the `PATCH` API endpoint (`${API}/onboard/${orgId}`) instead of direct Supabase client calls to ensure server-side trade logic triggers.
- **UI Components**: 
    - **Industry**: Use a `<select>` dropdown mapping through `trades()`.
    - **Description**: Use a `<textarea>` with `rows={3}`.
    - **State Binding**: Bind values using `(dirty() ? { ...org(), ...draft() } : org())` and handle changes via `update('field', value)`.

### 3. Code Snippets

**Backend Industry Update Logic:**
```typescript
let oldIndustry = (await adminClient.from('organizations').select('industry').eq('id', id).single()).data?.industry;
const newIndustry = payload.industry;
if (newIndustry !== undefined && newIndustry !== oldIndustry) {
  await adminClient.from('work_order_templates').delete().eq('organization_id', id).eq('scope', 'company').not('copied_from_template_id', 'is', null);
  newIndustry === 'General Contractor' ? await copyTemplatesToOrg(id) : await copyTemplatesToOrg(id, newIndustry);
}
```

**Frontend Save Implementation:**
```typescript
const saveProfile = async () => {
  const payload = dirty() ? draft() : {};
  await fetch(`${API}/onboard/${props.orgId}`, {
    method: 'PATCH',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify(payload),
  });
};
```

### 4. Troubleshooting & Data Integrity
- **Missing Trades**: Verify trades are promoted from `company` to `app` scope in `seed.sql`.
- **Migration Application**: Run `supabase db reset` to apply migrations.
- **Admin Client**: Use a separate `supabaseAdmin` client with `persistSession: false` to avoid session conflicts.

kvps.memories:
The onboarding system manages trade templates via the `work_order_templates` table, where app-scope templates (organization_id=NULL) serve as masters. During onboarding (POST `/onboard`) or profile updates (PATCH `/onboard/:id` via `TenantDetailPage.tsx`), trade assignment logic is triggered: selecting 'General Contractor' assigns all available app-scope templates to the organization, while specific trades (e.g., Painting, Carpentry) assign only their respective matching templates. When the organization's industry is updated via PATCH `/onboard/:id`, the system reassigns templates by deleting existing company-scope templates and copying new ones from the app-scope masters. The industry dropdown in the UI is populated by the `GET /onboard/trades` endpoint, which fetches distinct trade names from these app-scope templates.

Technical Implementation Details:
- Templates are initially defined in `supabase/seed.sql` as company-scope (ID prefix '20000000') and promoted to app-scope (ID prefix '30000000').
- The Painting template (ID: 20000000-...-001) includes Walls/Ceilings; Carpentry (ID: 20000000-...-002) includes Baseboards, Closets, and Doors.
- API routes are defined in `apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts`.

```typescript
  await fetch(`${API}/onboard/${props.orgId}`, {
    method: 'PATCH',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify(payload),
  });
};
```

### 4. Troubleshooting & Data Integrity
- **Missing Trades**: Verify trades are promoted from `company` to `app` scope in `seed.sql`.
- **Migration Application**: Run `supabase db reset` to apply migrations.
- **Admin Client**: Use a separate `supabaseAdmin` client with `persistSession: false` to avoid session conflicts.

kvps.memories:
The onboarding system manages trade templates via the `work_order_templates` table, where app-scope templates (organization_id=NULL) serve as masters. During onboarding (POST `/onboard`) or profile updates (PATCH `/onboard/:id` via `TenantDetailPage.tsx`), trade assignment logic is triggered: selecting 'General Contractor' assigns all available app-scope templates to the organization, while specific trades (e.g., Painting, Carpentry) assign only their respective matching templates. When the organization's industry is updated via PATCH `/onboard/:id`, the system reassigns templates by deleting existing company-scope templates and copying new ones from the app-scope masters. The industry dropdown in the UI is populated by the `GET /onboard/trades` endpoint, which fetches distinct trade names from these app-scope templates.

Technical Implementation Details:
- Templates are initially defined in `supabase/seed.sql` as company-scope (ID prefix '20000000') and promoted to app-scope (ID prefix '30000000').
- The Painting template (ID: 20000000-...-001) includes Walls/Ceilings; Carpentry (ID: 20000000-...-002) includes Baseboards, Closets, and Doors.
- API routes are defined in `apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts`.

PATCH `/onboard/:id` endpoint reassigns trade templates when the organization's industry is updated by deleting existing company-scope templates and copying new ones.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Implementing a multi-tenant onboarding system and organization management interface using SolidJS and Supabase, including handling industry-specific templates, profile field synchronization, and automatic template re-assignment upon industry updates.

# Solution

### 1. Backend API & Database Architecture (Hono/Supabase)
- **Transaction Flow**: Use `service_role` to bypass RLS during onboarding. Create organization and admin user via `supabase.auth.admin.createUser` with `organization_id` in metadata.
- **Industry & Template Logic**: 
    - Create `GET /onboard/trades` to fetch master templates (`scope = 'app'`).
    - **Automatic Assignment (PATCH /onboard/:id)**: Modify the endpoint in `apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts` to detect industry changes. Fetch the existing industry and compare it with the new payload.
    - **Trade Reassignment**: If the industry changed, delete existing `company` scope templates (where `copied_from_template_id` is not null) and trigger `copyTemplatesToOrg`.
    - **copyTemplatesToOrg**: If `industry === 'General Contractor'`, copy all active app templates; otherwise, filter by the specific `trade_name`. Insert copies into `work_order_templates` with `scope = 'company'` and the new `organization_id`.
- **Schema Maintenance**: Always include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` after migrations to refresh PostgREST cache.

### 2. Frontend Implementation (SolidJS/Tenant Portal)
- **Organization Profile Management**: Update `TenantDetailPage.tsx` to include `industry` and `description` fields.
- **Data Fetching**: Use `createResource` to fetch trades from `${API}/onboard/trades`.
- **Save Logic**: Centralize logic by using the `PATCH` API endpoint (`${API}/onboard/${orgId}`) instead of direct Supabase client calls to ensure server-side trade logic triggers.
- **UI Components**: 
    - **Industry**: Use a `<select>` dropdown mapping through `trades()`.
    - **Description**: Use a `<textarea>` with `rows={3}`.
    - **State Binding**: Bind values using `(dirty() ? { ...org(), ...draft() } : org())` and handle changes via `update('field', value)`.

### 3. Code Snippets

**Backend Industry Update Logic:**
```typescript
let oldIndustry = (await adminClient.from('organizations').select('industry').eq('id', id).single()).data?.industry;
const newIndustry = payload.industry;
if (newIndustry !== undefined && newIndustry !== oldIndustry) {
  await adminClient.from('work_order_templates').delete().eq('organization_id', id).eq('scope', 'company').not('copied_from_template_id', 'is', null);
  newIndustry === 'General Contractor' ? await copyTemplatesToOrg(id) : await copyTemplatesToOrg(id, newIndustry);
}
```

**Frontend Save Implementation:**
```typescript
const saveProfile = async () => {
  const payload = dirty() ? draft() : {};
  await fetch(`${API}/onboard/${props.orgId}`, {
    method: 'PATCH',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify(payload),
  });
};
```

### 4. Troubleshooting & Data Integrity
- **Missing Trades**: Verify trades are promoted from `company` to `app` scope in `seed.sql`.
- **Migration Application**: Run `supabase db reset` to apply migrations.
- **Admin Client**: Use a separate `supabaseAdmin` client with `persistSession: false` to avoid session conflicts.

kvps.memories:
The work-orders app (port 3004) dashboard was showing duplicate trades because the GET /templates API endpoint returned all active templates across all scopes (app, company, project).

The GET /templates API endpoint in apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts was updated to support an optional 'scope' query parameter for filtering.

The ApiClient in apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts was updated to allow passing a 'scope' parameter to the getTemplates method.

A new migration 20260223000002_sysadmin_rls_policies.sql was created, adding an is_sysadmin() helper and granting full RLS access to users with the 'sysadmin' role across 14 tables.

The tenant-portal (port 3005) App.tsx was updated to enforce sysadmin-only access, redirecting non-sysadmin users to an 'Access Denied' screen.

The scripts/reset-and-start.ps1 script was updated to include a step (Step 4) that automatically creates a sysadmin user (sysadmin@example.com / sysadmin123) using the Supabase admin API.

logs.heading: 4 memories and 1 relevant solutions found

kvps.memories:
The work-orders API and client have been updated to support scoped template filtering. The GET /templates endpoint (apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts) and the ApiClient.getTemplates method (apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts) now support an optional 'scope' parameter. This fix prevents duplicate trades on the dashboard by filtering templates by app, company, or project scope instead of returning all active templates.

Context on Template Management:
- The onboarding system (apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts) manages trade templates via the `work_order_templates` table.
- App-scope templates (organization_id=NULL) serve as masters (ID prefix '30000000'), while company-scope templates (ID prefix '20000000') are created during onboarding or industry updates.
- Selecting 'General Contractor' assigns all app-scope templates; specific trades assign only matching ones.
- Industry updates trigger a reassignment by deleting existing company-scope templates and copying from app-scope masters.
- UI: The industry dropdown is populated via `GET /onboard/trades`.

PATCH `/onboard/:id` endpoint reassigns trade templates when the organization's industry is updated by deleting existing company-scope templates and copying new ones.

The project uses a monorepo structure containing a SolidJS frontend (tenant-portal) and a Hono API (api). The tenant onboarding system uses this full-stack architecture with the frontend at http://localhost:3005, the Hono-based API at port 3000, and a Supabase database. Upon successful registration/onboarding, the system redirects users to the local work orders login page at http://localhost:3004/.

Frontend routes include /tenants, /tenant/:id (TenantDetailPage.tsx), and a public /onboard form. Both the /onboard form and the Tenant Detail page's Organization Profile section include an 'industry' selection dropdown and a 'description' textarea.

The Hono API (apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts) provides the following endpoints:
- POST /onboard (Public onboarding): Assigns trade templates based on industry ('General Contractor' assigns all; specific trades assign matching templates).
- GET /onboard (List organizations)
- GET /onboard/trades (Retrieves distinct trade names from active app-scope templates for industry selection)
- GET/PATCH /onboard/:id (View/Edit profiles): The PATCH endpoint reassigns trade templates when the industry is changed, following the same logic as the POST endpoint.
- GET/POST /onboard/:id/admins (Manage admin users)

Database management uses Supabase migrations (up to 20260223000001_org_industry_description.sql) via 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' or 'npm run db:migrate' with the '--local' flag. Migrations include 'NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';' to refresh the PostgREST cache.

- Frontend: SolidJS (Port 3005)
- API: Hono (Port 3000)
- Database/Auth: Supabase (Local development environment)

- API layer: PostgREST and Hono.

thoughts: All three changes are verified and correct:
1. API: GET /templates now accepts optional ?scope= query parameter to filter by template scope
2. api.ts client: getTemplates() now accepts optional scope argument
3. Dashboard: Now fetches only company-scope templates with getTemplates('company')

The root cause was that the Dashboard was calling getTemplates() without any scope filter, which returned ALL active templates (app + company + project scopes). Since each trade exists as both an app-scope master template AND a company-scope copy, every trade appeared at least twice.
The fix ensures only company-scope templates (the ones actually assigned to the tenant) are displayed on the Dashboard.
kvps.headline: Duplicate templates fix complete

tool_args.text:
## Fixed: Duplicate Templates on Dashboard

**Root Cause:** The Dashboard was fetching ALL active templates without filtering by scope. Since each trade exists at multiple scope levels (app-scope master + company-scope copy + sometimes project-scope copy), every trade appeared 2–3 times.

For example, "Painting" existed as:
- `app` scope (master template)
- `company` scope (copy assigned to your org)
- `project` scope (copy assigned to a project)

**Changes Made (3 files):**

1. **`apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts`** — `GET /templates` now accepts an optional `?scope=` query parameter to filter by `app`, `company`, or `project`

2. **`apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts`** — `getTemplates()` now accepts an optional `scope` argument that gets passed as a query parameter

3. **`apps/work-orders/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx`** — Changed from `api.getTemplates()` → `api.getTemplates('company')` so only the tenant's company-scope templates are displayed

The Templates page (`Templates.tsx`) was already correctly using `getCompanyTemplates()`, so no change was needed there. After a rebuild, the Dashboard should show each trade exactly once.

logs.content:
## Fixed: Duplicate Templates on Dashboard

**Root Cause:** The Dashboard was fetching ALL active templates without filtering by scope. Since each trade exists at multiple scope levels (app-scope master + company-scope copy + sometimes project-scope copy), every trade appeared 2–3 times.

For example, "Painting" existed as:
- `app` scope (master template)
- `company` scope (copy assigned to your org)
- `project` scope (copy assigned to a project)

**Changes Made (3 files):**

1. **`apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts`** — `GET /templates` now accepts an optional `?scope=` query parameter to filter by `app`, `company`, or `project`

2. **`apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts`** — `getTemplates()` now accepts an optional `scope` argument that gets passed as a query parameter

3. **`apps/work-orders/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx`** — Changed from `api.getTemplates()` → `api.getTemplates('company')` so only the tenant's company-scope templates are displayed

The Templates page (`Templates.tsx`) was already correctly using `getCompanyTemplates()`, so no change was needed there. After a rebuild, the Dashboard should show each trade exactly once.

kvps.memories:
The work-orders app runs on port 3004.

The GET /templates API endpoint in `apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts` was updated to support an optional `scope` query parameter ('app', 'company', or 'project') for filtering active templates.

The `ApiClient` in `apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts` was updated to allow passing a `scope` argument to the `getTemplates` method.

The Dashboard component in `apps/work-orders/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx` was modified to fetch only 'company' scope templates to prevent duplicate trade entries caused by overlapping template scopes.

kvps.solutions: {'problem': "The work orders dashboard at http://localhost:3004/#/login was showing duplicate trade templates (e.g., 'Painting' appearing multiple times). This was caused by the API returning templates from all scopes (app, company, and project) for the same trade, and the dashboard rendering all of them.", 'solution': "1. Modify the API route (e.g., `apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts`) to support scope filtering. Update the `GET /templates` handler to read a `scope` query parameter and apply it to the database query: `if (scope) { query = query.eq('scope', scope); }`.\n2. Update the frontend API client (e.g., `apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts`) to support the new parameter. Change `getTemplates()` to accept an optional scope argument: `async getTemplates(scope?: 'app' | 'company' | 'project') { const query = scope ? '?scope=' + scope : ''; return this.request('/templates' + query); }`.\n3. Update the Dashboard component (e.g., `apps/work-orders/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx`) to specifically request company-scope templates: `const [templates] = createResource(() => api.getTemplates('company'));`."}

kvps.memories:
> [!NOTE]
> Since v0.9.7, custom prompts belong in `/a0/agents/<agent_name>/prompts/` rather than a shared `/prompts` folder. See the [Extensions guide](../developer/extensions.md#prompts) for details.

> [!NOTE]
> The Hacker profile is included in the main image. After launch, choose the **hacker** agent profile in Settings if you want the security-focused prompts and tooling. The "hacker" branch is deprecated.

![settings](../res/setup/settings/1-agentConfig.png)

### Chat Model Settings

- **Provider:** Select the chat model provider (e.g., Anthropic)
- **Model Name:** Choose the specific model (e.g., claude-sonnet-4-5)
- **Context Length:** Set the maximum token limit for context window
- **Context Window Space:** Configure how much of the context window is dedicated to chat history

![chat model settings](../res/setup/settings/2-chat-model.png)

**Model naming is provider-specific.**

Use `claude-sonnet-4-5` for Anthropic, but use `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5` for OpenRouter. If you see "Invalid model ID," verify the provider and naming format on the provider website, or search the web for "<name-of-ai-model> model naming".

> [!TIP]
> **Context window tuning:** Set the total context window size first (for example, 100k), then adjust the chat history portion as a fraction of that total. A large fraction on a very large context window can still be enormous.

> [!TIP]
> **API URL:** URL of the API endpoint for the chat model - only needed for some providers like Ollama, LM Studio, Azure, etc.

### Utility Model Configuration

- **Provider & Model:** Select a model for utility tasks like memory organization and summarization
- **Temperature:** Adjust the determinism of utility responses

> [!NOTE]
> Utility models need to be strong enough to extract and consolidate memory reliably. Very small models (e.g., 4B) often fail at this; 70B-class models or high-quality cloud "flash/mini" models work best.

### Embedding Model Settings [Optional]

- **Provider:** Choose the embedding model provider (e.g., OpenAI)
- **Model Name:** Select the specific embedding model (e.g., text-embedding-3-small)

> [!NOTE]
> Agent Zero uses a local embedding model by default (runs on CPU), but you can switch to OpenAI embeddings like `text-embedding-3-small` or `text-embedding-3-large` if preferred.

### Speech to Text Options

- **Model Size:** Choose the speech recognition model size
- **Language Code:** Set the primary language for voice recognition
- **Silence Settings:** Configure silence threshold, duration, and timeout parameters for voice input

### API Keys

Configure API keys for various service providers directly within the Web UI. Click `Save` to confirm your settings.

> [!NOTE]
> **OpenAI API vs Plus subscription:** A ChatGPT Plus subscription does not include API credits. You must provide a separate API key for OpenAI usage in Agent Zero.

> [!TIP]
> For OpenAI-compatible providers (e.g., custom gateways or Z.AI/GLM), add the API key under **External Services → Other OpenAI-compatible API keys**, then select **OpenAI Compatible** as the provider in model settings.

> [!CAUTION]
> **GitHub Copilot Provider:** When using the GitHub Copilot provider, after selecting the model and entering your first prompt, the OAuth login procedure will begin. You'll find the authentication code and link in the output logs. Complete the authentication process by following the provided link and entering the code, then you may continue using Agent Zero.

### Authentication

- **UI Login:** Set username for web interface access
- **UI Password:** Configure password for web interface security
- **Root Password:** Manage Docker container root password for SSH access

![settings](../res/setup/settings/3-auth.png)

### Development Settings

- **RFC Parameters (local instances only):** Configure URLs and ports for remote function calls between instances
- **RFC Password:** Configure password for remote function calls

kvps.memories:
Project 'voicelog-scaffold' Overview:

Architecture & Tech Stack:
- Frontend: SolidJS (Port 3005)
- API: Hono (Port 3000)
- Database/Auth: Supabase (Local development environment)

Project Locations:
- Local Path: C:\Users\3900X\Documents\GitHub\agentZero\voicelog-scaffold
- Agent Environment Path: /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold

Infrastructure Details:
- Local Supabase dashboard/services: http://127.0.0.1:54321
- PostgreSQL database: postgresql://postgres:postgres@127.0.0.1:54322/postgres (or host.docker.internal:54322 from within containers)
- API layer: PostgREST and Hono.

The tenant onboarding system in `voicelog-scaffold` is a full-stack implementation using a SolidJS frontend (tenant-portal) and a Hono API (api).

The tenant portal utilizes two distinct Supabase clients: a standard `supabase` client (using the anon key with RLS enforced) and a `supabaseAdmin` client. The `supabaseAdmin` client is configured with the service_role key and 'persistSession: false' to bypass RLS for administrative tasks.

The sysadmin portal (tenant-portal) is located at http://localhost:3005/. Access is strictly enforced in App.tsx to users with `role: 'sysadmin'` in their metadata; non-sysadmin users are redirected to an 'Access Denied' screen. A default sysadmin user exists (email: sysadmin@example.com, password: sysadmin123). To facilitate database resets, the PowerShell script `scripts/reset-and-start.ps1` includes a specific step (Step 4) that automatically recreates this sysadmin user using the Supabase Admin API (service_role). Alternatively, the Bash script `scripts/create-sysadmin.sh` can be used for manual creation.

The project uses a monorepo structure containing a SolidJS frontend (tenant-portal) and a Hono API (api). The tenant onboarding system uses this full-stack architecture with the frontend at http://localhost:3005, the Hono-based API at port 3000, and a Supabase database. Upon successful registration/onboarding, the system redirects users to the local work orders login page at http://localhost:3004/.

Frontend routes include /tenants, /tenant/:id (TenantDetailPage.tsx), and a public /onboard form. Both the /onboard form and the Tenant Detail page's Organization Profile section include an 'industry' selection dropdown and a 'description' textarea.

The Hono API (apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts) provides the following endpoints:
- POST /onboard (Public onboarding): Assigns trade templates based on industry ('General Contractor' assigns all; specific trades assign matching templates).
- GET /onboard (List organizations)
- GET /onboard/trades (Retrieves distinct trade names from active app-scope templates for industry selection)
- GET/PATCH /onboard/:id (View/Edit profiles): The PATCH endpoint reassigns trade templates when the industry is changed, following the same logic as the POST endpoint.
- GET/POST /onboard/:id/admins (Manage admin users)

Database management uses Supabase migrations (up to 20260223000001_org_industry_description.sql) via 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' or 'npm run db:migrate' with the '--local' flag. Migrations include 'NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';' to refresh the PostgREST cache.

kvps.memories:
Project 'voicelog-scaffold' Overview:

Architecture & Tech Stack:
- Frontend: SolidJS (Port 3005)
- API: Hono (Port 3000)
- Database/Auth: Supabase (Local development environment)

Project Locations:
- Local Path: C:\Users\3900X\Documents\GitHub\agentZero\voicelog-scaffold
- Agent Environment Path: /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold

Infrastructure Details:
- Local Supabase dashboard/services: http://127.0.0.1:54321
- PostgreSQL database: postgresql://postgres:postgres@127.0.0.1:54322/postgres (or host.docker.internal:54322 from within containers)
- API layer: PostgREST and Hono.

The tenant onboarding system in `voicelog-scaffold` is a full-stack implementation using a SolidJS frontend (tenant-portal) and a Hono API (api).

The 'supabase/migrations/early-work/' subfolder is not automatically processed by Supabase because it is not referenced in 'supabase/config.toml'.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Supabase local development and system administration: managing migrations, stale schema caches, securing the admin portal with RBAC (RLS), and automating user creation during environment resets.

# Solution

### 1. Automated Migration & Cache Refresh
Use a script (e.g., `apply-migration.ps1`) to sequence operations:
- **Environment Check**: Verify status with `npx supabase status`.
- **Apply Migrations**: Run `npx supabase migration up --local`.
- **Refresh PostgREST Cache**: Prevent 'Could not find column' errors: `npx supabase db execute --local -q "NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';"`.
- **Regenerate Types**: Run `npx supabase gen types typescript` immediately after migrations.

### 2. System Administrator Portal Security (RBAC & RLS)
To secure a portal (e.g., SolidJS tenant portal) for 'sysadmin' roles:

**Database Helper Function:**
```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.is_sysadmin() RETURNS boolean
LANGUAGE plpgsql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER AS $$
BEGIN
    RETURN coalesce((auth.jwt() -> 'user_metadata' ->> 'role') = 'sysadmin', false);
END; $$;
```

**RLS Policies:**
Apply to sensitive tables to grant full access to sysadmins:
```sql
CREATE POLICY "sysadmin_full_access_table_name"
ON public.table_name FOR ALL
USING (is_sysadmin())
WITH CHECK (is_sysadmin());
```

**Frontend Enforcement (SolidJS/TypeScript):**
In `App.tsx`, implement a session check. If the user lacks the role, sign them out and redirect:
```typescript
function isSysadmin(s: Session | null): boolean {
  return s?.user?.user_metadata?.role === 'sysadmin';
}

// Inside component logic
if (session && !isSysadmin(session)) {
  await supabase.auth.signOut();
  // Redirect to 'Access Denied' view
}
```

### 3. Persistent Sysadmin User Creation (Automation)
To maintain a sysadmin user across `supabase db reset`, automate the Admin API call in your local environment setup.

**PowerShell Implementation (`reset-and-start.ps1`):**
```powershell
$sysadminBody = @{
    email = "sysadmin@example.com"
    password = "sysadmin123"
    email_confirm = $true
    user_metadata = @{ role = "sysadmin"; full_name = "System Administrator" }
} | ConvertTo-Json

# Use service_role key to bypass registration restrictions
$serviceRoleKey = "YOUR_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY"
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "http://127.0.0.1:54321/auth/v1/admin/users" `
    -Method Post `
    -Headers @{ "apikey" = $serviceRoleKey; "Authorization" = "Bearer $serviceRoleKey" } `
    -Body $sysadminBody
```

### 4. Local Connectivity & Troubleshooting
- **Docker Networking**: Access DB via `host.docker.internal:54322` or API via `54321`.
- **Hard Reset**: If issues persist, run `supabase stop && supabase start`.

# Problem
Implementing a multi-tenant onboarding system and organization management interface using SolidJS and Supabase. A specific issue was identified where the work orders dashboard (http://localhost:3004/#/login) showed duplicate trade templates (e.g., 'Painting' appearing multiple times) because the API returned templates from all scopes (app, company, and project) simultaneously.

# Solution

### 1. Backend API & Database Architecture (Hono/Supabase)
- **Transaction Flow**: Use `service_role` to bypass RLS during onboarding. Create organization and admin user via `supabase.auth.admin.createUser` with `organization_id` in metadata.
- **Scoped Template Retrieval**: Modify the API route (e.g., `apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts`) to support scope filtering. Update the `GET /templates` handler to read a `scope` query parameter and apply it to the database query: `if (scope) { query = query.eq('scope', scope); }`.
- **Automatic Assignment (PATCH /onboard/:id)**: Modify the endpoint in `apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts` to detect industry changes. Fetch the existing industry and compare it with the new payload.
- **Trade Reassignment**: If the industry changed, delete existing `company` scope templates (where `copied_from_template_id` is not null) and trigger `copyTemplatesToOrg`.
- **copyTemplatesToOrg**: If `industry === 'General Contractor'`, copy all active app templates; otherwise, filter by the specific `trade_name`. Insert copies into `work_order_templates` with `scope = 'company'` and the new `organization_id`.
- **Schema Maintenance**: Always include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` after migrations to refresh PostgREST cache.

### 2. Frontend Implementation (SolidJS/Tenant Portal)
- **Organization Profile Management**: Update `TenantDetailPage.tsx` to include `industry` and `description` fields.
- **API Client Update**: Update the frontend API client (e.g., `apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts`) to support the new parameter. Change `getTemplates()` to accept an optional scope argument: `async getTemplates(scope?: 'app' | 'company' | 'project') { const query = scope ? '?scope=' + scope : ''; return this.request('/templates' + query); }`.
- **Dashboard Component**: Update the Dashboard (e.g., `apps/work-orders/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx`) to specifically request company-scope templates to avoid duplicates: `const [templates] = createResource(() => api.getTemplates('company'));`.
- **Save Logic**: Centralize logic by using the `PATCH` API endpoint (`${API}/onboard/${orgId}`) instead of direct Supabase client calls.

### 3. Code Snippets

**Backend Scoped Query Logic:**
```typescript
workOrdersRoute.get('/templates', async (c) => {
    const scope = c.req.query('scope');
    let query = supabase.from('work_order_templates').select('*').eq('is_active', true);
    if (scope) { query = query.eq('scope', scope); }
    const { data } = await query.order('trade_name');
    return c.json(data);
});
```

**Frontend API Client:**
```typescript
async getTemplates(scope?: 'app' | 'company' | 'project') {
    const query = scope ? `?scope=${scope}` : '';
    return this.request(`/templates${query}`);
}
```

### 4. Troubleshooting & Data Integrity
- **Duplicate Dashboard Entries**: Caused by API returning all scopes. Ensure the frontend explicitly passes `?scope=company`.
- **Migration Application**: Run `supabase db reset` to apply migrations.

kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Supabase local development and system administration: managing migrations, stale schema caches, securing the admin portal with RBAC (RLS), and automating user creation during environment resets.

# Solution

### 1. Automated Migration & Cache Refresh
Use a script (e.g., `apply-migration.ps1`) to sequence operations:
- **Environment Check**: Verify status with `npx supabase status`.
- **Apply Migrations**: Run `npx supabase migration up --local`.
- **Refresh PostgREST Cache**: Prevent 'Could not find column' errors: `npx supabase db execute --local -q "NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';"`.
- **Regenerate Types**: Run `npx supabase gen types typescript` immediately after migrations.

### 2. System Administrator Portal Security (RBAC & RLS)
To secure a portal (e.g., SolidJS tenant portal) for 'sysadmin' roles:

**Database Helper Function:**
```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.is_sysadmin() RETURNS boolean
LANGUAGE plpgsql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER AS $$
BEGIN
    RETURN coalesce((auth.jwt() -> 'user_metadata' ->> 'role') = 'sysadmin', false);
END; $$;
```

**RLS Policies:**
Apply to sensitive tables to grant full access to sysadmins:
```sql
CREATE POLICY "sysadmin_full_access_table_name"
ON public.table_name FOR ALL
USING (is_sysadmin())
WITH CHECK (is_sysadmin());
```

**Frontend Enforcement (SolidJS/TypeScript):**
In `App.tsx`, implement a session check. If the user lacks the role, sign them out and redirect:
```typescript
function isSysadmin(s: Session | null): boolean {
  return s?.user?.user_metadata?.role === 'sysadmin';
}

// Inside component logic
if (session && !isSysadmin(session)) {
  await supabase.auth.signOut();
  // Redirect to 'Access Denied' view
}
```

### 3. Persistent Sysadmin User Creation (Automation)
To maintain a sysadmin user across `supabase db reset`, automate the Admin API call in your local environment setup.

**PowerShell Implementation (`reset-and-start.ps1`):**
```powershell
$sysadminBody = @{
    email = "sysadmin@example.com"
    password = "sysadmin123"
    email_confirm = $true
    user_metadata = @{ role = "sysadmin"; full_name = "System Administrator" }
} | ConvertTo-Json

# Use service_role key to bypass registration restrictions
$serviceRoleKey = "YOUR_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY"
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "http://127.0.0.1:54321/auth/v1/admin/users" `
    -Method Post `
    -Headers @{ "apikey" = $serviceRoleKey; "Authorization" = "Bearer $serviceRoleKey" } `
    -Body $sysadminBody
```

### 4. Local Connectivity & Troubleshooting
- **Docker Networking**: Access DB via `host.docker.internal:54322` or API via `54321`.
- **Hard Reset**: If issues persist, run `supabase stop && supabase start`.

kvps.memories:
The project 'voicelog-scaffold' is a pnpm monorepo using Turbo and SolidJS for the frontend and Hono for the API.

The tenant onboarding form was modified to include industry selection (from trades template table) and a description field.

Selecting 'general contractor' as an industry assigns all available trades to a tenant.

The system redirects to a local work orders login page at http://localhost:3004 after onboarding.

Trade templates are defined in 'supabase/seed.sql' and promoted to app-scope via migrations.

A sysadmin user exists with credentials 'sysadmin@example.com' / 'sysadmin123'.

The 'is_sysadmin()' helper and RLS policies allow sysadmins full access across 14 tables.

The 'GET /templates' API and frontend client were updated to support an optional 'scope' parameter ('app', 'company', 'project') to prevent duplicate displays.

The work-orders app (port 3004) now has an authentication gate requiring login via 'LoginPage.tsx'.

The work-orders 'Layout.tsx' was updated to display the user's email and a 'Sign Out' button in the header.

logs.heading: Solution memorization completed: 2 solutions processed, 2 intelligently consolidated

kvps.solutions:
{'problem': 'The work orders dashboard was displaying duplicate trade templates because it fetched all active templates (app, company, and project scopes) without filtering.', 'solution': "1. Modify the API handler in `apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts` for `GET /templates` to accept an optional `scope` query parameter and apply it to the Supabase query: `if (scope) { query = query.eq('scope', scope); }`.
2. Update the frontend API client in `apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts` to include a `scope` argument in the `getTemplates` method: `async getTemplates(scope?: 'app' | 'company' | 'project') { const query = scope ? `?scope=${scope}` : ''; return this.request<WorkOrderTemplate[]>(`/templates${query}`); }`.
3. Update the `Dashboard.tsx` component to use the filtered fetch: `const [templates] = createResource<WorkOrderTemplate[]>(() => api.getTemplates('company'));`."}

{'problem': 'The work orders application (localhost:3004) lacked authentication gating and a way for users to log in or out.', 'solution': "1. Create `apps/work-orders/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx` using SolidJS and `@supabase/supabase-js` to handle `signInWithPassword` and provide a login UI.
2. Update `apps/work-orders/src/App.tsx` to manage session state using `createSignal` and `onMount`. Wrap the application routes in a `<Show>` component that displays `LoginPage` if no session exists and the main `Router` if authenticated.
3. Implement an `onAuthStateChange` listener in `App.tsx` to reactively update the session state.
4. Modify `apps/work-orders/src/components/Layout.tsx` to accept `userEmail` and `onLogout` props. Add a 'Sign Out' button to the header that triggers `supabase.auth.signOut()` and a display for the current user's email.
5. Ensure dependencies are installed in the monorepo using `pnpm install` from the root before building with `npx vite build`."}

logs.content: Bill Hennessy Place is actually the name of a project alias Hennessy. originally the docs for Hennessy were used to seed the db and develop the app. The only tenant in the db should be Team Projects. There needs to be a way to manage employees and sub-contractors related to a general contractor. Employees will need to include time duration for a task reported on their timesheet, sub-contractors will not they only need to report work completed. Some sub-contractors will need to report their hours for their timesheets to be valid.

kvps.memories:
Project 'voicelog-scaffold' Overview:

Architecture & Tech Stack:
- Structure: pnpm monorepo managed with Turbo.
- Frontend: SolidJS (Port 3005). Includes 'tenant-portal' with routes for /tenants, /tenant/:id, and /onboard.
- API: Hono (Port 3000). Located in apps/api, handling onboarding, organization management, and trade template assignment.
- Database/Auth: Supabase (Local development environment at http://127.0.0.1:54321).
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL (Port 54322), PostgREST, and Hono API layer.

Key Systems:
- Tenant Onboarding: Full-stack implementation. The /onboard form and Tenant Detail page include industry selection (sourced from trades template table) and description fields. Redirects to work orders login (Port 3004) after success.
- API Endpoints: POST/GET /onboard, GET /onboard/trades, GET/PATCH /onboard/:id, and admin management.
- Database Management: Uses Supabase migrations (e.g., 20260223000001_org_industry_description.sql) via 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' or 'npm run db:migrate --local'.

Project Locations:
- Local Path: C:\Users\3900X\Documents\GitHub\agentZero\voicelog-scaffold
- Agent Environment Path: /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold
