bash>cat /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/supabase/migrations/20260217000002_core_tables....

-- =============================================================
-- Migration: Core Tables
-- Description: Organizations, rooms, template libraries, and metric definitions
-- =============================================================

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: organizations
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.organizations (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    name text NOT NULL,
    slug text NOT NULL,
    settings jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.organizations
    ADD CONSTRAINT organizations_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: metric_definitions
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.metric_definitions (
    metric_key text NOT NULL,
    metric_label text NOT NULL,
    category text NOT NULL,
    trade_key text,
    unit_of_measure text,
    default_formula text,
    description text,
    sort_order integer DEFAULT 0
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.metric_definitions
    ADD CONSTRAINT metric_definitions_pkey PRIMARY KEY (metric_key);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: rooms
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.rooms (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    organization_id uuid NOT NULL,
    name text NOT NULL,
    display_order integer DEFAULT 0,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.rooms
    ADD CONSTRAINT rooms_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: template_libraries
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.template_libraries (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    name text NOT NULL,
    description text,
    project_type text NOT NULL,
    scope text DEFAULT 'app'::text NOT NULL,
    organization_id uuid,
    icon text,
    color text,
    metadata jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
    is_active boolean DEFAULT true NOT NULL,
    is_default boolean DEFAULT false NOT NULL,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
    CONSTRAINT template_libraries_scope_check CHECK ((scope = ANY (ARRAY['app'::text, 'company'::text]))),
    CONSTRAINT valid_app_library CHECK ((((scope = 'app'::text) AND (organization_id IS NULL)) OR (scope <> 'app'::text))),
    CONSTRAINT valid_company_library CHECK ((((scope = 'company'::text) AND (organization_id IS NOT NULL)) OR (scope <> 'company'::text)))
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.template_libraries
    ADD CONSTRAINT template_libraries_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);
========== WORK ORDER TABLES ==========
-- =============================================================
-- Migration: Work Order Tables
-- Description: Projects, work orders, tasks, notes, and templates
-- =============================================================

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: wo_projects
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.wo_projects (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    organization_id uuid NOT NULL,
    name text NOT NULL,
    description text,
    status text DEFAULT 'active'::text NOT NULL,
    metadata jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
    project_type text,
    library_id uuid,
    CONSTRAINT wo_projects_status_check CHECK ((status = ANY (ARRAY['active'::text, 'completed'::text, 'archived'::text])))
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.wo_projects
    ADD CONSTRAINT wo_projects_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: work_orders
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.work_orders (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    organization_id uuid NOT NULL,
    unit_id uuid NOT NULL,
    template_id uuid NOT NULL,
    trade_name text NOT NULL,
    status text DEFAULT 'pending'::text,
    assigned_contractor_id uuid,
    inspector_id uuid,
    start_date timestamp with time zone,
    completion_date timestamp with time zone,
    inspector_signoff_date timestamp with time zone,
    metadata jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.work_orders
    ADD CONSTRAINT work_orders_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: work_order_tasks
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.work_order_tasks (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    work_order_id uuid NOT NULL,
    room_id uuid NOT NULL,
    row_order integer DEFAULT 0,
    task_data jsonb NOT NULL,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.work_order_tasks
    ADD CONSTRAINT work_order_tasks_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: work_order_notes
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.work_order_notes (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    work_order_id uuid NOT NULL,
    author_id uuid NOT NULL,
    content text NOT NULL,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.work_order_notes
    ADD CONSTRAINT work_order_notes_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: work_order_templates
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.work_order_templates (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    organization_id uuid,
    trade_name text NOT NULL,
    version integer DEFAULT 1,
    is_active boolean DEFAULT true,
    definition jsonb NOT NULL,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    scope text DEFAULT 'company'::text NOT NULL,
    project_id uuid,
    parent_template_id uuid,
    copied_from_template_id uuid,
    created_by uuid,
    updated_by uuid,
    library_id uuid,
    CONSTRAINT valid_app_scope CHECK ((((scope = 'app'::text) AND (organization_id IS NULL) AND (project_id IS NULL)) OR (scope <> 'app'::text))),
    CONSTRAINT valid_company_scope CHECK ((((scope = 'company'::text) AND (organization_id IS NOT NULL) AND (project_id IS NULL)) OR (scope <> 'company'::text))),
    CONSTRAINT valid_project_scope CHECK ((((scope = 'project'::text) AND (organization_id IS NOT NULL) AND (project_id IS NOT NULL)) OR (scope <> 'project'::text))),
    CONSTRAINT work_order_templates_scope_check CHECK ((scope = ANY (ARRAY['app'::text, 'company'::text, 'project'::text])))
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.work_order_templates
    ADD CONSTRAINT work_order_templates_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

        "4. How do workers authenticate? Do they get Supabase accounts or are they just records?",

    "headline": "Clarifying workforce management requirements",
    "tool_name": "response",
    "tool_args": {
        "text": "Great context on the project history. I can see the current schema has `assigned_contractor_id` and `inspector_id` on work orders but no workforce tables yet. Before I build the employee/sub-contractor management system, I need to clarify a few things:

### Data Model Questions

1. **Worker Authentication**: Should employees and sub-contractors get their own Supabase login accounts (so they can log into the work-orders app themselves), or are they just records managed by the general contractor?

2. **Sub-contractor "requires hours" flag**: You mentioned *some* sub-contractors need to report hours. Should this be:
   - A per-contractor setting (e.g., "Bob's Plumbing must track hours")
   - A per-work-order setting (e.g., "this specific job requires hours")
   - Or both?

3. **Timesheet Granularity**: For employees reporting time, what level of detail?
   - Per work order ("I spent 4 hours on WO-123")
   - Per task within a work order ("2 hours on prep, 2 hours on painting")
   - Daily timesheet with multiple entries?

4. **"Work Completed" for Sub-contractors**: Does this mean:
   - Simply marking work order tasks as done (checkboxes)
   - Submitting a completion report with notes/photos
   - Something else?

### UI Questions

5. **Management Location**: Should the employee/sub-contractor management UI live in:
   - The **work-orders app** (localhost:3004) — managed by the tenant/GC
   - The **tenant-portal** (localhost:3005) — managed by sysadmin
   - Or both?

6. **Worker Assignment**: How should workers get assigned to work orders? Manual selection from a dropdown, or something more sophisticated (by trade, availability, etc.)?

Once I understand these, I'll design the database schema and build out the full feature."
    }
}

kvps.memories:
The project 'Bill Hennessy Place' (alias 'Hennessy') was used as the initial development seed, but the primary tenant in the database should be 'Team Projects'.

The system requires a workforce management feature to distinguish between employees and sub-contractors for a general contractor.

Employees are required to report time duration for tasks on their timesheets.

Sub-contractors primarily report work completed without time tracking, though some specific sub-contractors must report hours for their timesheets to be valid.

The 'work_orders' table contains 'assigned_contractor_id' and 'inspector_id' fields, but a dedicated workers/workforce table is not yet implemented.

The work-orders app (localhost:3004) now includes a login/logout system with a LoginPage and a Layout header displaying the user's email and a sign-out button.

logs.heading: Solution memorization completed: 2 solutions processed, 2 intelligently consolidated

kvps.solutions:
{'problem': 'The work orders dashboard was displaying duplicate trade templates because it fetched all active templates across all scopes (app, company, and project) without filtering.', 'solution': "1. Modify the API endpoint `GET /templates` in `apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts` to accept an optional `scope` query parameter and apply it to the Supabase query: `if (scope) { query = query.eq('scope', scope); }`.
2. Update the frontend API client in `apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts` to support the scope parameter: `async getTemplates(scope?: 'app' | 'company' | 'project') { const query = scope ? `?scope=${scope}` : ''; return this.request<WorkOrderTemplate[]>(`/templates${query}`); }`.
3. Update the Dashboard component in `apps/work-orders/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx` to specifically request company-scope templates: `const [templates] = createResource<WorkOrderTemplate[]>(() => api.getTemplates('company'));`."}

{'problem': 'The work orders application (port 3004) lacked authentication, allowing direct access to the dashboard without a login/logout mechanism.', 'solution': "1. Create `apps/work-orders/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx` using SolidJS and Supabase Auth (`supabase.auth.signInWithPassword`) to handle user authentication.
2. Update `apps/work-orders/src/App.tsx` to implement an authentication gate: 
    - Use `onMount` to check for an existing session via `supabase.auth.getSession()`.
    - Use `supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange` to sync the session state.
    - Wrap the application routes in a `<Show>` component that displays the `LoginPage` if no session exists.
3. Modify `apps/work-orders/src/components/Layout.tsx` to accept `userEmail` and `onLogout` props.
4. Add a 'Sign Out' button to the Layout header that triggers `supabase.auth.signOut()` and clears the local session state."}

bash>echo '=== Template Hierarchy Design ===' && head -60 /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffol...

=== Template Hierarchy Design ===
# Template Hierarchy & Inheritance System

**Date**: 2026-02-03
**Status**: Design Document
**Priority**: CRITICAL - Affects Phase 3 Implementation

## Overview

The Work Orders app needs a **three-tier template hierarchy** with inheritance and scoping:

1. **App-Level Templates** (Global defaults)
2. **Company-Level Templates** (Tenant-specific, inherited from app)
3. **Project-Level Templates** (Project-specific, inherited from company or other projects)

## Template Hierarchy

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ APP-LEVEL TEMPLATES (System Defaults)              │
│ - Managed by: System Admins                        │
│ - Scope: Global (all tenants)                      │
│ - Examples: Painting, Plumbing, Electrical         │
└──────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                   │ Inherits/Copies
                   ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ COMPANY-LEVEL TEMPLATES (Tenant Defaults)          │
│ - Managed by: Tenant Admins                        │
│ - Scope: Organization (single tenant)              │
│ - Origin: Copied from app templates                │
│ - Customizable: Yes (tenant admin can edit)        │
└──────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                   │ Inherits/Copies
                   ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ PROJECT-LEVEL TEMPLATES (Project-Specific)         │
│ - Managed by: Tenant Admins                        │
│ - Scope: Project (single project/property)         │
│ - Origin: Copied from company OR other project     │
│ - Customizable: Yes (tenant admin can edit)        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## User Stories

### As a System Admin
- I can create and manage app-level templates
- These templates serve as the base for all tenants
- I cannot see or edit tenant-specific templates

### As a Tenant Admin
- I can view app-level templates (read-only)
- I can copy app-level templates to create company defaults
- I can edit my company's templates
- I can create project-specific templates from company templates
- I can copy templates between my projects
- I cannot see other tenants' templates

### As a Project User
- I can only use templates assigned to my project

=== Project Lifecycle Migration ===
-- =============================================================
-- Migration: Project Lifecycle & Template Inheritance
-- Description:
--   1. Expand wo_projects.status to include 'staged' and 'paused'
--   2. Add is_frozen column to work_order_templates
--   3. Allow NULL definition for lazy-copy project templates
--   4. Create freeze_project_templates() trigger function
--   5. Seed app-scope templates from existing company templates
-- =============================================================

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 1. Expand project status CHECK constraint
-- -----------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE public.wo_projects DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS wo_projects_status_check;
ALTER TABLE public.wo_projects ADD CONSTRAINT wo_projects_status_check
  CHECK (status IN ('staged', 'active', 'paused', 'completed', 'archived'));

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 2. Add is_frozen to templates
-- -----------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE public.work_order_templates
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS is_frozen BOOLEAN DEFAULT false;

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 3. Allow NULL definition for lazy-copy templates
--    (project templates reference parent until frozen)
-- -----------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE public.work_order_templates
  ALTER COLUMN definition DROP NOT NULL;

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 4. Trigger: freeze project templates on activation
--    When a project transitions to 'active', copy the
--    definition from the parent company template into
--    each unfrozen project template.
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.freeze_project_templates()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  -- Only fire when status changes TO 'active'
  IF NEW.status = 'active' AND (OLD.status IS DISTINCT FROM 'active') THEN
    UPDATE public.work_order_templates pt
    SET
      definition = ct.definition,
      is_frozen = true,
      updated_at = now()
    FROM public.work_order_templates ct
    WHERE pt.project_id = NEW.id
      AND pt.scope = 'project'
      AND pt.parent_template_id = ct.id
      AND pt.is_frozen = false
      AND pt.definition IS NULL;
  END IF;
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_freeze_project_templates ON public.wo_projects;
CREATE TRIGGER trg_freeze_project_templates
  AFTER UPDATE OF status ON public.wo_projects

kvps.memories:
The project 'Bill Hennessy Place' (alias 'Hennessy') was used to seed the database and develop the application.

The primary tenant in the database is 'Team Projects'.

The system requires a workforce management feature for general contractors to manage employees and sub-contractors.

Employees must report time duration for tasks on their timesheets.

Sub-contractors generally only report work completed, though some specific sub-contractors will be required to report hours for valid timesheets.

The codebase 'voicelog-scaffold' uses a SolidJS frontend and Hono API with a Supabase/PostgreSQL backend.

A three-tier template inheritance system (App-Level, Company-Level, Project-Level) is implemented, where project templates are 'frozen' (definitions copied from parents) when a project status changes to 'active'.

The work-orders application is hosted at localhost:3004 and the tenant-portal at localhost:3005.

A sysadmin user exists with credentials sysadmin@example.com / sysadmin123, supported by a 'is_sysadmin()' helper and RLS policies across 14 tables.

logs.heading: Solution memorization completed: 4 solutions processed, 4 intelligently consolidated

kvps.solutions:
{'problem': 'The work orders dashboard was displaying duplicate trade templates because it fetched all active templates across app, company, and project scopes without filtering.', 'solution': "1. Modify the API's `GET /templates` endpoint to accept an optional `scope` query parameter.
2. Update the frontend API client library to support passing the `scope` argument in requests.
3. Update the Dashboard component to specifically request templates with `scope=company` to ensure each trade appears only once for the tenant."}

{'problem': 'Implement a secure sysadmin portal with full data access and restricted entry.', 'solution': "1. Create a database migration to define an `is_sysadmin()` helper function and apply Row Level Security (RLS) policies across all relevant tables (e.g., 14 tables) to grant full access to users with the sysadmin role.
2. Update the frontend `App.tsx` to include a sysadmin-only gate that checks user roles, signs out unauthorized users, and displays an 'access denied' message.
3. Enhance the environment setup script (e.g., `reset-and-start.ps1`) to automatically create a default sysadmin user (e.g., `sysadmin@example.com`) during the database reset process."}

{'problem': 'Enhance tenant onboarding with industry-specific trade templates and post-onboarding redirection.', 'solution': '1. Update the `organizations` table via migration to include `industry` and `description` columns.
2. Modify the `PATCH /onboard/:id` API to detect industry selection, remove existing templates, and copy new app-scope templates to the company-scope based on the chosen industry.
3. Update the SolidJS onboarding form to include a dynamic industry dropdown and a description textarea.
4. Implement a post-onboarding redirect that sends the user to the local work orders login page (e.g., `localhost:3004`).'}

{'problem': 'Implement authentication for a secondary application (work-orders) within a monorepo using existing patterns.', 'solution': "1. Create a `LoginPage.tsx` component utilizing Supabase's `signInWithPassword` method.
2. Update `App.tsx` to wrap routes in a session check and set up an `onAuthStateChange` listener to manage authentication state.
3. Modify the global `Layout.tsx` to display the authenticated user's email and a 'Sign Out' button.
4. Resolve any monorepo dependency issues by running `pnpm install` to ensure the API client and shared packages are correctly linked."}

kvps.memories:
A three-tier template inheritance system (App-Level, Company-Level, Project-Level) is implemented, where project templates are 'frozen' (definitions copied from parents) when a project status changes to 'active'.

The application architecture (voicelog-scaffold) utilizes a standardized four-tier scope hierarchy (System, Company, Project, and Unit/DWO) governed by a 'copy-on-inherit' model for customizations.

1. Scope Hierarchy: System (Global Defaults), Company (Organization Overrides), Project (Project-Specific), and Unit (Instance/DWO). The 'app' scope has been fully renamed to 'system' across the database, API, and frontend components. System flexibility documentation is located at /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/docs/general/system_flexibility.md.

2. Work Order Hierarchy (5-tier): S-SOP -> SWO (System) -> COW (Company) -> PWO (Project) -> DWO (Unit). DWOs are implemented as instances with a 'not_required' toggle in task_data JSONB.

3. Type Order (TO) Hierarchy: Defines workspaces (replacing legacy 'rooms') at System, Company, and Project levels. TOs can be 'Atomic' (e.g., Kitchen) or 'Composite' (e.g., Studio unit templates) and use a hierarchical 'type_orders' system.

4. Database Evolution (Migration 20260304000001): Phase 1 schema evolution introduced eight new tables: trades, organization_trades, project_outcomes, trade_outcome_assignments, type_orders, type_order_compositions, type_order_default_work_orders, and bom_action_requests. This replaced legacy structures like 'template_libraries' and flat 'rooms'.

5. Technical Status: API layer (api.ts) currently lacks CRUD methods for new entities. The migration from 'app' to 'system' terminology is now complete across all layers. Database triggers (e.g., sync_trade_name) are active to sync denormalized trade names, supported by a 41-test validation suite.

The 'voicelog-scaffold' (also referred to as 'voice-scaffold') project is a multi-tenant construction management and renovation platform for trades, focusing on timesheet and work-order management. It is organized as a pnpm/Turborepo monorepo and follows a 'schema-first' philosophy with Zod and a three-tier template inheritance system (App-Level, Company-Level, Project-Level).

Template Logic:
- Inheritance: App -> Company -> Project.
- Freezing Mechanism: Project templates are 'frozen' (definitions copied from parents) when a project status changes to 'active' to ensure downstream isolation and prevent retroactive changes.

Architecture & Apps:
- API (:3000): 'The Brain'. Features 12 route modules and an A2A JSON-RPC protocol with a Placeholder Recovery system.
- Web Dashboard (:3001): Legacy interface.
- Material Admin (:3002): Manages supplier catalogs and inventory via JsonTreeEditor.
- Site Companion (:3003): Mobile PWA for field workers with voice-to-structured-data capture.
- Work Orders (:3004): Primary management for work order lifecycles and project documents.
- Tenant Portal (:3005): Custom-built web interface for organization-level configuration.
- doc-parser (:5000): FastAPI/Detectron2 for PDF analysis. Codebase located at /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/doc-parser/.

Environment & Tech Stack:
- Local Path: 'C:\Users\Casey\voicelog-scaffold' (Windows) bind-mounted to '/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/' (Container).
- Stack: SolidJS, TypeScript, Vite 5, Tailwind CSS, Hono, Supabase (PostgreSQL/Storage), NocoDB, Zod.
- LLM Integration: Uses `litellm` to communicate with providers (e.g., Anthropic/Claude).
- Shared Packages: @repo/config, @repo/db, @repo/ui, @repo/logger, @repo/schemas, @repo/utils.

Documentation is located in 'docs/general/platform_analysis.md' and 'docs/general/dbTables.md'.

The system implements a four-tier scope hierarchy (System, Company, Project, and Unit) using a 'copy-on-inherit' model for customizations to ensure downstream isolation.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Transition a multi-tenant construction management application to a five-tier hierarchy (System, Company, Project, Unit) using a 'copy-on-inherit' model, while enhancing tenant onboarding and resolving dashboard duplication issues.

# Solution
1. **Database Schema & Backend Evolution**:
    - Implement migrations for core tables: `trades`, `project_outcomes`, `type_orders`, `work_order_templates`, `units`, `bom_action_requests`, and `sops`.
    - **Tenant Onboarding**: Update `organizations` table with `industry` and `description`. Modify `PATCH /onboard/:id` to detect industry selection, purge existing templates, and copy industry-specific app-scope templates to the company-scope.
    - **API Enhancement**: Modified `GET /templates` with a `scope` query parameter to prevent duplicate listings.

2. **Navigation & UI Implementation**:
    - **Main Layout**: Consolidate configuration into a 'Settings' section with `SettingsLayout.tsx`.
    - **Onboarding Form**: Update SolidJS form with dynamic industry dropdowns and description textareas.
    - **Template Editor**: Replace free-text inputs with relational `<select>` dropdowns.

3. **Dashboard & Redirection**:
    - **Duplicate Fix**: Updated Dashboard and API client to use `scope=company`, ensuring trades appear only once by filtering out redundant app/project-scope templates.
    - **Post-Onboarding**: Implement redirect to local work orders login (e.g., `localhost:3004`) upon completion.

4. **Roadmap Status**:
    - Core schema and navigation implemented.
    - Scope-based filtering resolved dashboard duplication.
    - Industry-specific template inheritance and onboarding flow enhancement integrated.

# Problem
Build a tenant-facing web portal for a Supabase-backed construction management platform within a Turborepo monorepo. The portal must allow specific database tables and views to be visible or editable based on per-tenant configuration, supporting Supabase Auth (email/password and magic links) and iframe-compatible routing.

# Solution
1. **Database Schema & RLS**: Create a `portal_table_config` table to manage per-organization access:
```sql
CREATE TABLE portal_table_config (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    organization_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    table_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    display_name TEXT,
    access_level TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'hidden' CHECK (access_level IN ('hidden', 'readonly', 'editable')),
    column_config JSONB, -- Stores visibility/editability per column
    visible_columns TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
    editable_columns TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
    display_order INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    UNIQUE(organization_id, table_name)
);
ALTER TABLE portal_table_config ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
```
2. **App Scaffolding**: Create a SolidJS application in `apps/tenant-portal/` using Vite and Tailwind CSS. Configure `vite.config.ts` to run on port 3005. Update the monorepo shared config and `CORS_ORIGINS` to include this new app.
3. **Authentication**: Implement a `LoginPage` using `@supabase/supabase-js` supporting `signInWithPassword` and `signInWithOtp` (magic links). Use an `AuthGuard` component for route protection. Secure data access using Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) policies to ensure tenants only see data belonging to their `organization_id` across all application tables.
4. **Routing**: Use `<Router source={hashIntegration()}>` from `@solidjs/router` to enable hash-based routing (`#/dashboard`), ensuring compatibility when the portal is embedded in iframes.
5. **Dynamic Data Grid**: Build a `DataGrid` component using SolidJS `createResource` that:
    - Fetches data: `supabase.from(tableName).select('*', { count: 'exact' })`.
    - Dynamically discovers columns and filters them based on `column_config` or `visible_columns` from the config.
    - Implements inline editing for `editable` tables via `supabase.update()` on cell blur/Enter.
    - Supports server-side pagination and sorting.
6. **Admin Configuration**: Create an `AdminConfigPage` for super-admins to manage the `portal_table_config` for different organizations. Use a Supabase client with the `service_role` key to bypass RLS for administrative setup.

Sub-contractors generally only report work completed, though some specific sub-contractors will be required to report hours for valid timesheets.

The application implements a multi-tier template inheritance system (App → Company → Project) documented in 'docs/workorders/Template_Hierarchy_Design.md'.

A 5-tier work order hierarchy exists: S-SOP → SWO (System) → COW (Company) → PWO (Project) → DWO (Unit/Daily), where DWOs use a 'not_required' toggle in task_data.

Database migration '20260222000002_project_lifecycle_template_inheritance.sql' handles template freezing and lazy-copying when a project becomes active.

kvps.solutions:
{'problem': 'The user needs to implement a workforce management system for a general contractor tenant, distinguishing between employees and sub-contractors with different timesheet reporting requirements.', 'solution': "1. Identify the two primary worker types: Employees (require time duration tracking) and Sub-contractors (primarily report work completion, though some may require hour tracking).
2. Define the data model requirements: a 'requires hours' flag for specific sub-contractors and a mechanism for employees to log durations against work orders or specific tasks.
3. Integrate with the existing work order system which already contains `assigned_contractor_id` and `inspector_id` fields in the `work_orders` table.
4. Plan the UI placement: determine if management should occur in the work-orders app (tenant-facing) or the tenant-portal (sysadmin-facing)."}

{'problem': 'The system requires a multi-tier template inheritance and hierarchy system (App -> Company -> Project) to manage work order templates across different scopes.', 'solution': "1. Implement a three-tier scope hierarchy: App-Level (Global defaults), Company-Level (Tenant-specific, inherited from app), and Project-Level (Project-specific, inherited from company).
2. Add inheritance columns to the `work_order_templates` table: `parent_template_id`, `copied_from_template_id`, `is_frozen`, and `scope` (app, company, project).
3. Create a database migration (e.g., `20260222000002_project_lifecycle_template_inheritance.sql`) to handle 'lazy-copying'.
4. Implement a PostgreSQL trigger function `freeze_project_templates()` that automatically copies the definition from the parent company template into the project template when a project status transitions to 'active'.
5. Ensure the frontend filters templates by scope to prevent duplicate displays (e.g., only showing company-scope templates on a tenant dashboard)."}

can you make a plan to resolve how wo are used with this

discuss updating

C:\Users\3900X\Documents\GitHub\agentZero\agent-zero\data\projects\bmad_and_agent-zero\skills\sop_library_manager\README.md

Supabase Storage (object/file storage) — minimal benefit for SOPs. It's basically S3. You'd trade git versioning and Mermaid rendering for a file bucket. Worse in almost every way for markdown documents.

Supabase Database (PostgreSQL) — this is the real question, and yes, there's a clear case for it if SOPs need to be app-integrated:

Capability	Git files	Supabase DB
Version history	✅ Git	❌ Manual
Human authoring	✅ Native	⚠️ Needs UI
Full-text search	⚠️ Grep/index	✅ tsvector
Work order linking	❌	✅ FK relations
AI agent querying	⚠️ File reads	✅ Structured query
Mobile/web delivery	❌	✅ REST/realtime
Field worker access	❌	✅
Mermaid rendering	✅ Native	⚠️ Client-side
Recommendation: Keep markdown as the source of truth in git (authoring, review, version history). Sync parsed/structured SOP content into Supabase when you need to power:

Work orders referencing SOPs by ID
AI agents querying step content
A field worker app serving SOPs on mobile
That hybrid approach (git → parse → DB sync) is standard for docs-as-code systems and avoids losing the authoring workflow you've already built.

What's the intended consumer of the SOPs — a web/mobile app, an AI agent, or both?

both
Good — that combination is the strongest case for the hybrid approach. Here's how it maps out:

Architecture: Git → Supabase

Git (markdown)          Supabase PostgreSQL
─────────────           ───────────────────
Source of truth    →    Serving layer
Human authoring    →    App queries + AI retrieval
Version history    →    pgvector embeddings
Review workflow    →    Real-time work order state
Schema shape that fits your SOP structure

-- Core metadata (mirrors index)
sops (id, sop_number, trade_code, task, revision, status, est_duration_min, est_duration_max)

-- Sections for full-text / AI context
sop_sections (id, sop_id, section_number, section_type, title, content_md, content_plain)
-- section_type: 'purpose'|'scope'|'safety'|'tools'|'procedure'|'troubleshooting'...

-- Structured steps for work order progress tracking
sop_steps (id, sop_id, phase_name, phase_number, step_number, step_name, tasks_summary, detail_md)

-- For the dependency graph you've already modelled in Mermaid
sop_dependencies (from_sop_id, to_sop_id, link_type, condition_note)
-- link_type: 'prerequisite'|'sub-procedure'|'optional'|'cross-reference'

-- For AI retrieval
sop_embeddings (id, sop_step_id, embedding vector(1536), chunk_text)
For the AI agent specifically
The step-level granularity you've already enforced (Executive Checklist + Detailed Steps) maps perfectly to RAG chunks. Each sop_step row becomes one retrievable unit. The agent can:

Semantic search: pgvector on sop_embeddings → "how do I reconnect supply lines?"
Structured query: "give me all steps for PLB-SOP-001, phase 2 in order"
Dependency walk: resolve sub-procedure chains before dispatching a work order
For the field app
Work orders reference sop_id + track step completion against sop_steps
sop_sections.content_md renders directly in-app (Mermaid, checkboxes, etc.)
Supabase RLS gates access by role (field worker vs. supervisor)
Sync pipeline (simple starting point)

on: git push to main
  → parse markdown files
  → upsert sops + sop_sections + sop_steps
  → re-embed changed steps
  → update sop_dependencies from index
A GitHub Action running a Node/Python parser is sufficient at this scale. No need for a complex ETL. The parser just needs to understand your heading structure — which is already rigidly enforced by the authoring guide.

Net: your investment in the SOP format pays double — it's good for human authors AND makes the parse/embed pipeline trivial because the structure is predictable.

kvps.memories:
The `sops` table in Supabase serves as the master index for the SOP Library Manager, storing trade codes, task names, and storage paths for the markdown files (SKILL.md, README.md, AGENT_GUIDE.md).

The SOP Library Manager is a Python-based system (located at /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/skills/sop_library_manager) integrated with Supabase for managing trade-specific documentation (PLB, ELC, HVC, CRP). Documentation includes SKILL.md (manifest), README.md (technical overview), and AGENT_GUIDE.md (agent usage workflows). It supports a CLI and Python API (via scripts/sop_manager.py) for uploading, syncing, searching, and listing SOPs. All SOPs must follow a mandatory 15-section structure defined in the SOP Authoring Guide (SOP-META-001) with a three-tier hierarchy: Executive Checklists, Detailed Checklists, and Step-by-Step instructions/Mandatory Videos. The system uses a naming convention {TRADE}-SOP-{NNN}, maintains a master registry (SOP_INDEX.md), and includes a 'check_and_request' method for automated gap identification and agent request generation.

SOP Library Manager documentation includes SKILL.md (manifest), README.md (technical overview), and AGENT_GUIDE.md (agent usage workflows)

Supabase Storage and Document Processing Architecture:

1. Storage Structure: Organized by tenant and project IDs. Primary buckets include 'document-images' and 'project-documents' (private bucket; returns raw storage paths).

2. Processing Pipeline (Page-Rendering Architecture):
   - Save original PDF to Supabase.
   - Render pages as images for analysis.
   - Use LLM-detected bounding boxes and CV2 analysis to crop individual elements (floorplans, site photos, specification tables, diagrams).
   - Generate 400px thumbnails for mobile display.
   - Persist extracted images and table markdown files to Supabase.
   - Temporary full-size page images are discarded after processing.

3. Storage & Fallback Mechanisms:
   - Primary: Uploads to Supabase Storage.
   - Local Fallback: Implemented local file-saving capabilities when Supabase is unavailable. Images saved as PNGs to `parsed_images/<job_id>/` and table markdown to `parsed_tables/<job_id>/`. URL fields are populated with local file paths to ensure consistent output structure.

4. Supporting Services:
   - Doc Parser (Port 5000): Python-based engine (IBM Docling, PyMuPDF, LLM vision) tracking jobs in 'doc_parser_jobs'.
   - NocoDB (Port 8082): Spreadsheet interface for bulk data management.

5. Extraction Status & Known Issues:
   - Successfully extracts and crops individual site photos, floorplans, and specification tables.
   - Issue: 'floorplan' extractions occasionally capture entire pages.
   - Issue: Multiple photos sometimes captured as single composite images due to cropping logic.

SOPs must follow a mandatory 15-section structure defined in the SOP Authoring Guide (SOP-META-001)
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Establish a robust, standardized system for authoring, indexing, and programmatically managing trade-specific Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) across different trades (Plumbing, Electrical, HVAC, etc.) with automated lifecycle management and multimedia integration.

# Solution
1. **Standardized Three-Tier Structure (SOP-META-001)**:
    - **Format**: Mandatory 15-section template (Purpose, Scope, Safety, etc.) with a Three-Tier Procedure: Tier 1 (Executive Checklists), Tier 2 (Detailed Checklists), and Tier 3 (Step-by-Step Instructions).
    - **Multimedia**: Mandatory 'Section 10: Recommended Videos' using 11 curation rules and standardized tables mapped to procedure phases.
    - **Naming**: Unique IDs like `{TRADE}-SOP-{NNN}`.

2. **Database Schema & Storage (Supabase)**:
    - Tables: `sops` (metadata/file_hash), `sop_executive_checklists`, `sop_detailed_steps`, and `sop_recommended_videos`.
    - **Connectivity**: Use `host.docker.internal` for `SUPABASE_URL` in containerized environments.

3. **Programmatic Management (SOPLibraryManager)**:
    - **Sync Logic**: Python script using regex and MD5 hashing to detect file changes and update the DB only when content differs.
    - **Search & Retrieval**: Multi-column keyword searches via Supabase `.or_()` filters and `show(sop_number)` for full object retrieval.
    - **Agent Integration**: `check_and_request(trade, task)` method; if an SOP is missing, it generates a JSON payload with a pre-formatted prompt for an AI agent to author the document per the 'SOP Authoring Guide'.

4. **Interface & Quality Control**:
    - **CLI/API**: Unified interface for `upload`, `sync`, `search`, `list`, and `show` operations.
    - **Migration**: Group tasks into 5 logical Executive phases; maintain a central `SOP_INDEX.md` for status tracking (max 2 levels of nesting).

# Problem
Migrating, documenting, and restoring a complex live Supabase/PostgreSQL database schema (150+ tables) for local development. The goal is to filter internal metadata (NocoDB/system schemas), respect foreign key dependencies, and replace raw SQL dumps with structured, manageable migrations and documentation.

# Solution
1. **Extraction & Filtering**:
   - Use `pg_dump -s --no-owner` for schema-only extraction and `--data-only --inserts` for seed data.
   - Use Python/Bash scripts to filter out system schemas (`auth`, `storage`) and third-party prefixes (e.g., `nc_` or `xc_` from NocoDB).

2. **Structured Migration Generation**:
   - Parse the SQL dump and organize output into numbered files in `supabase/migrations/` following dependency order: Extensions -> Types -> Tables -> Constraints -> Functions -> Views -> RLS.
   - Example: `00001_extensions.sql`, `00002_tables.sql`.

3. **Dependency-Aware Seeding**:
   - Generate a `seed.sql` file by extracting data in an order that satisfies foreign key constraints.
   - **Circular Reference Handling**: Inject `SET session_replication_role = 'replica';` at the start of the seed file and reset to `'origin'` at the end to bypass trigger and constraint checks during bulk insertion.

4. **Documentation & Verification**:
   - **Documentation**: Rewrite raw SQL into Markdown organized by domain (Core, AI, etc.). Include RLS status (🔒 badge), JSONB structure examples, and an ASCII 'Entity Relationship Overview'.
   - **Deployment**: Execute `supabase db reset` to apply clean migrations and verify row counts/schema integrity against the source.
   - Document PostgreSQL views with aggregation logic descriptions.

=== README.md ===
# SOP Library Manager

> **Skill for Agent Zero** — Manages a Supabase-backed library of Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for trade work.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [What It Does](#what-it-does)
2. [Architecture](#architecture)
3. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
4. [CLI Reference](#cli-reference)
5. [Python API Reference](#python-api-reference)
6. [Database Schema](#database-schema)
7. [File Structure](#file-structure)
8. [Environment Variables](#environment-variables)
9. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)

---

## What It Does

The SOP Library Manager provides a **complete lifecycle** for Standard Operating Procedures:

| Capability | Description |
|---|---|
| **Upload** | Push `.md` SOP files to Supabase Storage |
| **Parse** | Extract executive checklists, detailed steps, and video links from structured markdown |
| **Sync** | Keep the database in sync with storage (change detection via MD5 hash) |
| **Search** | Full-text keyword search across SOP number, trade, task, and description |
| **Show** | Retrieve complete SOP data including all cached sub-tables |
| **Check & Request** | Look up whether an SOP exists; if not, generate an agent-ready creation prompt |

### Who Is This For?

- **Agent Zero agents** that need to find, retrieve, or create SOPs during task execution
- **Developers** building trade-management applications on top of the SOP library
- **Administrators** maintaining the SOP library and ensuring quality standards

---

## Architecture

```

---

## Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `Connection refused` | Supabase not running | Run `supabase start` on host |
| `relation "sops" does not exist` | Migration not applied | Run `supabase db reset` or apply migration SQL |
| `Bucket not found` | First run | Script auto-creates bucket; check storage permissions |
| `Parse returns 0 items` | SOP format mismatch | Verify SOP follows Authoring Guide exactly |
| `Hash unchanged, skipping` | File identical to last sync | Content hasn't changed; force with `sync` command |
| `Connection refused` (Docker) | Wrong URL | Set `SUPABASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:54321` |

=== SKILL.md ===
# SOP Library Manager

## Metadata
- **Name**: sop_library_manager
- **Version**: 1.1.0
- **Description**: Manages the SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) library in Supabase Storage and Database. Handles uploading, parsing, syncing, searching, and agent-driven creation of SOPs.
- **Tags**: sop, library, supabase, storage, database, procedures, trade, plumbing, electrical, hvac
- **Author**: Agent Zero
- **Created**: 2026-02-28
- **Updated**: 2026-02-28

## Overview

This skill provides a complete lifecycle manager for Standard Operating Procedures stored as `.md` files in Supabase Storage (`apps/voicelog/sops/`). It parses structured SOP markdown into database tables for fast retrieval of executive checklists, detailed steps, and recommended videos.

### Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   SOP Library Flow                       │
│                                                         │
│  .md File ──► Upload to Storage ──► Parse Markdown      │
│                                         │               │
│                                         ▼               │
│                              ┌──────────────────┐       │
│                              │   sops (master)   │       │
│                              └────────┬─────────┘       │
│                    ┌──────────────────┼──────────────┐   │
│                    ▼                  ▼              ▼   │
│          sop_executive_    sop_detailed_   sop_recommended│
│          checklists        steps           _videos        │
│                                                         │
│  Search/Query ◄── Database Tables (fast retrieval)      │
│  Missing SOP? ──► Agent Request ──► Create + Upload     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Database Tables

| Table | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `sops` | Master index — SOP number, trade, task, revision, status, storage path, file hash |
| `sop_executive_checklists` | Cached executive checklist items per procedure phase |
| `sop_detailed_steps` | Cached detailed step content per procedure phase |
| `sop_recommended_videos` | Cached video links organized by procedure phase |
| `sop_sync_log` | Audit trail for all library operations |
| `v_sop_library` | Convenience view with content counts |

### Storage

- **Bucket**: `apps`
- **Path**: `voicelog/sops/{filename}.md`
- **Format**: Structured markdown following SOP Authoring Guide (SOP-META-001)

## Prerequisites

- Supabase instance running (local or remote)
- Migration `20260228000001_sop_library.sql` applied
- Python packages: `supabase` (auto-installed if missing)

## Procedures

### 1. Upload an SOP to the Library

Uploads a `.md` file to Supabase Storage, parses it, and syncs all structured data to the database.

```bash
cd /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py upload <path-to-sop.md>
```

**Example:**
```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py upload docs/sop/SOP_Plumbing_Sink_Replacement.md
```

**Output:** JSON with sop_id, sop_number, sync counts for checklists/steps/videos.

### 2. Sync Database from Storage

Re-parses SOP files from storage and updates the database. Detects changes via MD5 hash.

```bash
# Sync all SOPs
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py sync --all

# Sync a specific SOP
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py sync PLB-SOP-001
```

### 3. Search the Library

Search by keyword across SOP number, trade, task name, and description.

```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py search "plumbing"
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py search "outlet"
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py search "sink"
```

### 4. List All SOPs

```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py list
```

### 5. Show SOP Details with Cached Data

Retrieves the full SOP record plus all cached checklists, steps, and videos from the database.

```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py show PLB-SOP-001
```

### 6. Check if SOP Exists — Request Creation if Not

This is the key workflow for agent integration. Checks the library for a matching SOP. If none found, generates an agent request payload with the prompt to create one.

```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py check "Plumbing" "Water Heater Replacement"
```

**If found:** Returns matching SOP records.
**If not found:** Returns an `agent_request` payload:
```json
{
  "found": false,
  "message": "No SOP found for 'Plumbing — Water Heater Replacement'. Agent creation requested.",
  "agent_request": {
    "action": "create_sop",
    "sop_number": "PLB-SOP-002",
    "trade_code": "PLB",
    "trade_name": "Plumbing",
    "task_name": "Water Heater Replacement",
    "prompt": "Create a complete Standard Operating Procedure for Plumbing — Water Heater Replacement..."
  }
}
```

### 7. Agent-Driven SOP Creation Workflow

When `check` returns `found: false`, the agent should:

1. **Read the agent_request payload** from the check result
2. **Call a subordinate agent** (developer profile) with the prompt from the payload
3. **The subordinate creates the SOP** following the Authoring Guide
4. **Upload the new SOP** using the `upload` command
5. **Verify** using the `show` command

See **AGENT_GUIDE.md** for detailed step-by-step instructions with exact tool calls.

## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Solution |
|---|---|
| `Connection refused` | Ensure Supabase is running: `supabase status` |
| `relation "sops" does not exist` | Apply migration: `supabase db reset` or run the SQL manually |
| `Bucket not found` | Script auto-creates the bucket; check storage permissions |
| `Parse returns 0 items` | Verify SOP follows the Authoring Guide format exactly |
| `Hash unchanged` | File content identical to last sync; force with `sync` command |
| `Connection refused` (Docker) | Set `SUPABASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:54321` |

        "reset": "true"
    }
}

    }
}

### Step 4 — Verify

        "runtime": "terminal",

## Python API (Alternative to CLI)

For more complex operations, use the Python API directly:

```json
{
    "tool_name": "code_execution_tool",
    "tool_args": {
        "runtime": "python",
        "code": "import sys\nsys.path.insert(0, '/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/skills/sop_library_manager/scripts')\nfrom sop_manager import SOPLibraryManager\n\nmgr = SOPLibraryManager()\n\n# Search\nresults = mgr.search('plumbing')\nfor r in results:\n    print(r['sop_number'], r['task_name'])\n\n# Show full details\ndetails = mgr.show('PLB-SOP-001')\nprint(f\"Checklists: {len(details['executive_checklists'])}\")"
    }
}
```

### Available Methods

| Method | Args | Returns | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `mgr.search(query)` | `str` | `list[dict]` | Keyword search across all SOP fields |
| `mgr.list_all()` | — | `list[dict]` | All SOPs with summary metadata |
| `mgr.show(sop_number)` | `str` | `dict` | Full SOP + checklists + steps + videos |
| `mgr.check_and_request(trade, task)` | `str, str` | `dict` | Check existence; generate creation request if missing |
| `mgr.upload(file_path)` | `str` | `dict` | Upload .md to storage + sync to DB |
| `mgr.sync_file(storage_path)` | `str` | `dict` | Re-sync one file from storage |
| `mgr.sync_all()` | — | `list[dict]` | Re-sync all files from storage |

## SOP Document Format

All SOPs must follow the **SOP Authoring Guide** (`SOP-META-001`). Key requirements:

### Mandatory 15-Section Structure

| # | Section | Content |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Purpose | Why this SOP exists |
| 2 | Scope | What's covered and what's not |
| 3 | References & Codes | Applicable standards (NEC, IPC, UPC, etc.) |
| 4 | Personnel & Qualifications | Who can perform this work |
| 5 | Safety Requirements | PPE, lockout/tagout, hazards |
| 6 | Tools & Equipment | Required tools and materials |
| 7 | Pre-Work Assessment | Site evaluation checklist |
| 8 | Procedure Phase 1 | Removal/Demolition (Executive Checklist + Detailed Steps) |
| 9 | Procedure Phase 2 | Installation/Assembly (Executive Checklist + Detailed Steps) |
| 10 | Recommended Videos | YouTube tutorials mapped to procedure phases |
| 11 | Testing & Commissioning | Verification procedures |
| 12 | Cleanup & Restoration | Site cleanup requirements |
| 13 | Troubleshooting | Common issues and solutions |
| 14 | Quality Standards | Acceptance criteria |
| 15 | Revision History | Document change log |

### Three-Tier Procedure Hierarchy

Each procedure phase (Sections 8 & 9) uses three tiers:

1. **Executive Checklist** — 5 high-level phases with checkboxes for quick tracking
2. **Detailed Steps** — Numbered sub-steps under each phase heading
3. **Step-by-step instructions** — Granular procedural content within each step

### Naming Convention

```
{TRADE_CODE}-SOP-{NNN}
```

| Trade | Code | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Plumbing | PLB | PLB-SOP-001 |
| Electrical | ELC | ELC-SOP-001 |
| HVAC | HVA | HVA-SOP-001 |
| Carpentry | CRP | CRP-SOP-001 |
| Painting | PNT | PNT-SOP-001 |
| Drywall | DRY | DRY-SOP-001 |
| Roofing | ROF | ROF-SOP-001 |
| Flooring | FLR | FLR-SOP-001 |

## Troubleshooting

| Problem | Solution |
|---|---|
| `Connection refused` | Supabase not running. User must start it on host: `supabase start` |
| `relation "sops" does not exist` | Migration not applied. Run: `supabase db reset` |
| `Parse returns 0 items` | SOP doesn't follow the Authoring Guide format. Check section headings. |
| `Hash unchanged, skipping` | File hasn't changed since last sync. Edit the file or use `sync` to force. |
| Search returns nothing | Try broader keywords. Search checks: sop_number, trade_name, task_name, description. |

## Environment Notes

- **Inside Docker container**: Supabase URL = `http://host.docker.internal:54321`
- **On host machine**: Supabase URL = `http://127.0.0.1:54321`

    "headline": "Providing comprehensive plan for SOP system evolution",
    "tool_name": "response",
    "tool_args": {
        "text": "# Plan: Evolving the SOP Library Manager

## Context

You have **two sources** that need to be reconciled:

| Source | Location | What It Defines |
|---|---|---|
| **Current README.md** | `skills/sop_library_manager/README.md` | Existing implementation — upload/parse/sync with flat tables (`sop_executive_checklists`, `sop_detailed_steps`, `sop_recommended_videos`) |
| **sop.md** (Claude command) | `.claude/commands/sop.md` (Windows) | Richer architecture — `sop_sections`, `sop_steps`, `sop_dependencies`, `sop_embeddings` + hybrid git→DB sync pipeline |

> ⚠️ I can't access the Windows path directly. I'm working from the content you pasted in your message. If there's more in `sop.md` you'd like me to see, please paste it or copy it into the project.

---

## Part 1: What sop.md Adds That README.md Lacks

### 1A. Richer Schema Model

**Current (README.md):**
```
sops → sop_executive_checklists  (flat phase/step/task)
     → sop_detailed_steps        (flat section/step/content)
     → sop_recommended_videos    (flat title/url/phase)
     → sop_sync_log              (audit)
```

**Proposed (sop.md):**
```
sops → sop_sections     (purpose, scope, safety, tools, procedure, troubleshooting...)
     → sop_steps        (phase_name, phase_number, step_number, step_name, tasks_summary, detail_md)
     → sop_dependencies (prerequisite, sub-procedure, optional, cross-reference)
     → sop_embeddings   (pgvector for RAG/AI retrieval)
```

**Key differences:**
- `sop_sections` replaces the implicit section structure with explicit typed sections — enables full-text search per section type
- `sop_steps` unifies executive checklists + detailed steps into one table with richer metadata — each row becomes a RAG chunk
- `sop_dependencies` is entirely new — models the Mermaid dependency graphs already in the SOPs
- `sop_embeddings` is entirely new — enables semantic search via pgvector

### 1B. Hybrid Git→DB Architecture

The README currently describes: **file → Supabase Storage → parse → DB**

sop.md proposes: **git (source of truth) → parse → DB (serving layer)** with:
- Git for authoring, version history, review workflow
- DB for app queries, AI retrieval, real-time work order state
- GitHub Action pipeline: `git push → parse markdown → upsert → re-embed → update dependencies`

### 1C. Work Order Integration

This is the critical missing piece. sop.md explicitly calls out:
- Work orders referencing `sop_id` 
- Step completion tracking against `sop_steps`
- Field worker app serving SOPs on mobile
- AI agents querying step content for dispatch

---

## Part 2: How This Changes the Schema

### Current Schema (migration `20260228000001_sop_library.sql`)
```sql
sops (id, sop_number, trade_code, trade_name, task_name, revision, ...)
sop_executive_checklists (sop_id, section_type, step_number, step_name, tasks, is_checked)
sop_detailed_steps (sop_id, section_type, section_number, step_number, step_title, content)
sop_recommended_videos (sop_id, title, url, procedure_phase, sort_order)
sop_sync_log (sop_id, sop_number, action, details)
```

### Proposed Evolved Schema

```sql
-- 1. ENHANCED MASTER TABLE
sops (
  id, sop_number, trade_code, trade_name, task_name,
  revision, status, effective_date, description, scope,
  est_duration_min, est_duration_max,  -- NEW: from sop.md
  storage_path, file_hash, tags,
  organization_id,                      -- NEW: tenant scoping
  created_at, updated_at
)

-- 2. REPLACES executive_checklists + detailed_steps
sop_sections (
  id, sop_id, section_number,
  section_type,  -- 'purpose'|'scope'|'safety'|'tools'|'procedure'|'troubleshooting'|'videos'|...
  title,
  content_md,    -- original markdown
  content_plain, -- stripped text for full-text search
  created_at, updated_at
)

-- 3. UNIFIED STEP TABLE (replaces both checklist + detailed)
sop_steps (
  id, sop_id,
  phase_name,      -- 'removal', 'installation', etc.
  phase_number,
  step_number,
  step_name,
  tasks_summary,   -- executive checklist level
  detail_md,       -- detailed step content
  is_checked,      -- completion tracking (for WO integration)
  created_at, updated_at
)

-- 4. NEW: Dependency graph
sop_dependencies (
  id,
  from_sop_id,
  to_sop_id,
  link_type,       -- 'prerequisite'|'sub-procedure'|'optional'|'cross-reference'
  condition_note,
  created_at
)

-- 5. NEW: AI/RAG embeddings
sop_embeddings (
  id,
  sop_step_id,     -- FK → sop_steps.id
  chunk_text,
  embedding vector(1536),
  created_at
)

-- 6. KEEP: Videos (already good)
sop_recommended_videos (...same...)

-- 7. KEEP: Audit log
sop_sync_log (...same...)
```

### Migration Strategy

Since the current tables may have data:
1. Create new tables alongside old ones
2. Migrate data from `sop_executive_checklists` + `sop_detailed_steps` → `sop_sections` + `sop_steps`
3. Drop old tables
4. Update `sop_manager.py` parser to populate new structure

---

## Part 3: How This Connects to Work Orders

This is where it gets powerful. The current `work_orders` table already has:
- `template_id` → links to `work_order_templates`
- `trade_name` → matches SOP trade codes

**New linkage:**
```sql
-- Add to work_orders or work_order_tasks:
ALTER TABLE work_orders ADD COLUMN sop_id uuid REFERENCES sops(id);

-- Track step completion per work order:
CREATE TABLE work_order_sop_progress (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY,
  work_order_id uuid REFERENCES work_orders(id),
  sop_step_id uuid REFERENCES sop_steps(id),
  completed_by uuid,           -- worker who completed
  completed_at timestamptz,
  notes text,
  photo_urls text[],           -- evidence photos
  duration_minutes integer     -- ← ties into your timesheet requirement!
);
```

This directly connects to your **employee/sub-contractor timesheet** requirement:
- Employees report `duration_minutes` per SOP step
- Sub-contractors mark steps complete (no duration unless flagged)
- The SOP provides the structured checklist that validates work completion

---

## Part 4: Plan to Update README.md

### Sections to Add/Modify

| Section | Action | Content |
|---|---|---|
| **Architecture** | **Rewrite** | Replace storage-centric diagram with hybrid git→DB diagram |
| **Data Flow** | **Rewrite** | Add git→parse→DB→embed pipeline |
| **Database Schema** | **Rewrite** | New 7-table schema with section types, unified steps, dependencies, embeddings |
| **Work Order Integration** | **NEW section** | How SOPs link to WOs, step completion tracking, timesheet connection |
| **AI/RAG Integration** | **NEW section** | pgvector embeddings, semantic search, structured queries |
| **Dependency Graph** | **NEW section** | How `sop_dependencies` models prerequisite chains |
| **Sync Pipeline** | **Rewrite** | GitHub Action / git push trigger instead of manual upload |
| **Field Worker Access** | **NEW section** | Mobile delivery via Supabase REST + RLS |
| **CLI Reference** | **Update** | Add commands for dependency management, embedding generation |
| **Python API** | **Update** | Add methods for dependency queries, semantic search |

---

## Part 5: Recommended Execution Order

1. **📋 Copy `sop.md` into the project** — so I can read the full file and not miss anything
2. **📝 Draft the updated README.md** — incorporating all new sections
3. **🗄️ Write the schema evolution migration** — new tables + data migration
4. **🔧 Update `sop_manager.py`** — new parser targeting `sop_sections` + `sop_steps`
5. **🔗 Add WO↔SOP linkage migration** — `sop_id` on work_orders + progress tracking table
6. **🤖 Add embedding pipeline** — pgvector setup + embedding generation on sync
7. **👷 Build workforce tables** — employees/sub-contractors with timesheet reporting tied to SOP steps

Steps 6-7 connect directly to your earlier workforce management request — the SOP step structure becomes the backbone for timesheet validation.

---

**Next step**: Would you like me to start with the README update, or would you prefer to first copy `sop.md` into the project so I can review the complete file? Also — should I proceed with the schema migration, or do you want to review/adjust the proposed schema first?"
    }
}

logs.heading: Memorization completed: 7 memories processed, 7 intelligently consolidated
logs.content:
```json
[
  "The project uses a hybrid SOP architecture where Markdown files in Git are the source of truth, synced to a Supabase PostgreSQL serving layer for app queries and AI retrieval.",
  "The evolved SOP database schema includes: `sops` (master metadata), `sop_sections` (typed content like safety/tools), `sop_steps` (unified checklist and detailed instructions), `sop_dependencies` (prerequisite/sub-procedure links), and `sop_embeddings` (pgvector for RAG).",
  "SOP steps are designed to integrate with work orders and timesheets: employees report task duration per step, while sub-contractors primarily report completion unless flagged for hours.",
  "The system implements a multi-tier work order and template hierarchy: System (S-SOP/SWO) -> Company (COW) -> Project (PWO) -> Unit (DWO).",
  "A PostgreSQL trigger `freeze_project_templates()` handles template inheritance by copying definitions from parent company templates to project templates when a project status changes to 'active'.",
  "The 'Bill Hennessy Place' (alias 'Hennessy') project was used for initial development and seeding; the primary production tenant is 'Team Projects'.",
  "Workforce management requirements include distinguishing between employees (must report task duration) and sub-contractors (report completion only, with optional hour tracking)."
]
```

kvps.memories:
The project uses a hybrid SOP architecture where Markdown files in Git are the source of truth, synced to a Supabase PostgreSQL serving layer for app queries and AI retrieval.

The evolved SOP database schema includes: `sops` (master metadata), `sop_sections` (typed content like safety/tools), `sop_steps` (unified checklist and detailed instructions), `sop_dependencies` (prerequisite/sub-procedure links), and `sop_embeddings` (pgvector for RAG).

SOP steps are designed to integrate with work orders and timesheets: employees report task duration per step, while sub-contractors primarily report completion unless flagged for hours.

The system implements a multi-tier work order and template hierarchy: System (S-SOP/SWO) -> Company (COW) -> Project (PWO) -> Unit (DWO).

A PostgreSQL trigger `freeze_project_templates()` handles template inheritance by copying definitions from parent company templates to project templates when a project status changes to 'active'.

The 'Bill Hennessy Place' (alias 'Hennessy') project was used for initial development and seeding; the primary production tenant is 'Team Projects'.

Workforce management requirements include distinguishing between employees (must report task duration) and sub-contractors (report completion only, with optional hour tracking).

kvps.solutions:
{'problem': 'The user needs to implement a multi-tier template inheritance and scoping system (App-Level, Company-Level, Project-Level) for work orders to allow global defaults to be inherited and customized by tenants and specific projects.', 'solution': "1. **Database Schema Update**: Add `scope` (app, company, project), `is_frozen` (boolean), `parent_template_id` (uuid), and `library_id` (uuid) columns to the `work_order_templates` table.
2. **Lazy-Copy Mechanism**: Allow the `definition` column in `work_order_templates` to be NULL for project-level templates, indicating they should reference the parent template until 'frozen'.
3. **Automation Trigger**: Create a PostgreSQL function `freeze_project_templates()` that copies the `definition` from the parent company template into the project template when a project status changes to 'active'.
4. **Trigger Implementation**: Attach an `AFTER UPDATE OF status` trigger to the `wo_projects` table to execute the freeze function.
5. **Scoping Logic**: Implement check constraints to ensure `app` scope has no `organization_id`, while `company` and `project` scopes require valid organization and project IDs respectively."}

{'problem': 'Duplicate work order templates appear on the dashboard because the system fetches all active templates across all scopes (app, company, project) simultaneously.', 'solution': "1. **API Modification**: Update the `GET /templates` endpoint in the Hono API to accept an optional `scope` query parameter.
2. **Filtering Logic**: Implement server-side filtering to return only templates matching the requested scope (e.g., `where('scope', '==', scope)`).
3. **Client Update**: Modify the frontend API client library to support passing the `scope` parameter in requests.
4. **UI Integration**: Update the Dashboard component to specifically request `company` scope templates, ensuring each trade appears only once for the tenant user."}

{'problem': 'A sysadmin user needs full-access permissions across all tenant data in a Supabase environment with Row Level Security (RLS) enabled.', 'solution': "1. **Helper Function**: Create a database function `is_sysadmin()` that checks if the `auth.jwt()` contains a `service_role` or a specific metadata flag identifying the user as a system administrator.
2. **RLS Policy Overhaul**: Apply `CREATE POLICY` statements to all relevant tables (e.g., organizations, projects, templates) using `USING (is_sysadmin())` and `WITH CHECK (is_sysadmin())` to grant bypass permissions.
3. **Frontend Gating**: In the React/SolidJS `App.tsx`, implement a check on the authenticated session. If the user lacks the sysadmin role when accessing the admin portal, trigger an immediate sign-out and redirect to an 'Access Denied' page.
4. **Automation**: Add a step to the environment setup script (e.g., `reset-and-start.ps1`) to automatically insert a default sysadmin user into `auth.users` and the profiles table during database resets."}

{'problem': 'The system needs a hybrid SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) management architecture that keeps Markdown as the source of truth in Git while serving structured data via Supabase for AI agents and work order integration.', 'solution': '1. **Schema Design**: Establish a 5-table structure: `sops` (master metadata), `sop_sections` (typed content like safety/tools), `sop_steps` (unified checklist and detailed instructions), `sop_dependencies` (Mermaid-based prerequisite links), and `sop_embeddings` (pgvector for RAG).
2. **Sync Pipeline**: Implement a Python-based parser (`sop_manager.py`) that uses regex to extract structured data from Markdown files.
3. **Change Detection**: Use MD5 hashing of file content to detect changes and only update the database/embeddings when the Git source file is modified.
4. **Work Order Linkage**: Add an `sop_id` foreign key to the `work_orders` table and create a `work_order_sop_progress` table to track completion of individual SOP steps, enabling granular progress reporting and timesheet validation.'}

logs.content:
this is commands/sop.md. 
# /sop — Create or Locate an SOP

Generate or locate a Standard Operating Procedure following SOP-META-001.

**Arguments:** `$ARGUMENTS` — trade name (or 3-letter code) and task description.
Examples: `plumbing toilet replacement`, `ELC light fixture replacement`, `painting cabinet repainting`

---

## Step 1 — Parse Input

Extract from `$ARGUMENTS`:
- **Trade**: match to the trade code table (PLB, ELC, HVC, CRP, PNT, RFG, FLR, DRW, LND, GEN, MSN, WLD)
- **Task**: the remaining text is the task description

If the trade cannot be matched, ask the user to clarify before continuing.

---

## Step 2 — Check the Index

Read `voicelog-scaffold/docs/sop/SOP_INDEX.md`.

Search the **Full SOP Registry** for a row matching this trade + task (fuzzy match on task name is acceptable).

**If a matching row exists AND has a file link (✅ Active):**
- Report: `SOP already exists: {TRADE}/SOP_{TRADE}_{Task_Name}.md`
- Output the full relative path and stop. Do not recreate it.

**If a matching row exists as 📋 Planned (no file):**
- Use the existing SOP number from the index row.
- Proceed to Step 4 (skip Step 3).

**If no matching row exists:**
- Proceed to Step 3.

---

## Step 3 — Add Planned Entry to Index

Determine the next available SOP number for the trade by scanning the Full SOP Registry for the highest existing `{TRADE}-SOP-{NNN}` number and incrementing by 1.

Add a new row to the Full SOP Registry:
```
| {TRADE}-SOP-{NNN} | {Trade} | {Task} | — | 📋 Planned | — | — | None | None |
```

Update the **Quick Reference** count for the trade (+1).

Update the **Revision History** in `SOP_INDEX.md` with a new row noting the planned entry was added.

---

## Step 4 — Create REF Doc If Needed

Check whether `voicelog-scaffold/docs/sop/{TRADE}/REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md` exists.

**If it exists:** proceed to Step 5.

**If it does NOT exist:** create it now before authoring the SOP.

### REF Doc: Required Content

Create the file at: `voicelog-scaffold/docs/sop/{TRADE}/REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md`
(Create the `{TRADE}/` folder if it does not yet exist.)

Use the following structure exactly (per SOP-META-001 §8.3):

```
# Reference Document — {Trade} Trade: Codes & Standards

| Field | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Document ID** | REF-{TRADE}-001 |
| **Applies To** | All {TRADE}-SOP-### procedures |
| **Revision** | 1.0 |
| **Effective Date** | {today's date YYYY-MM-DD} |

---

## 1. Regulatory References
{Applicable federal/state regulations for this trade — e.g., NEC/OSHA subparts for electrical,
IPC/UPC for plumbing, NFPA for fire-related trades, EPA rules where applicable}

## 2. Codes & Standards
{Primary industry codes and standards — e.g., NEC editions, ASME, ASTM, manufacturer
installation requirements, jurisdiction permit requirements}

## 3. Documentation Requirements
{What must be on-site and reviewed — SDS sheets, permit cards, manufacturer spec sheets,
inspection sign-off requirements}

---

## 4. Personnel & Qualifications

{Applies to all {trade} SOPs unless an individual SOP specifies otherwise.}

| Role | Requirement |
|---|---|
| {Lead role} | {License, certification, or experience requirement} |
| {Helper/apprentice role} | {Supervision requirement} |

---

## 5. Standard PPE

{Required for all {trade} procedures unless an individual SOP notes exceptions or additions.}

- {PPE item — include the reason/condition it is required}
- {PPE item}
...

---

## 6. Common Hazard Awareness

{The following hazards apply across all {trade} procedures. Individual SOPs list additional task-specific hazards.}

| Hazard | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| {Hazard} | {Mitigation} |
...

---

## 7. Standard Cleanup, Documentation & Customer Handoff

### Cleanup
1. {Step}
...

### Documentation
1. {Step — record materials, photos, test results, conditions found}
...

### Customer Handoff
- {Item — demonstrate operation, advise on maintenance, obtain sign-off}
...

---

## Revision History

| Rev | Date | Author | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1.0 | {today's date} | — | Initial release |
```

### After creating the REF doc:

Register it in `SOP_INDEX.md` **Reference Document Registry**:
```
| REF-{TRADE}-001 | [REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md]({TRADE}/REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md) | {Trade} | All {TRADE}-SOP-### | 1.0 | ✅ Active |
```

Update `SOP_AUTHORING_GUIDE.md` §8.6 (Existing REF Docs table) with a new row:
```
| REF-{TRADE}-001 | [REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md]({TRADE}/REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md) | {Trade} | All {TRADE}-SOP-### |
```

Update the **Revision History** in `SOP_INDEX.md` noting the new REF doc was created.

---

## Step 5 — Generate the SOP

Create the file at: `voicelog-scaffold/docs/sop/{TRADE}/SOP_{TRADE}_{Task_Name}.md`

Where `{Task_Name}` is the task description in Title_Case with spaces replaced by underscores.

Follow **SOP-META-001 Rev 1.3** exactly. The REF doc now exists for this trade — use pointer links throughout (never write shared sections in full in the SOP).

### Header Table
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| SOP Number | `{TRADE}-SOP-{NNN}` |
| Trade | {Full trade name} |
| Task | {Task description} |
| Revision | 1.0 |
| Effective Date | Today's date (YYYY-MM-DD) |
| Approved By | `DRAFT` |
| Estimated Duration | Realistic estimate for the task |

### Mandatory Sections (in order)

1. **Purpose** — what this procedure covers and why it exists

2. **Scope** — what IS and IS NOT covered; cross-reference related SOPs using `(see {TRADE}-SOP-{NNN}: {Title})`

3. **References & Codes**
   ```
   See [REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md](REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md) — {Trade} Trade: Codes & Standards Reference.
   ```

4. **Personnel & Qualifications**
   ```
   See [REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md](REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md) — Section 4: Personnel & Qualifications.
   ```
   Add SOP-specific role notes below the pointer only if this task requires a role not covered by the REF doc.

5. **Safety Requirements**
   - §5.1 PPE:
     ```
     See [REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md](REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md) — Section 5: Standard PPE.
     ```
     Add `**SOP-specific note:**` below if this task requires additional PPE.
   - §5.2 Hazard Awareness:
     ```
     See [REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md](REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md) — Section 6: Common Hazard Awareness.

     **Additional hazards for this SOP:**
     | Hazard | Mitigation |
     |---|---|
     | {task-specific hazard} | {mitigation} |
     ```
   - §5.3 Pre-Work Safety Checklist — **always written in full; never a REF pointer**

6. **Tools & Equipment**
   - §6.1 Required Tools table (Tool | Purpose) — always written in full
   - §6.2 Required Materials table (Material | Specification) — always written in full

7. **Pre-Work Assessment**
   - §7.1 Site Assessment — numbered steps, always written in full
   - §7.2 Customer Communication — bulleted list, always written in full

### Procedure Phases (§8 onward)

Create one section per logical phase (e.g., Removal, Installation; or Prep, Apply, Finish).

Each phase MUST follow this exact internal structure:
```
## {N}. Procedure — {Phase Name}

### Executive Checklist

Complete each step in order. Check off the step when **all tasks within it are finished**.

| Step | Step Name | Tasks | ✓ |
|:---:|---|---|:---:|
| **1** | **{Step Name}** | {Brief task description} | ☐ |
...

> ✅ **All steps complete?** → Proceed to Section {next} — {next section name}.

---

### Detailed Steps

#### Step 1: {Step Name}
{numbered instructions}
...
```

Rules:
- Maximum 7 steps per Executive Checklist — split into named sub-phases if more are needed
- Step Names MUST match exactly between Executive Checklist and Detailed Steps headings
- Sub-groupings within a step use bold labels (e.g., `**Supply lines:**`)
- Use `> ⚠️ **Code Check:**` for code warnings
- Use `> **Best practice:**` for recommendations

### Recommended Videos

Place after all procedure phases, before Testing & Commissioning. One table per procedure phase:
```
## {N}. Recommended Videos

The following videos demonstrate the techniques described in the Detailed Steps above.

### {Phase Name} (Section {N})
| Step | Video | Duration | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Step 1: {Name} | 🔍 *Video to be sourced* | — | — |
...
```
Use `🔍 *Video to be sourced*` placeholders. Max 5 videos per phase.

### Remaining Sections (continue numbering from procedures)

- **Testing & Commissioning** — staged checkbox verification (Visual → Functional → Final)
- **Cleanup & Closeout**:
  ```
  See [REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md](REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md) — Section 7: Standard Cleanup, Documentation & Customer Handoff.

  **Additional steps for this SOP:**
  - {task-specific cleanup or documentation item, if any}
  ```
- **Troubleshooting** — table (Issue | Probable Cause | Resolution)
- **Quality Standards** — bulleted list of code compliance and workmanship standards
- **Revision History** — table with initial 1.0 entry

---

## Step 6 — Update the Index

In `SOP_INDEX.md` Full SOP Registry, update the row for this SOP:
- Set **Rev** to `1.0`
- Set **Status** to `✅ Active`
- Set **Est. Duration** to the value used in the SOP header
- Set **File** to `[SOP_{TRADE}_{Task_Name}.md]({TRADE}/SOP_{TRADE}_{Task_Name}.md)`
- Fill in **Prerequisites** and **Sub-procedures** based on the Scope section

Update the **Revision History** in `SOP_INDEX.md` noting the new SOP was activated.

---

## Step 7 — Report

Output a summary:
```
✅ SOP created: {TRADE}/SOP_{TRADE}_{Task_Name}.md
   SOP Number:  {TRADE}-SOP-{NNN}
   Trade:       {Trade}
   Task:        {Task}
   REF doc:     {Created: {TRADE}/REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md / Already existed}
   Index:       Updated — status set to ✅ Active
```

Originally the app would allow a default set of system wo to be the parent of company wo which would be the parent of project wo which would be the parent of a wo for a workspace or unit. There should already exist code for this hierarchy. The idea was to be light and have each level of wo to be editable and yet be able to reference the step in an sop. Periodically we could reconcile discrepancies between wo and sops. So, can we use the richer schema model  with the existing wo model or do you have a better recommendation.

        "- Trade folder organization (docs/sop/{TRADE}/SOP_{TRADE}_{Task}.md)",
        "- The full 15-section SOP structure with exact formatting rules",

        "",
