        "- WOs reference sop_steps for procedure guidance",
        "- WO tasks can diverge from SOP steps (editable at each level)",
        "- Reconciliation compares WO task_data against sop_steps",
        "",
        "Option B: Merge SOP steps INTO the WO template hierarchy",
        "- System WO templates ARE the SOPs (or derived from them)",
        "- Company/Project/Unit WOs inherit and can customize",
        "- No separate sop_steps table needed",
        "",
        "Option C: Hybrid - SOPs are the source of truth, WO templates reference them",
        "- sop_steps exist as the canonical procedure",
        "- work_order_templates.definition references sop_id + allows overrides",
        "- At each WO level, you can see the SOP step + any local modifications",
        "- Reconciliation = diff between WO customizations and current SOP version",
        "",
        "Option C seems best because it preserves the light/editable WO model while giving SOPs their proper role as the authoritative procedure source.",
        "",
        "Let me present this comprehensive plan."
    ],
    "headline": "Comprehensive plan: SOP + WO integration architecture",
    "tool_name": "response",
    "tool_args": {
        "text": "# Plan: SOP System Evolution + Work Order Integration

Now that I have the full `sop.md` command, here's the complete picture and my recommendation.

---

## What sop.md Adds to the README

The Claude command defines a **7-step authoring workflow** with concepts the current skill doesn't handle:

| Concept | In README? | In sop.md? | Impact |
|---|---|---|---|
| REF docs (trade-level shared references) | ❌ | ✅ Step 4 | SOPs use pointer links instead of duplicating PPE/codes/personnel |
| SOP_INDEX.md as master registry | Mentioned | ✅ Steps 2,3,6 | Planned → Active lifecycle, prerequisite/sub-procedure tracking |
| Trade folder organization | ❌ | ✅ Step 5 | `docs/sop/{TRADE}/SOP_{TRADE}_{Task}.md` |
| REF doc registration in index + authoring guide | ❌ | ✅ Step 4 | Cross-document consistency maintenance |
| Exact 15-section SOP structure with REF pointers | Partial | ✅ Step 5 | Sections 3,4,5,cleanup use `See REF_...` pointers |
| Executive Checklist max 7 steps rule | ❌ | ✅ Step 5 | Split into sub-phases if >7 |
| Video placeholder pattern | ❌ | ✅ Step 5 | `🔍 *Video to be sourced*` |
| Planned entry workflow | ❌ | ✅ Step 3 | Reserve SOP number before authoring |

---

## The Core Architecture Question

You have two hierarchies that need to work together:

```
SOP Hierarchy (authoritative procedures):
  REF Doc (trade-level)  ←  shared PPE, codes, personnel
    └── SOP (task-level)  ←  procedure phases, steps, checklists

WO Hierarchy (editable work instances):
  System WO Template  →  Company WO  →  Project WO  →  Unit WO
  (S-SOP)               (COW)          (PWO)          (DWO)
```

### Three Options

#### Option A: Separate but Linked
```
sop_steps (canonical)  ←──FK──  work_order_tasks (customized copy)
```
SOPs and WOs are separate tables. WO tasks are copies that can diverge. Reconciliation = diff.

**Pro**: Clean separation, SOPs are pure reference docs  
**Con**: Data duplication, reconciliation is complex

#### Option B: SOPs ARE the System WO Templates
```
work_order_templates (scope='system') = SOP content
  └── company WO inherits
      └── project WO inherits
          └── unit WO inherits
```
No separate SOP tables. The system-level WO template IS the SOP.

**Pro**: Single hierarchy, no reconciliation needed  
**Con**: Loses SOP-specific structure (REF docs, sections, videos, dependencies). WO templates become bloated.

#### Option C: SOPs as Source of Truth, WOs Reference + Override ⭐ RECOMMENDED
```
sop_steps (canonical procedure)     work_order_templates.definition
  │                                   │
  │  ┌─────────────────────────────┐  │
  └──│  sop_id FK on WO template   │──┘
     │  Each WO step references     │
     │  an sop_step_id + allows     │
     │  local overrides in JSONB    │
     └─────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Recommended Architecture (Option C)

### Why This Works

1. **SOPs stay rich** — full sections, REF doc pointers, videos, dependencies, embeddings
2. **WOs stay light** — each level just stores overrides/customizations, not full procedure copies
3. **Each WO level is editable** — company can customize steps, project can further customize, unit can mark not_required
4. **SOP reference is always available** — field worker sees both the canonical SOP step AND any local modifications
5. **Reconciliation is trivial** — compare WO override JSONB against current sop_step content; flag diffs

### Schema Design

```sql
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════
-- SOP TABLES (source of truth, git-synced)
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════

-- Master SOP record (enhanced from current)
sops (
  id uuid PK,
  sop_number text UNIQUE,        -- 'PLB-SOP-001'
  trade_code text,               -- 'PLB'
  trade_name text,               -- 'Plumbing'
  task_name text,
  revision text,
  status text,                   -- 'draft'|'active'|'archived'
  est_duration_min int,          -- NEW
  est_duration_max int,          -- NEW
  ref_doc_id uuid,               -- NEW: FK → sop_ref_docs
  storage_path text,
  file_hash text,
  tags text[],
  created_at, updated_at
)

-- NEW: Trade-level reference documents
sop_ref_docs (
  id uuid PK,
  doc_id text UNIQUE,            -- 'REF-PLB-001'
  trade_code text,
  trade_name text,
  revision text,
  content_md text,               -- full markdown
  storage_path text,
  file_hash text,
  created_at, updated_at
)

-- REPLACES: sop_executive_checklists + sop_detailed_steps
sop_sections (
  id uuid PK,
  sop_id uuid FK → sops,
  section_number int,
  section_type text,             -- 'purpose'|'scope'|'safety'|'tools'|'procedure'|...
  title text,
  content_md text,
  content_plain text,            -- for tsvector search
  is_ref_pointer boolean,        -- true if section says "See REF doc"
  ref_section text,              -- which REF section it points to
  created_at, updated_at
)

-- UNIFIED step table (each row = one RAG chunk = one trackable unit)
sop_steps (
  id uuid PK,
  sop_id uuid FK → sops,
  section_id uuid FK → sop_sections,  -- which procedure section
  phase_name text,               -- 'Removal', 'Installation'
  phase_number int,
  step_number int,
  step_name text,                -- matches Executive Checklist
  tasks_summary text,            -- checklist-level description
  detail_md text,                -- detailed step content
  created_at, updated_at
)

-- NEW: Dependency graph
sop_dependencies (
  id uuid PK,
  from_sop_id uuid FK → sops,
  to_sop_id uuid FK → sops,
  link_type text,                -- 'prerequisite'|'sub-procedure'|'optional'|'cross-reference'
  condition_note text
)

-- KEEP: Videos (add sop_step_id for step-level mapping)
sop_recommended_videos (
  id uuid PK,
  sop_id uuid FK → sops,
  sop_step_id uuid FK → sop_steps,  -- NEW: which step this video covers
  title text,
  url text,
  procedure_phase text,
  sort_order int
)

-- NEW: AI embeddings
sop_embeddings (
  id uuid PK,
  sop_step_id uuid FK → sop_steps,
  chunk_text text,
  embedding vector(1536)
)

-- KEEP: Audit log
sop_sync_log (...same...)

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════
-- WO ↔ SOP LINKAGE (the bridge)
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════

-- Add to existing work_order_templates:
ALTER TABLE work_order_templates
  ADD COLUMN sop_id uuid REFERENCES sops(id),
  ADD COLUMN sop_overrides jsonb DEFAULT '{}';  
  -- overrides = { "step_3": { "detail_md": "custom instructions..." } }

-- Add to existing work_orders:
ALTER TABLE work_orders
  ADD COLUMN sop_id uuid REFERENCES sops(id);

-- Step-level progress tracking (for timesheets!)
CREATE TABLE work_order_step_progress (
  id uuid PK,
  work_order_id uuid FK → work_orders,
  sop_step_id uuid FK → sop_steps,
  status text DEFAULT 'pending',  -- 'pending'|'in_progress'|'completed'|'skipped'
  completed_by uuid,              -- FK → worker (employee/sub-contractor)
  completed_at timestamptz,
  duration_minutes int,           -- employees report this
  notes text,
  photo_urls text[],
  override_detail text,           -- if this WO level customized the step
  created_at, updated_at
);
```

### How the WO Hierarchy Uses SOPs

```
System WO Template (scope='app')
  └── sop_id = PLB-SOP-001
  └── sop_overrides = {}  (pure SOP)

Company WO Template (scope='company', parent=system)
  └── sop_id = PLB-SOP-001 (inherited)
  └── sop_overrides = { "step_2": { "detail_md": "We use SharkBite fittings" } }

Project WO Template (scope='project', parent=company)
  └── sop_id = PLB-SOP-001 (inherited)
  └── sop_overrides = { "step_2": ...(inherited), "step_5": { "not_required": true } }

Unit WO (actual work order instance)
  └── sop_id = PLB-SOP-001
  └── work_order_step_progress rows (one per sop_step)
      └── Each tracks: status, who completed, duration, notes, photos
```

### Reconciliation

Periodic reconciliation becomes a simple query:
```sql
-- Find WO templates with overrides that differ from current SOP
SELECT wot.id, wot.trade_name, wot.scope,

# Plan: SOP System Evolution + Work Order Integration

Now that I have the full `sop.md` command, here's the complete picture and my recommendation.

---

## What sop.md Adds to the README

The Claude command defines a **7-step authoring workflow** with concepts the current skill doesn't handle:

| Concept | In README? | In sop.md? | Impact |
|---|---|---|---|
| REF docs (trade-level shared references) | ❌ | ✅ Step 4 | SOPs use pointer links instead of duplicating PPE/codes/personnel |
| SOP_INDEX.md as master registry | Mentioned | ✅ Steps 2,3,6 | Planned → Active lifecycle, prerequisite/sub-procedure tracking |
| Trade folder organization | ❌ | ✅ Step 5 | `docs/sop/{TRADE}/SOP_{TRADE}_{Task}.md` |
| REF doc registration in index + authoring guide | ❌ | ✅ Step 4 | Cross-document consistency maintenance |
| Exact 15-section SOP structure with REF pointers | Partial | ✅ Step 5 | Sections 3,4,5,cleanup use `See REF_...` pointers |
| Executive Checklist max 7 steps rule | ❌ | ✅ Step 5 | Split into sub-phases if >7 |
| Video placeholder pattern | ❌ | ✅ Step 5 | `🔍 *Video to be sourced*` |
| Planned entry workflow | ❌ | ✅ Step 3 | Reserve SOP number before authoring |

---

## The Core Architecture Question

You have two hierarchies that need to work together:

```
SOP Hierarchy (authoritative procedures):
  REF Doc (trade-level)  ←  shared PPE, codes, personnel
    └── SOP (task-level)  ←  procedure phases, steps, checklists

WO Hierarchy (editable work instances):
  System WO Template  →  Company WO  →  Project WO  →  Unit WO
  (S-SOP)               (COW)          (PWO)          (DWO)
```

### Three Options

#### Option A: Separate but Linked
```
sop_steps (canonical)  ←──FK──  work_order_tasks (customized copy)
```
SOPs and WOs are separate tables. WO tasks are copies that can diverge. Reconciliation = diff.

**Pro**: Clean separation, SOPs are pure reference docs  
**Con**: Data duplication, reconciliation is complex

#### Option B: SOPs ARE the System WO Templates
```
work_order_templates (scope='system') = SOP content
  └── company WO inherits
      └── project WO inherits
          └── unit WO inherits
```
No separate SOP tables. The system-level WO template IS the SOP.

**Pro**: Single hierarchy, no reconciliation needed  
**Con**: Loses SOP-specific structure (REF docs, sections, videos, dependencies). WO templates become bloated.

#### Option C: SOPs as Source of Truth, WOs Reference + Override ⭐ RECOMMENDED
```
sop_steps (canonical procedure)     work_order_templates.definition
  │                                   │
  │  ┌─────────────────────────────┐  │
  └──│  sop_id FK on WO template   │──┘
     │  Each WO step references     │
     │  an sop_step_id + allows     │
     │  local overrides in JSONB    │
     └─────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Recommended Architecture (Option C)

### Why This Works

1. **SOPs stay rich** — full sections, REF doc pointers, videos, dependencies, embeddings
2. **WOs stay light** — each level just stores overrides/customizations, not full procedure copies
3. **Each WO level is editable** — company can customize steps, project can further customize, unit can mark not_required
4. **SOP reference is always available** — field worker sees both the canonical SOP step AND any local modifications
5. **Reconciliation is trivial** — compare WO override JSONB against current sop_step content; flag diffs

### Schema Design

```sql
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════
-- SOP TABLES (source of truth, git-synced)
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════

-- Master SOP record (enhanced from current)
sops (
  id uuid PK,
  sop_number text UNIQUE,        -- 'PLB-SOP-001'
  trade_code text,               -- 'PLB'
  trade_name text,               -- 'Plumbing'
  task_name text,
  revision text,
  status text,                   -- 'draft'|'active'|'archived'
  est_duration_min int,          -- NEW
  est_duration_max int,          -- NEW
  ref_doc_id uuid,               -- NEW: FK → sop_ref_docs
  storage_path text,
  file_hash text,
  tags text[],
  created_at, updated_at
)

-- NEW: Trade-level reference documents
sop_ref_docs (
  id uuid PK,
  doc_id text UNIQUE,            -- 'REF-PLB-001'
  trade_code text,
  trade_name text,
  revision text,
  content_md text,               -- full markdown
  storage_path text,
  file_hash text,
  created_at, updated_at
)

-- REPLACES: sop_executive_checklists + sop_detailed_steps
sop_sections (
  id uuid PK,
  sop_id uuid FK → sops,
  section_number int,
  section_type text,             -- 'purpose'|'scope'|'safety'|'tools'|'procedure'|...
  title text,
  content_md text,
  content_plain text,            -- for tsvector search
  is_ref_pointer boolean,        -- true if section says "See REF doc"
  ref_section text,              -- which REF section it points to
  created_at, updated_at
)

-- UNIFIED step table (each row = one RAG chunk = one trackable unit)
sop_steps (
  id uuid PK,
  sop_id uuid FK → sops,
  section_id uuid FK → sop_sections,  -- which procedure section
  phase_name text,               -- 'Removal', 'Installation'
  phase_number int,
  step_number int,
  step_name text,                -- matches Executive Checklist
  tasks_summary text,            -- checklist-level description
  detail_md text,                -- detailed step content
  created_at, updated_at
)

-- NEW: Dependency graph
sop_dependencies (
  id uuid PK,
  from_sop_id uuid FK → sops,
  to_sop_id uuid FK → sops,
  link_type text,                -- 'prerequisite'|'sub-procedure'|'optional'|'cross-reference'
  condition_note text
)

-- KEEP: Videos (add sop_step_id for step-level mapping)
sop_recommended_videos (
  id uuid PK,
  sop_id uuid FK → sops,
  sop_step_id uuid FK → sop_steps,  -- NEW: which step this video covers
  title text,
  url text,
  procedure_phase text,
  sort_order int
)

-- NEW: AI embeddings
sop_embeddings (
  id uuid PK,
  sop_step_id uuid FK → sop_steps,
  chunk_text text,
  embedding vector(1536)
)

-- KEEP: Audit log
sop_sync_log (...same...)

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════
-- WO ↔ SOP LINKAGE (the bridge)
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════

-- Add to existing work_order_templates:
ALTER TABLE work_order_templates
  ADD COLUMN sop_id uuid REFERENCES sops(id),
  ADD COLUMN sop_overrides jsonb DEFAULT '{}';  
  -- overrides = { "step_3": { "detail_md": "custom instructions..." } }

-- Add to existing work_orders:
ALTER TABLE work_orders
  ADD COLUMN sop_id uuid REFERENCES sops(id);

-- Step-level progress tracking (for timesheets!)
CREATE TABLE work_order_step_progress (
  id uuid PK,
  work_order_id uuid FK → work_orders,
  sop_step_id uuid FK → sop_steps,
  status text DEFAULT 'pending',  -- 'pending'|'in_progress'|'completed'|'skipped'
  completed_by uuid,              -- FK → worker (employee/sub-contractor)
  completed_at timestamptz,
  duration_minutes int,           -- employees report this
  notes text,
  photo_urls text[],
  override_detail text,           -- if this WO level customized the step
  created_at, updated_at
);
```

### How the WO Hierarchy Uses SOPs

```
System WO Template (scope='app')
  └── sop_id = PLB-SOP-001
  └── sop_overrides = {}  (pure SOP)

Company WO Template (scope='company', parent=system)
  └── sop_id = PLB-SOP-001 (inherited)
  └── sop_overrides = { "step_2": { "detail_md": "We use SharkBite fittings" } }

Project WO Template (scope='project', parent=company)
  └── sop_id = PLB-SOP-001 (inherited)
  └── sop_overrides = { "step_2": ...(inherited), "step_5": { "not_required": true } }

Unit WO (actual work order instance)
  └── sop_id = PLB-SOP-001
  └── work_order_step_progress rows (one per sop_step)
      └── Each tracks: status, who completed, duration, notes, photos
```

### Reconciliation

Periodic reconciliation becomes a simple query:
```sql
-- Find WO templates with overrides that differ from current SOP
SELECT wot.id, wot.trade_name, wot.scope,
       wot.sop_overrides,
       s.revision as current_sop_revision
FROM work_order_templates wot
JOIN sops s ON wot.sop_id = s.id
WHERE wot.sop_overrides != '{}';
```

### Timesheet Connection

`work_order_step_progress.duration_minutes` is where employee timesheets come from:
- **Employees**: MUST fill in `duration_minutes` for each step they complete
- **Sub-contractors (default)**: Only need to set `status = 'completed'`
- **Sub-contractors (hours required)**: Also fill in `duration_minutes`

---

## Part 3: README.md Update Plan

### Sections to Add

| New Section | Content Source |
|---|---|
| **REF Document Management** | sop.md Step 4 — creating/registering trade-level reference docs |
| **SOP Authoring Workflow** | sop.md Steps 1-7 — the full create/locate lifecycle |
| **Index Management** | sop.md Steps 2,3,6 — planned→active lifecycle |
| **Work Order Integration** | Option C design above |
| **AI/RAG Integration** | sop_embeddings + pgvector semantic search |
| **Dependency Graph** | sop_dependencies + Mermaid rendering |
| **Reconciliation** | WO override diffing against current SOP |
| **Git→DB Sync Pipeline** | GitHub Action: parse → upsert → embed |

### Sections to Update

| Existing Section | Changes |
|---|---|
| **Architecture** | Add REF doc layer, git→DB pipeline, WO linkage |
| **Database Schema** | New 10-table schema (sops, ref_docs, sections, steps, deps, embeddings, videos, sync_log, + WO bridge tables) |
| **CLI Reference** | Add: `ref create`, `ref list`, `dep show`, `reconcile` commands |
| **Python API** | Add: ref doc management, dependency queries, embedding generation, reconciliation |

---

## Execution Order

1. **📝 Update README.md** — incorporate sop.md workflow + Option C architecture
2. **🗄️ Schema migration** — new SOP tables + WO linkage columns
3. **🔧 Update sop_manager.py** — REF doc support, new parser for sections/steps, index management
4. **🔗 WO template sop_id linkage** — connect existing templates to SOPs
5. **👷 Workforce tables** — employees/sub-contractors with timesheet rules
6. **📊 Step progress tracking** — work_order_step_progress for completion + duration
7. **🤖 Embedding pipeline** — pgvector setup + sync
8. **🔄 Reconciliation tooling** — diff WO overrides vs current SOP

kvps.memories:
The project uses a multi-tier work order (WO) hierarchy: System WO → Company WO → Project WO → Unit/Workspace WO, where each level is editable and can reference SOP steps.

The SOP system is evolving into a hybrid architecture where Git (Markdown) is the source of truth for authoring and versioning, while Supabase PostgreSQL serves as the structured layer for app queries, AI retrieval (RAG), and work order linking.

The proposed SOP schema includes: `sops` (master), `sop_ref_docs` (trade-level shared references), `sop_sections` (typed content), `sop_steps` (unified checklist and detailed instructions), `sop_dependencies` (Mermaid-based prerequisite graphs), and `sop_embeddings` (pgvector for semantic search).

Work orders will link to SOPs via `sop_id` and `sop_overrides` JSONB columns in the `work_order_templates` table, allowing each hierarchy level to customize procedures while maintaining a link to the canonical source.

Workforce management requirements: Employees must report task duration on timesheets; sub-contractors generally only report work completion, though some specific sub-contractors may be flagged to require hour reporting.

A new `work_order_step_progress` table will track status, completion, and duration per SOP step, serving as the data source for both work validation and timesheet generation.

The 'Bill Hennessy Place' project (alias 'Hennessy') was used to seed the initial database; the primary tenant in the system should be 'Team Projects'.

SOP authoring follows a 7-step workflow: Parse Input, Check Index, Add Planned Entry, Create REF Doc (if needed), Generate SOP (with pointers to REF docs), Update Index, and Report.

Trade codes used in the system: PLB (Plumbing), ELC (Electrical), HVC (HVAC), CRP (Carpentry), PNT (Painting), RFG (Roofing), FLR (Flooring), DRW (Drywall), LND (Landscaping), GEN (General), MSN (Masonry), WLD (Welding).

logs.content:
```json
[
  {
    "problem": "The user needs to evolve the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) management system to support a hybrid Git-to-Database architecture and integrate it with a multi-tier Work Order (WO) hierarchy (System -> Company -> Project -> Unit).",
    "solution": "1. **Adopt a Hybrid Architecture**: Use Git (Markdown) as the source of truth for authoring and versioning, and sync parsed content to Supabase (PostgreSQL) for application queries, AI RAG retrieval, and Work Order linking.
2. **Implement a 7-Table SOP Schema**:
    - `sops`: Master record with `sop_number`, `trade_code`, `status`, and `file_hash`.
    - `sop_ref_docs`: Trade-level reference documents (e.g., REF-PLB-001) for shared codes, PPE, and safety standards to avoid duplication in individual SOPs.
    - `sop_sections`: Typed sections (purpose, scope, safety, procedure) to enable granular search.
    - `sop_steps`: Unified table for executive checklists and detailed steps; each row serves as a trackable unit and a RAG chunk.
    - `sop_dependencies`: Models prerequisite and sub-procedure chains (e.g., 'prerequisite', 'cross-reference').
    - `sop_embeddings`: Stores `pgvector` embeddings for semantic search.
    - `sop_recommended_videos`: Maps tutorial videos to specific procedure phases.
3. **Integrate with Work Order Hierarchy (Option C: Reference + Override)**:
    - Add `sop_id` and `sop_overrides` (JSONB) columns to the `work_order_templates` table.
    - Allow each level (Company, Project, Unit) to inherit the SOP but store local modifications (e.g., specific materials or skipped steps) in the `sop_overrides` field.
    - Create a `work_order_step_progress` table to track real-time status, worker ID, completion timestamp, and `duration_minutes` for each SOP step.
4. **Connect to Workforce Management**:
    - Use `work_order_step_progress.duration_minutes` as the source for employee timesheets.
    - Configure the system so employees must report duration per step, while sub-contractors only mark completion unless specifically flagged for hour tracking.
5. **Implement Reconciliation**: Create a diffing mechanism to compare `sop_overrides` in WO templates against the current version of the canonical `sop_steps` to identify and resolve discrepancies."
  },
  {
    "problem": "The user requested a plan to manage employees and sub-contractors for a general contractor, specifically handling different timesheet reporting requirements (duration for employees vs. completion for sub-contractors).",
    "solution": "1. **Define Worker Types**: Create a workforce model distinguishing between 'Employee' and 'Sub-contractor'.
2. **Configure Reporting Rules**: Implement a 'requires_hours' flag at the contractor or work-order level to determine if duration must be reported for a timesheet to be valid.
3. **Link to SOP Steps**: Integrate timesheet entries with the `work_order_step_progress` table. 
4. **Granular Tracking**: 
    - Employees: Record `duration_minutes` for each SOP step completed.
    - Sub-contractors: Default to marking `status = 'completed'` for steps; if `requires_hours` is true, enforce `duration_minutes` entry.
5. **Management UI**: Host the workforce management interface in the `work-orders` app (localhost:3004) for tenant/GC use, while maintaining sysadmin oversight in the `tenant-portal` (localhost:3005)."
  }
]

kvps.memories:
SOP authoring follows a 7-step workflow: Parse Input, Check Index, Add Planned Entry, Create REF Doc (if needed), Generate SOP (with pointers to REF docs), Update Index, and Report.

The application architecture (voicelog-scaffold) utilizes a standardized multi-tier hierarchy governed by a 'copy-on-inherit' model to ensure downstream isolation. 

1. Scope & Work Order (WO) Hierarchy: System (Global Defaults/S-SOP/SWO) → Company (Organization Overrides/COW) → Project (Project-Specific/PWO) → Unit/Workspace (Instance/DWO). Each level is editable and can reference SOP steps. 
2. Implementation Details: DWOs are unit-level instances utilizing a 'not_required' toggle within the 'task_data' JSONB field to optimize data volume and provide granular flexibility. 
3. Terminology: The 'app' scope has been fully renamed to 'system' across the database, API, and frontend. 
4. Type Order (TO) Hierarchy: Defines workspaces (replacing legacy 'rooms') at System, Company, and Project levels; can be 'Atomic' or 'Composite'. 
5. Database & Technical Status: Phase 1 schema evolution (Migration 20260304000001) introduced eight tables (e.g., trades, organization_trades) with denormalized trade names via triggers (sync_trade_name). As of March 2026, the API layer (api.ts) lacks CRUD methods for these new entities.

SOP linking and nesting rules allow for cross-references, prerequisites, and sub-procedures (e.g., a sink install SOP calling a garbage disposal sub-procedure), with a maximum nesting depth of 2 levels and a prohibition on circular references.

The Phase 1 database schema evolution introduced eight new tables, including 'bom_action_requests' for PDF-to-work-order automation, and the 'work_order_step_progress' table. The 'work_order_step_progress' table tracks status, completion, and duration per SOP step, serving as the data source for both work validation and timesheet generation. Other tables include 'trades', 'organization_trades', 'project_outcomes', 'trade_outcome_assignments', 'type_orders', 'type_order_compositions', and 'type_order_default_work_orders'. The frontend 'ProjectDetail' page supports matching/rejecting BOM requests derived from architectural documents.

## 🎯 Changelog

    - In-browser file editor for viewing and editing files without leaving the UI

    - Redesigned sidebar with reusable dropdown component and streamlined buttons
    - Inline button confirmations for critical actions

- Git projects
    - Git-based projects with clone authentication for public and private repositories
- Four new LLM providers: CometAPI, Z.AI, Moonshot AI, and AWS Bedrock
- Microsoft Dev Tunnels integration for secure remote access
- User data migration to `/usr` directory for cleaner separation of user and system files
- Subagents system with configurable agent profiles for different roles
- Memory operations offloaded to deferred tasks for better performance
- Environment variables can now configure settings via `A0_SET_*` prefix in `.env`
- Automatic migration with overwrite support for `.env`, scheduler, knowledge, and legacy directories
- Projects support extended to MCP, A2A, and external API

kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Evolve the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) management system from a basic Git-to-DB sync into a multi-tenant, hierarchical architecture that supports inheritance (App -> Company -> Project -> Unit) and granular workforce tracking.

# Solution
1. **Hybrid Git-to-Database Architecture**: 
   - Use Git (Markdown) as the source of truth for authoring (15-section template, 3-tier procedure depth).
   - Sync via Python parser (`sop_manager.py`) using MD5 hashing for change detection.
   - Store in Supabase (PostgreSQL) for RAG retrieval and Work Order (WO) linking.

2. **Expanded 7-Table SOP Schema**:
   - `sops`: Master records with `sop_number`, `trade_code`, and `file_hash`.
   - `sop_ref_docs`: Shared trade-level reference documents (PPE, safety) to reduce redundancy.
   - `sop_sections` & `sop_steps`: Granular storage of typed sections and unified checklist steps for RAG chunking.
   - `sop_dependencies`: Prerequisite/cross-reference chains (Mermaid-based).
   - `sop_embeddings`: `pgvector` for semantic search.
   - `sop_recommended_videos`: Mapping tutorial videos to procedure phases.

3. **Hierarchical Inheritance & Overrides**:
   - **Hierarchy**: App-Level (Global) -> Company-Level -> Project-Level -> Unit-Level.
   - **Reference + Override Model**: Add `sop_id` and `sop_overrides` (JSONB) to `work_order_templates`. 
   - **Lazy-Copy**: Project-level templates remain dynamic until 'frozen' (status change to 'active'), triggering a copy of parent definitions.
   - **Reconciliation**: Diffing mechanism to compare local `sop_overrides` against canonical `sop_steps` versions.

4. **Workforce & Progress Integration**:
   - **Tracking**: `work_order_step_progress` table tracks real-time status, worker ID, and `duration_minutes` per step.
   - **Timesheets**: Step duration serves as the source for employee timesheets; sub-contractors mark completion only unless flagged for hours.
   - **Agent Integration**: `check_and_request(trade, task)` method to trigger AI-assisted SOP authoring if a document is missing.

5. **Interface & Search**:
   - Multi-column keyword search via Supabase `.or_()` filters.
   - Unified CLI/API for `sync`, `search`, and `show` operations.

kvps.solutions:
# Problem
A sysadmin user needs full-access permissions across all tenant data in a Supabase environment with Row Level Security (RLS) enabled.

# Solution
1. **Helper Function**: Create a database function `is_sysadmin()` that checks if the `auth.jwt()` contains a `service_role` or a specific metadata flag identifying the user as a system administrator.
2. **RLS Policy Overhaul**: Apply `CREATE POLICY` statements to all relevant tables (e.g., organizations, projects, templates) using `USING (is_sysadmin())` and `WITH CHECK (is_sysadmin())` to grant bypass permissions.
3. **Frontend Gating**: In the React/SolidJS `App.tsx`, implement a check on the authenticated session. If the user lacks the sysadmin role when accessing the admin portal, trigger an immediate sign-out and redirect to an 'Access Denied' page.
4. **Automation**: Add a step to the environment setup script (e.g., `reset-and-start.ps1`) to automatically insert a default sysadmin user into `auth.users` and the profiles table during database resets.

(venv) root@d2d162d983f0:/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold# -- =============================================================
-- Migration: Project Lifecycle & Template Inheritance
-- Description:
--   1. Expand wo_projects.status to include 'staged' and 'paused'
--   2. Add is_frozen column to work_order_templates
--   3. Allow NULL definition for lazy-copy project templates
--   4. Create freeze_project_templates() trigger function
--   5. Seed app-scope templates from existing company templates
-- =============================================================

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 1. Expand project status CHECK constraint
-- -----------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE public.wo_projects DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS wo_projects_status_check;
ALTER TABLE public.wo_projects ADD CONSTRAINT wo_projects_status_check
  CHECK (status IN ('staged', 'active', 'paused', 'completed', 'archived'));

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 2. Add is_frozen to templates
-- -----------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE public.work_order_templates
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS is_frozen BOOLEAN DEFAULT false;

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 3. Allow NULL definition for lazy-copy templates
--    (project templates reference parent until frozen)
-- -----------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE public.work_order_templates
  ALTER COLUMN definition DROP NOT NULL;

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 4. Trigger: freeze project templates on activation
--    When a project transitions to 'active', copy the
--    definition from the parent company template into
--    each unfrozen project template.
-- -----------------------------------------------
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.freeze_project_templates()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  -- Only fire when status changes TO 'active'
  IF NEW.status = 'active' AND (OLD.status IS DISTINCT FROM 'active') THEN
    UPDATE public.work_order_templates pt
    SET
      definition = ct.definition,
      is_frozen = true,
      updated_at = now()
    FROM public.work_order_templates ct
    WHERE pt.project_id = NEW.id
      AND pt.scope = 'project'
      AND pt.parent_template_id = ct.id
      AND pt.is_frozen = false
      AND pt.definition IS NULL;
  END IF;
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_freeze_project_templates ON public.wo_projects;
CREATE TRIGGER trg_freeze_project_templates
  AFTER UPDATE OF status ON public.wo_projects
  FOR EACH ROW
  EXECUTE FUNCTION public.freeze_project_templates();

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- 5. Seed app-scope templates
--    Create app-scope copies of each unique trade
--    from the existing company templates (org 00...01)
-- -----------------------------------------------
INSERT INTO public.work_order_templates (
  id, organization_id, trade_name, version, is_active,
  definition, scope, project_id, is_frozen
)
SELECT
  -- Deterministic UUIDs: A0000000-... prefix for app templates
  ('a0000000-0000-0000-0000-' || lpad(row_number() OVER (ORDER BY trade_name)::text, 12, '0'))::uuid,
  NULL,                    -- no org for app-scope
  trade_name,
  1,
  true,
  definition,              -- copy full definition
  'app',                   -- app scope
  NULL,                    -- no project
  false                    -- not frozen (app templates are the source of truth)
FROM public.work_order_templates
WHERE scope = 'company'
  AND organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001'
  AND is_active = true
ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;

-- Update the existing company templates to point back to their app source
UPDATE public.work_order_templates ct
SET copied_from_template_id = at.id
FROM public.work_order_templates at
WHERE at.scope = 'app'
  AND ct.scope = 'company'
  AND ct.organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001'
  AND ct.trade_name = at.trade_name
  AND ct.copied_from_template_id IS NULL;

kvps.memories:
The application implements a multi-tier template inheritance system (App/System → Company → Project) where project-level templates are 'frozen' when a project status changes to 'active'. This freezing mechanism ensures downstream isolation by copying definitions from parent templates to the project level, preventing retroactive changes. 

Implementation Details:
- Logic: Handled by the PostgreSQL trigger `freeze_project_templates()`.
- Database Migration: `20260222000002_project_lifecycle_template_inheritance.sql`.
- Documentation: 'docs/workorders/Template_Hierarchy_Design.md'.
- Mechanism: Uses a 'copy-on-inherit' model to create independent snapshots.

kvps.memories:
The application architecture (voicelog-scaffold) utilizes a standardized four-tier scope hierarchy (System, Company, Project, and Unit/DWO) governed by a 'copy-on-inherit' model for customizations.

1. Scope Hierarchy: System (Global Defaults), Company (Organization Overrides), Project (Project-Specific), and Unit (Instance/DWO). The 'app' scope has been fully renamed to 'system' across the database, API, and frontend components. System flexibility documentation is located at /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/docs/general/system_flexibility.md.

2. Work Order Hierarchy (5-tier): S-SOP -> SWO (System) -> COW (Company) -> PWO (Project) -> DWO (Unit). DWOs are implemented as instances with a 'not_required' toggle in task_data JSONB.

3. Type Order (TO) Hierarchy: Defines workspaces (replacing legacy 'rooms') at System, Company, and Project levels. TOs can be 'Atomic' (e.g., Kitchen) or 'Composite' (e.g., Studio unit templates) and use a hierarchical 'type_orders' system.

4. Database Evolution (Migration 20260304000001): Phase 1 schema evolution introduced eight new tables: trades, organization_trades, project_outcomes, trade_outcome_assignments, type_orders, type_order_compositions, type_order_default_work_orders, and bom_action_requests. This replaced legacy structures like 'template_libraries' and flat 'rooms'.

5. Technical Status: API layer (api.ts) currently lacks CRUD methods for new entities. The migration from 'app' to 'system' terminology is now complete across all layers. Database triggers (e.g., sync_trade_name) are active to sync denormalized trade names, supported by a 41-test validation suite.

The application architecture (voicelog-scaffold) utilizes a standardized multi-tier hierarchy governed by a 'copy-on-inherit' model to ensure downstream isolation. 

1. Scope & Work Order (WO) Hierarchy: System (Global Defaults/S-SOP/SWO) → Company (Organization Overrides/COW) → Project (Project-Specific/PWO) → Unit/Workspace (Instance/DWO). Each level is editable and can reference SOP steps. 
2. Implementation Details: DWOs are unit-level instances utilizing a 'not_required' toggle within the 'task_data' JSONB field to optimize data volume and provide granular flexibility. 
3. Terminology: The 'app' scope has been fully renamed to 'system' across the database, API, and frontend. 
4. Type Order (TO) Hierarchy: Defines workspaces (replacing legacy 'rooms') at System, Company, and Project levels; can be 'Atomic' or 'Composite'. 
5. Database & Technical Status: Phase 1 schema evolution (Migration 20260304000001) introduced eight tables (e.g., trades, organization_trades) with denormalized trade names via triggers (sync_trade_name). As of March 2026, the API layer (api.ts) lacks CRUD methods for these new entities.

The 'voicelog-scaffold' (also referred to as 'voice-scaffold') project is a multi-tenant construction management and renovation platform for trades, focusing on timesheet and work-order management. It is organized as a pnpm/Turborepo monorepo and follows a 'schema-first' philosophy with Zod and a three-tier template inheritance system (App-Level, Company-Level, Project-Level).

Template Logic:
- Inheritance: App -> Company -> Project.
- Freezing Mechanism: Project templates are 'frozen' (definitions copied from parents) when a project status changes to 'active' to ensure downstream isolation and prevent retroactive changes.

Architecture & Apps:
- API (:3000): 'The Brain'. Features 12 route modules and an A2A JSON-RPC protocol with a Placeholder Recovery system.
- Web Dashboard (:3001): Legacy interface.
- Material Admin (:3002): Manages supplier catalogs and inventory via JsonTreeEditor.
- Site Companion (:3003): Mobile PWA for field workers with voice-to-structured-data capture.
- Work Orders (:3004): Primary management for work order lifecycles and project documents.
- Tenant Portal (:3005): Custom-built web interface for organization-level configuration.
- doc-parser (:5000): FastAPI/Detectron2 for PDF analysis. Codebase located at /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/doc-parser/.

Environment & Tech Stack:
- Local Path: 'C:\Users\Casey\voicelog-scaffold' (Windows) bind-mounted to '/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/' (Container).
- Stack: SolidJS, TypeScript, Vite 5, Tailwind CSS, Hono, Supabase (PostgreSQL/Storage), NocoDB, Zod.
- LLM Integration: Uses `litellm` to communicate with providers (e.g., Anthropic/Claude).
- Shared Packages: @repo/config, @repo/db, @repo/ui, @repo/logger, @repo/schemas, @repo/utils.

Documentation is located in 'docs/general/platform_analysis.md' and 'docs/general/dbTables.md'.

{
    "thoughts": [
        "Now I have the complete picture. Let me compile a comprehensive inventory of all WO inheritance code, organized by layer, and then answer the user's question about whether I'll be editing existing files or creating new ones.",
        "",

    ],
    "headline": "Complete WO inheritance code inventory and integration plan",
    "tool_name": "response",
    "tool_args": {
        "text": "# Complete WO Inheritance Code Inventory

Here's every file that currently manages WO inheritance, what it does, and exactly what I'd touch.

---

## Layer 1: Database (4 migrations)

| File | Lines | Role | Touch? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `supabase/migrations/20260222000002_project_lifecycle_template_inheritance.sql` | 95 | **Core inheritance engine**: `is_frozen` column, NULL definition for lazy-copy, `freeze_project_templates()` trigger (copies parent definition when project goes active), seeds app-scope templates | ❌ No edit |
| `supabase/migrations/20260304000001_schema_evolution_phase1.sql` | 520 | Added `sop_id`, `trade_id`, `inheritance_depth`, `source_template_id` to `work_order_templates`; renamed `app`→`system` scope; created `sops` table | ❌ No edit |
| `supabase/migrations/20260228000001_sop_library.sql` | ~120 | Created `sops`, `sop_executive_checklists`, `sop_detailed_steps`, `sop_recommended_videos`, `sop_sync_log` | ❌ No edit |
| `supabase/migrations/20260304000002_schema_evolution_seed.sql` | ~300 | Seeded trades, type_orders, etc. | ❌ No edit |

**What exists in the DB today:**
```
work_order_templates
  ├── scope          ('system' | 'company' | 'project')
  ├── parent_template_id    → FK self (company→project link)
  ├── copied_from_template_id → FK self (tracks origin)
  ├── source_template_id    → FK self (added in phase1, unused)
  ├── is_frozen             (bool, set when project activates)
  ├── definition            (JSONB, NULL = lazy-copy from parent)
  ├── sop_id               → FK sops (added in phase1, unused)
  ├── trade_id             → FK trades (added in phase1)
  ├── inheritance_depth     (int, added in phase1, unused)
  └── project_id           → FK wo_projects

sops (exists but disconnected from WO flow)
  ├── sop_number, trade_code, trade_name, task_name
  ├── trade_id             (added in phase1)
  └── scope                (added in phase1)
```

---

## Layer 2: API (2 files)

| File | Lines | Inheritance Code | Touch? |
|---|---|---|---|
| **`apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts`** | 1,121 | **3 endpoints + 1 resolver**: | ✏️ Edit |
| | | `GET /templates/project/:projectId` (lines 433–465) — fetches project templates, resolves NULL definitions from `parent_template_id` | |
| | | `POST /templates/assign-to-company` (lines 468–520) — hard-copies system→company template | |
| | | `POST /templates/assign-to-project` (lines 523–595) — lazy-ref or frozen-copy company→project, checks project status for auto-freeze | |
| | | `DELETE /templates/:id/unassign` (lines 598+) — removes project template with WO check | |
| **`apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts`** | 415 | **1 function**: | ✏️ Edit |
| | | `copyTemplatesToOrg()` (lines 62–93) — copies system-scope templates to company-scope during onboarding, filtered by trade/industry | |

---

## Layer 3: Frontend (5 files)

| File | Lines | Inheritance Code | Touch? |
|---|---|---|---|
| **`apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts`** | 746 | `WorkOrderTemplate` interface with `parent_template_id`, `copied_from_template_id`, `is_frozen`, `_inherited`, `scope` fields; `getTemplates(scope?)` method | ✏️ Edit (add types) |
| **`apps/work-orders/src/pages/ProjectTemplates.tsx`** | 274 | Displays frozen/inherited badges, info banners for staged vs active projects | ⚠️ Minor edit |
| **`apps/work-orders/src/pages/TemplateDetail.tsx`** | 219 | Shows parent_template_id and copied_from_template_id as links | ⚠️ Minor edit |
| **`apps/work-orders/src/pages/TemplateEditor.tsx`** | 531 | Frozen template warning banner, prevents editing frozen templates | ⚠️ Minor edit |
| **`apps/work-orders/src/pages/CompanyTemplates.tsx`** | ? | Lists company-scope templates | ⚠️ Minor edit |

---

## Layer 4: DB Trigger (in migration 2)

```sql
freeze_project_templates()  -- AFTER UPDATE OF status ON wo_projects
  -- When project → 'active': copies parent definition into unfrozen project templates
```

---

## The Integration Plan: No Clutter

### What I will NOT do
- ❌ Create parallel SOP API routes that duplicate WO logic
- ❌ Create new frontend pages for SOP-WO linking (use existing template pages)
- ❌ Create new tables that overlap with existing `sop_*` tables
- ❌ Add a separate inheritance system

### What I WILL do

#### 1 new migration file (the only new file in DB layer)
```
supabase/migrations/20260310000001_sop_wo_bridge.sql
```
Contents:
- `sop_sections` table (replaces the flat `sop_executive_checklists` + `sop_detailed_steps` with a unified structure)
- `sop_steps` table (one row = one trackable/RAG-chunkable unit)
- `sop_dependencies` table (prerequisite/sub-procedure graph)
- `work_order_step_progress` table (step-level completion tracking → feeds timesheets)
- Wire up the **already-existing** `work_order_templates.sop_id` column (it's there, just unused)
- Wire up the **already-existing** `sops.trade_id` column
- Update `freeze_project_templates()` trigger to also copy `sop_id` when freezing

#### Edits to existing files (surgical additions, not rewrites)

| File | What changes | Lines affected |
|---|---|---|
| `work-orders.ts` | Add `sop_id` to template create/update. Add `GET /templates/:id/sop-steps` endpoint (resolves SOP steps with any WO-level overrides). Add `POST /work-orders/:id/step-progress` for completion tracking. | ~60 new lines in 3 blocks |
| `onboard.ts` | No change needed — `sop_id` on templates is populated at the system-scope level, inherited automatically via existing copy logic | 0 lines |
| `api.ts` (frontend) | Add `sop_id` to `WorkOrderTemplate` interface. Add `SopStep` interface. Add `getStepProgress()` / `updateStepProgress()` methods. | ~30 new lines |
| `TemplateDetail.tsx` | Show linked SOP name/number when `sop_id` is set | ~10 new lines |
| `TemplateEditor.tsx` | No change — SOP linkage is set at system level, not edited per-template | 0 lines |

#### Summary: File count

```
New files:     1  (migration)
Edited files:  3  (work-orders.ts, api.ts, TemplateDetail.tsx)
Total:         4 files touched
```

The existing inheritance chain (`system → company → project → unit`) stays exactly as-is. The `sop_id` rides along with the template through every copy/freeze operation automatically because the existing `copyTemplatesToOrg()` and `assign-to-project` endpoints already copy all columns.

**Want me to proceed with the migration file first?**"
    }
}

kvps.memories:
The system uses a four-tier Work Order (WO) template inheritance hierarchy: System (app-scope) -> Company (tenant-scope) -> Project (project-scope) -> Unit/Workspace (actual instance).

Inheritance logic is primarily managed in 'supabase/migrations/20260222000002_project_lifecycle_template_inheritance.sql' and 'apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts'.

Project-level templates use a 'lazy-copy' model where the 'definition' column is NULL and resolves from 'parent_template_id' until the project status is set to 'active'.

A database trigger 'freeze_project_templates()' automatically copies the parent template definition and sets 'is_frozen' to true when a project transitions to 'active' status.

The 'work_order_templates' table already contains unused columns for SOP integration: 'sop_id', 'trade_id', 'inheritance_depth', and 'source_template_id'.

The 'onboard.ts' API handles copying system-scope templates to company-scope during tenant onboarding, filtered by industry or trade.

SOP authoring follows a 7-step workflow defined in 'commands/sop.md', utilizing 'REF' documents for trade-level shared references (PPE, codes, personnel) to avoid duplication.

The proposed SOP-WO integration (Option C) maintains SOPs as the source of truth while allowing WO templates to store local overrides in a JSONB column, enabling easy reconciliation and step-level progress tracking for timesheets.

logs.content:
```json
[
  {
    "problem": "The user requested an enhanced tenant onboarding system with industry-specific trade assignments, organization profile management, and a secure sysadmin portal with Row Level Security (RLS) across multiple tables.",
    "solution": "1. **Database Schema Updates**: Create a migration to add `industry` and `description` columns to the `organizations` table. Implement an `is_sysadmin()` helper function and apply RLS policies across all relevant tables (e.g., 14 tables) to allow sysadmins full access while maintaining tenant isolation for others.
2. **API Enhancements**: 
    - Modify `PATCH /onboard/:id` to detect industry changes, remove old templates, and copy new ones from the app-scope based on the selected industry (e.g., 'general contractor' assigns all trades).
    - Update `GET /templates` to support an optional `scope` query parameter ('system', 'company', 'project') to prevent duplicate displays on dashboards.
3. **Frontend Implementation (SolidJS)**:
    - Update `TenantDetailPage.tsx` and `OrgProfile` interface to include industry dropdowns and description fields.
    - Implement a `fetchTrades` resource to populate industry selections from the API.
    - Update `App.tsx` in the tenant portal to enforce a sysadmin-only gate that signs out unauthorized users.
4. **Automation**: Update the environment setup script (e.g., `reset-and-start.ps1`) to automatically create a default sysadmin user (e.g., `sysadmin@example.com`) and ensure trade templates from `supabase/seed.sql` are correctly promoted to app-scope."
  },
  {
    "problem": "Implement a multi-tier Work Order (WO) template inheritance system (System -> Company -> Project -> Unit) where templates can be lazily referenced or 'frozen' into independent copies.",
    "solution": "1. **Database Logic**: 
    - Add `scope` ('system', 'company', 'project'), `parent_template_id`, and `is_frozen` columns to `work_order_templates`.
    - Create a PostgreSQL trigger `freeze_project_templates()` that fires when a project status changes to 'active'. This trigger copies the `definition` from the `parent_template_id` into the project template and sets `is_frozen = true`.
2. **API Implementation**:
    - In the `GET /templates/project/:projectId` endpoint, implement 'lazy-copy' resolution: if a template's `definition` is NULL and it has a `parent_template_id`, fetch and return the parent's definition dynamically.
    - Create `POST /templates/assign-to-project` logic that checks the project status; if the project is already 'active', perform a 'hard copy' of the definition immediately; otherwise, create a lazy reference (NULL definition).
3. **Frontend Integration**: Update the API client to handle the `_inherited` flag and `is_frozen` status. Modify template editors to display warning banners when a template is frozen, preventing accidental global changes from affecting active projects."
  },
  {
    "problem": "Design a 'docs-as-code' SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) system that integrates with the existing Work Order hierarchy for step-level tracking and timesheet validation.",
    "solution": "1. **SOP Structure**: Organize SOPs in Git as Markdown files using a specific directory structure: `docs/sop/{TRADE}/SOP_{TRADE}_{Task}.md`. Use 'REF' documents for shared trade-level data (PPE, codes) to avoid duplication.
2. **Unified Schema**: Create a migration for a unified SOP-WO bridge:
    - `sop_sections`: Stores parsed markdown sections (Purpose, Tools, etc.).
    - `sop_steps`: Stores individual trackable procedure steps (RAG-chunkable).
    - `work_order_step_progress`: Links a specific `work_order_id` to a `sop_step_id`, tracking `status`, `completed_by`, and `duration_minutes`.
3. **Inheritance Linkage**: Add a `sop_id` foreign key to `work_order_templates`. This ID is inherited through the existing `system -> company -> project` chain. 
4. **Reconciliation**: Implement logic to compare the `sop_overrides` JSONB field in a `work_order_template` against the canonical `sop_steps` to identify and resolve discrepancies between field practices and official procedures."
  }
]
```

prompt = r"""# SOP ↔ Work Order Integration — Implementation Prompt

> **Date**: 2026-03-09
> **Project**: voicelog-scaffold (Hennessy / Team Projects)
> **Codebase**: `/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold`
> **Stack**: SolidJS frontend, Hono API, Supabase (PostgreSQL), pnpm monorepo

---

## Executive Summary

This prompt implements the bridge between the existing SOP library system and the existing Work Order (WO) template inheritance hierarchy. The goal is to let each WO template reference an SOP, expose SOP steps through the WO API, and track step-level completion/duration at the unit work order level — feeding a separate timesheet application.

The design principle is **surgical integration**: we add to existing files rather than creating parallel structures. The existing WO inheritance chain (system → company → project → unit) stays untouched. SOP linkage rides along automatically through the existing copy/freeze mechanisms.

---

## Context: What Already Exists

### WO Template Inheritance (DO NOT MODIFY)

The three-tier template hierarchy is fully implemented:

```
System WO Template (scope='system', org=NULL, project=NULL)
  └── copied to → Company WO Template (scope='company', org=X, project=NULL)
      └── lazy-ref → Project WO Template (scope='project', org=X, project=Y)
          └── (frozen when project activates)
```

**Key files** (read these first to understand the patterns):

| File | Lines | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `supabase/migrations/

<< 31190 Characters hidden >>


def sync_sop_to_new_schema(supabase_client, sop_id: str, sections: list, steps: list):
    # Delete existing sections/steps for this SOP
    supabase_client.table('sop_steps').delete().eq('sop_id', sop_id).execute()
    supabase_client.table('sop_sections').delete().eq('sop_id', sop_id).execute()
    # Insert new
    if sections:
        supabase_client.table('sop_sections').insert(sections).execute()
    if steps:
        supabase_client.table('sop_steps').insert(steps).execute()
```

#### 4C. Add REF doc parsing

Parse `REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md` files into `sop_ref_docs` table.

#### 4D. Add dependency extraction

Parse the Scope section of each SOP for `(see {TRADE}-SOP-{NNN}: {Title})` references and create `sop_dependencies` records.

#### 4E. Update README.md

Update `skills/sop_library_manager/README.md` to document:
- New tables (sop_sections, sop_steps, sop_dependencies, sop_ref_docs, work_order_step_progress)
- New parsing functions
- REF doc management
- Dependency graph
- WO integration pattern

---

### Conversation 5 (Optional): Seed Data & Verification

#### 5A. Link existing SOPs to WO templates

Write a one-time script that:
1. Queries all `sops` with `trade_code`
2. Queries all `work_order_templates` with `scope='system'` and matching `trade_name`
3. Updates `work_order_templates.sop_id` to point to the matching SOP
4. Verifies the linkage propagated to company-scope templates

#### 5B. Parse existing SOPs into new schema

Run the updated `sop_manager.py` against all existing SOP markdown files to populate `sop_sections` and `sop_steps`.

#### 5C. End-to-end verification

Test the full flow:
1. System template has `sop_id` → ✓
2. Company template inherits `sop_id` via `copyTemplatesToOrg()` → ✓
3. Project template inherits `sop_id` via lazy-copy → ✓
4. `GET /templates/:id/sop` resolves steps → ✓
5. Create work order → initialize steps → update progress → ✓
6. Step progress records have `duration_minutes` for timesheet app → ✓

---

## File Impact Summary

```
NEW FILES (2):
  supabase/migrations/20260310000001_sop_wo_bridge.sql    — 6 tables, trigger update, RLS
  (none in API or frontend — all edits to existing files)

EDITED FILES (4):
  apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts     — +6 endpoints (~150 lines)
  apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts        — +4 interfaces, +6 methods (~80 lines)
  apps/work-orders/src/pages/TemplateDetail.tsx  — +1 SOP info section (~25 lines)
  apps/work-orders/src/pages/WorkOrderDetail.tsx — +1 StepProgressPanel (~60 lines)

UPDATED SKILL (1):
  skills/sop_library_manager/            — new parsing functions, README update

TOTAL: 2 new files, 4 edited files, 1 updated skill
```

## Conversation Splitting Guide

If splitting across conversations:

| Conv | Focus | Files | Depends On |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Database migration | `20260310000001_sop_wo_bridge.sql` | Nothing |
| 2 | API endpoints | `work-orders.ts` | Conv 1 |
| 3 | Frontend types + UI | `api.ts`, `TemplateDetail.tsx`, `WorkOrderDetail.tsx` | Conv 2 |
| 4 | SOP Manager update | `sop_manager.py`, `README.md` | Conv 1 |
| 5 | Seed data + verification | One-time scripts | Conv 1-4 |

Conversations 2+3 can be combined. Conversation 4 is independent of 2+3.
"""

    f.write(prompt)

kvps.memories:
The 'Hennessy' project (Team Projects) requires a workforce management system for employees and sub-contractors under a general contractor.

Employees report task durations on timesheets, while sub-contractors report completed work (with some hour-tracking exceptions).

A hybrid 'docs-as-code' architecture for the SOP Library Manager integrates Git-based markdown with a Supabase PostgreSQL layer for AI retrieval and app integration.

The system uses a three-tier template inheritance hierarchy: System WO Template (scope='system') -> Company WO Template (scope='company') -> Project WO Template (scope='project').

Project templates are 'frozen' (is_frozen=true) when a project activates, copying definitions from the parent.

A comprehensive implementation plan for SOP and Work Order integration is documented in 'docs/workorders/SOP_WO_INTEGRATION_PROMPT.md'.

New database tables for the SOP-WO bridge include 'sop_sections', 'sop_steps', 'sop_dependencies', 'sop_ref_docs', and 'work_order_step_progress'.

The 'work_order_step_progress' table tracks step-level completion and duration (duration_minutes) to feed a separate timesheet application.

The SOP authoring format (SOP-META-001 Rev 1.3) includes Executive Checklists (max 7 steps per phase) and Detailed Steps.

The system supports 'lazy-copying' where project templates reference parents until activation.

logs.content:
```json
[
  {
    "problem": "Design and implement a bridge between an existing SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) library and a multi-tier Work Order (WO) template inheritance system (system -> company -> project -> unit) to track step-level completion and duration for timesheet integration.",
    "content": "The solution involves a multi-step implementation plan across database, API, and frontend layers, focusing on 'surgical integration' into the existing `voicelog-scaffold` codebase.

### 1. Database Schema (Supabase/PostgreSQL)
Create a migration `supabase/migrations/20260310000001_sop_wo_bridge.sql` with the following key tables:
- **sop_sections**: Unified structure for SOP markdown sections (purpose, safety, procedure, etc.).
- **sop_steps**: Individual trackable units of work parsed from SOP procedure phases. Includes `est_duration_minutes` and `sort_order`.
- **work_order_step_progress**: The bridge table for unit work orders. Tracks `status` (pending, in_progress, completed), `duration_minutes` (for timesheets), and `sop_step_id`.
- **sop_ref_docs**: Trade-level reference documents linked by SOPs to avoid redundancy.
- **Trigger Update**: Modify `freeze_project_templates()` to ensure `sop_id` is preserved when a project template is 'frozen' (activated).

### 2. API Layer (Hono/Node.js)
Update `apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts` to include:
- **GET /templates/:id/sop**: Resolves the linked SOP by checking the template or its parent (inheritance resolution).
- **POST /work-orders/:id/initialize-steps**: Creates `work_order_step_progress` rows for every step in the linked SOP when a WO starts.
- **PATCH /work-orders/step-progress/:id**: Allows field workers to update status and duration.
- **GET /sops**: Lists active SOPs with optional trade filtering.

### 3. Frontend Layer (SolidJS)
- **API Client**: Update `apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts` with interfaces for `SopStep` and `StepProgress`, and methods for the new endpoints.
- **TemplateDetail.tsx**: Add a `SopLink` component to display the linked SOP and its revision.
- **WorkOrderDetail.tsx**: Implement a `StepProgressPanel` component. This UI allows workers to initialize steps and toggle statuses (e.g., 'Completed', 'In Progress') which updates the backend progress records.

### 4. SOP Manager Skill (Python)
Update the `sop_library_manager` skill to parse markdown files into the new hierarchical schema:
- Implement `parse_to_sections_and_steps()` to extract Executive Checklist tables and detailed step headings.
- Implement `sync_sop_to_new_schema()` to upsert parsed data into `sop_sections` and `sop_steps` using a delete-then-insert pattern for idempotency."
  }
]
```

# SOP ↔ Work Order Integration — Workflow & Logic

> **Last Updated**: 2026-03-09
> **Status**: Post-implementation reference document
> **Audience**: Developers, system administrators, and the timesheet application team

---

## Table of Contents

1. [System Overview](#1-system-overview)
2. [Data Model](#2-data-model)
3. [SOP Authoring & Ingestion](#3-sop-authoring--ingestion)
4. [WO Template Inheritance Chain](#4-wo-template-inheritance-chain)
5. [SOP ↔ WO Template Linkage](#5-sop--wo-template-linkage)
6. [Unit Work Order Lifecycle](#6-unit-work-order-lifecycle)
7. [Step Progress Tracking](#7-step-progress-tracking)
8. [Timesheet App Integration](#8-timesheet-app-integration)
9. [API Reference](#9-api-reference)
10. [Frontend Surfaces](#10-frontend-surfaces)
11. [Edge Cases & Business Rules](#11-edge-cases--business-rules)

---

## 1. System Overview

The system connects three domains:

```
┌─────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐
│  SOP Library    │────▶│  Work Order System   │────▶│  Timesheet App  │
│  (Knowledge)    │     │  (Execution)         │     │  (Reporting)    │
└─────────────────┘     └──────────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘
     Markdown               Templates + WOs            Duration data
     docs-as-code           3-tier inheritance          per employee
     Git-managed            Step-level tracking         per step
```

**Core principle**: SOPs are the single source of truth for *how* work is done. Work orders are the execution layer that tracks *who* did *what* and *how long* it took. The timesheet app consumes duration data to produce payroll-ready reports.

---

## 2. Data Model

### Entity Relationship Overview

```
sop_ref_docs (1 per trade)
    │
    ▼
sops (master index)
    │
    ├──▶ sop_sections (structured content)
    │       │
    │       └──▶ sop_steps (trackable units of work)
    │               │
    │               └──▶ work_order_step_progress (completion tracking)
    │                       │
    │                       └──▶ [Timesheet App reads this]
    │
    ├──▶ sop_dependencies (prerequisite graph)
    │
    └──▶ sop_recommended_videos (training links)

work_order_templates (3-tier hierarchy)
    │
    ├── scope='system'  ──(sop_id)──▶ sops
    │       │
    │       └── copied to ──▶ scope='company'  ──(sop_id inherited)──▶ sops
    │                               │
    │                               └── lazy-ref ──▶ scope='project'  ──(sop_id inherited)──▶ sops
    │
    └──▶ work_orders (unit instances)
            │
            └──▶ work_order_step_progress
```

### Key Tables

| Table | Purpose | Row Count Pattern |
|---|---|---|
| `sops` | Master SOP index | ~50-200 (one per trade task) |
| `sop_sections` | Structured content blocks | ~10-15 per SOP |
| `sop_steps` | Trackable work units | ~5-30 per SOP (max 7 per phase) |
| `sop_ref_docs` | Shared trade reference docs | 1 per trade |
| `sop_dependencies` | SOP prerequisite graph | Sparse |
| `work_order_templates` | 3-tier template hierarchy | ~8 per scope tier |
| `work_orders` | Unit work instances | Grows with projects |
| `work_order_step_progress` | Step c

<< 11379 Characters hidden >>

n      step_name: "Clean Surfaces",
      tasks_summary: "Remove dust, grease, mildew per SSPC-SP1",
      est_duration_minutes: 30
    }
  },
  ...
]
```

The joined `sop_step` data provides context without requiring a separate SOP lookup.

---

## 8. Timesheet App Integration

### 8.1 Data Contract

The timesheet app (separate application) reads `work_order_step_progress` to build employee timesheets. The contract is:

```sql
SELECT
    wosp.completed_by,
    wosp.duration_minutes,
    wosp.started_at,
    wosp.completed_at,
    wosp.status,
    wo.project_id,
    wo.template_id,
    ss.phase_name,
    ss.step_name,
    ss.est_duration_minutes,
    s.trade_code,
    s.trade_name,
    s.task_name
FROM work_order_step_progress wosp
JOIN work_orders wo ON wo.id = wosp.work_order_id
JOIN sop_steps ss ON ss.id = wosp.sop_step_id
JOIN sops s ON s.id = ss.sop_id
WHERE wosp.status = 'completed'
  AND wosp.completed_by = :user_id
  AND wosp.completed_at BETWEEN :period_start AND :period_end
ORDER BY wosp.completed_at;
```

### 8.2 Timesheet Generation Flow

```
work_order_step_progress (this system)
    │
    │  completed steps with duration_minutes
    │
    ▼
Timesheet App reads via Supabase client
    │
    ├── Groups by: employee + date + project
    ├── Sums: duration_minutes per grouping
    ├── Compares: actual vs estimated (from sop_steps.est_duration_minutes)
    ├── Flags: overrides (is_override=true) for review
    │
    ▼
Timesheet records (in timesheet app's own tables)
    │
    ▼
Payroll export
```

### 8.3 Estimated vs Actual Duration

Each `sop_steps` row has `est_duration_minutes` (from the SOP). Each `work_order_step_progress` row has `duration_minutes` (actual). The timesheet app can:

- Flag steps where actual >> estimated (potential inefficiency or SOP underestimate)
- Flag steps where actual << estimated (potential quality concern or SOP overestimate)
- Aggregate variance data to improve SOP estimates over time

---

## 9. API Reference

All endpoints are in `apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts`.

### SOP Endpoints

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/sops` | List active SOPs. Optional filters: `?trade_id=`, `?trade_code=` |
| `GET` | `/sops/:id` | SOP detail with sections, steps, and dependencies |

### Template-SOP Endpoints

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/templates/:id/sop` | Get SOP linked to template (with inheritance resolution) |
| `PATCH` | `/templates/:id` | Update template fields including `sop_id` |

### Step Progress Endpoints

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/work-orders/:id/step-progress` | Get all step progress for a WO (with joined SOP step data) |
| `POST` | `/work-orders/:id/initialize-steps` | Create pending progress records from SOP steps (one-time) |
| `PATCH` | `/work-orders/step-progress/:id` | Update individual step (status, duration, notes, photos) |

### Existing Endpoints (unchanged)

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `POST` | `/templates/assign-to-company` | Copy system → company template (preserves sop_id) |
| `POST` | `/templates/assign-to-project` | Create project template from company (lazy sop_id) |
| `GET` | `/templates/project/:projectId` | List project templates (resolves from parents) |

---

## 10. Frontend Surfaces

### 10.1 Template Detail Page (`TemplateDetail.tsx`)

Displays a blue info box when the template has a linked SOP:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  LINKED SOP                         │
│  PNT-SOP-001  Interior Painting     │
│  Rev 1.2                            │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

For project-scope templates, shows whether the SOP was inherited from the parent template.

### 10.2 Work Order Detail Page (`WorkOrderDetail.tsx`)

Displays a "Procedure Steps" panel with:

- **Initialize button**: Appears when no step progress exists. Creates pending records.
- **Step list**: Each step shows phase name, step number, step name, and status dropdown.
- **Status colors**: Green (completed), yellow (in progress), white (pending).
- **Duration display**: Shows recorded minutes when available.
- **Task summary**: Brief description from the Executive Checklist.

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Procedure Steps                                │
│                                                 │
│  [Completed ▼] Surface Prep - Step 1:           │
│                Inspect Surfaces          45m    │
│                Check for damage, moisture...     │
│                                                 │
│  [In Progress ▼] Surface Prep - Step 2:         │
│                   Clean Surfaces                │
│                   Remove dust, grease...         │
│                                                 │
│  [Pending ▼] Surface Prep - Step 3:             │
│              Repair Defects                     │
│              Fill holes, caulk gaps...           │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 11. Edge Cases & Business Rules

### 11.1 SOP Updates After Template Freeze

If an SOP is updated after a project template is frozen:
- The frozen template's `sop_id` still points to the same SOP
- `sop_steps` content may have changed
- Existing `work_order_step_progress` records still reference the old step IDs
- **Rule**: SOP updates should create new step records (new UUIDs), not modify existing ones. This preserves the integrity of in-progress work orders.

### 11.2 Template Without SOP

Not all templates require an SOP link. Templates without `sop_id`:
- Display no SOP section on the detail page
- Cannot initialize step progress on work orders
- Work orders function normally but without step-level tracking
- The timesheet app falls back to WO-level duration tracking

### 11.3 Multiple Phases

An SOP can have multiple procedure phases (e.g., Removal + Installation). Each phase has its own Executive Checklist with up to 7 steps. Steps are ordered by `phase_number` then `step_number`:

```
Phase 1: Removal
  Step 1: Disconnect fixtures
  Step 2: Remove old piping
  Step 3: Cap and test

Phase 2: Installation
  Step 1: Layout and measure
  Step 2: Install supply lines
  Step 3: Install drain lines
  Step 4: Connect fixtures
  Step 5: Pressure test
```

All steps across all phases are initialized as progress records for a single work order.

### 11.4 SOP Dependencies

The `sop_dependencies` table tracks relationships between SOPs:

| Link Type | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `prerequisite` | Must be completed before this SOP can start |
| `sub_procedure` | Called inline during a step of this SOP |
| `cross_reference` | Related but not blocking |
| `optional` | May be needed depending on conditions |

Currently informational — displayed in SOP detail views. Future enhancement: enforce prerequisite ordering at the work order level.

### 11.5 RLS (Row-Level Security)

| Table | Policy |
|---|---|
| `sop_sections` | Read: all authenticated users. Write: sysadmin only. |
| `sop_steps` | Read: all authenticated users. Write: sysadmin only. |
| `sop_dependencies` | Read: all authenticated users. Write: sysadmin only. |
| `sop_ref_docs` | Read: all authenticated users. Write: sysadmin only. |
| `work_order_step_progress` | Read/Write: tenant-isolated by `organization_id`. Sysadmin: full access. |

SOPs are global knowledge — any authenticated user can read them. Step progress is tenant-isolated — users only see their organization's data.

### 11.6 Idempotency Guards

- `POST /work-orders/:id/initialize-steps`: Returns HTTP 409 if steps already exist. Safe to retry.
- `PATCH /work-orders/step-progress/:id`: Idempotent — setting the same status twice is a no-op (timestamps only set on first transition).
- SOP Manager sync: Deletes and re-inserts sections/steps. Idempotent by design.

---

## Appendix: Complete Data Flow Diagram

```
                    ┌──────────────────────┐
                    │  SOP Markdown Files   │
                    │  (Git repository)     │
                    └──────────┬───────────┘
                               │
                    sop_manager.py parse
                               │
                    ┌──────────▼───────────┐
                    │  sop_ref_docs        │
                    │  sops                │
                    │  sop_sections        │
                    │  sop_steps           │
                    │  sop_dependencies    │
                    └──────────┬───────────┘
                               │
                    sop_id FK on templates
                               │
          ┌────────────────────▼────────────────────┐
          │         Work Order Templates             │
          │                                          │
          │  system ──copy──▶ company ──lazy──▶ project
          │  (sop_id)        (sop_id)          (sop_id)
          │                                    (frozen)
          └────────────────────┬────────────────────┘
                               │
                    create work order
                               │
                    ┌──────────▼───────────┐
                    │  work_orders          │
                    │  (unit instances)     │
                    └──────────┬───────────┘
                               │
                    initialize-steps
                               │
                    ┌──────────▼───────────┐
                    │  work_order_step_    │
                    │  progress             │
                    │                       │
                    │  status: pending      │
                    │  → in_progress        │
                    │  → completed          │
                    │  duration_minutes: N  │
                    └──────────┬───────────┘
                               │
                    Timesheet App reads
                               │
                    ┌──────────▼───────────┐
                    │  Employee Timesheets  │
                    │  (separate app)       │
                    └──────────────────────┘
```
ENDOFFILE

    }
}
